name: Home
title: Home
description: Homepage that aggregates current tennis programs and bookings (group classes, courts, camps, memberships, articles, promotions) and provides shortcuts into detailed booking and information flows.

core_features: Load aggregated homepage data using getHomepageOverview on page initialization to display this week’s group classes, next available court slots, featured membership plans, featured camp sessions, featured articles, and current promotions.
Load the current cart snapshot using getCartSummary to show a mini booking summary (number of items and total amount) and link to the full cart.

Provide quick navigation pathways into detailed booking flows for group classes (programs.html and class_detail.html), court reservations (court_booking.html), camps (camps.html), memberships (memberships.html and membership_detail.html), tournaments (tournaments.html), and training content (articles.html and article_detail.html).
Allow users to filter and focus the ‘This Week’s Classes’ list (e.g., by audience and level) client-side, and pass selected filters to programs.html via URL parameters.
Allow users to select a court slot from the ‘Next Available Courts’ list and deep-link into court_booking.html with pre-filled date, time, surface, and location type via URL parameters.
Highlight featured membership plans, camp sessions, and articles, with direct navigation to their respective list and detail pages using the appropriate URL parameters.
Display current promotions from getHomepageOverview and allow navigation to the most relevant section (e.g., memberships, camps, or programs) based on user action.
Keep homepage view state (selected focus section and any class filters) in URL query parameters so the state can be shared/bookmarked without using localStorage.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: Browse and book a group class from the homepage
1. User opens index.html.
2. The homepage-data-loader component calls getHomepageOverview and populates this_week_group_classes.
3. The class-filters component reads optional URL parameters audience and level; if present, it initializes filter controls accordingly.
4. The this-week-classes-list component applies the current filters to this_week_group_classes and renders the filtered sessions.
5. User optionally refines filters (e.g., audience, level). class-filters updates the URL query string (audience, level) via history.replaceState and notifies this-week-classes-list to re-render with the new filters (no new API call).
6. User clicks a specific class card. this-week-classes-list navigates to class_detail.html?id={class_type_id}&sessionId={session_id} using the values from the clicked item.
7. Alternatively, user clicks a CTA such as 'View Adult Classes' or 'View Junior Classes' in booking-shortcuts-panel. The page navigates to programs.html?audience={selectedAudience}&level={selectedLevel or 'all_levels'} based on the current filter or the CTA the user selected.

Workflow 2: Find and book a court from the homepage
1. User opens index.html.
2. homepage-data-loader calls getHomepageOverview and populates next_available_court_slots.
3. next-available-courts-list renders the list of upcoming available slots with court name, surface, location_type, and start/end datetime.
4. User clicks on a specific slot. next-available-courts-list computes:
   - date from slot_start_datetime (in the site’s expected date format),
   - timeFrom from slot_start_datetime,
   - timeTo from slot_end_datetime,
   - surface from surface,
   - locationType from location_type.
5. The browser navigates to court_booking.html?date={date}&timeFrom={timeFrom}&timeTo={timeTo}&surface={surface}&locationType={locationType}.
6. User completes the booking on court_booking.html; that page will handle cart updates. When the user returns to index.html, cart-summary-loader refreshes getCartSummary to show updated cart contents.

Workflow 3: Explore memberships and proceed to a membership detail
1. User opens index.html.
2. homepage-data-loader calls getHomepageOverview and populates featured_membership_plans.
3. featured-memberships-grid lists all featured_membership_plans with key fields (name, monthly_price, included_group_sessions_per_month, description, benefits as appropriate).
4. User clicks the overall 'View Memberships' CTA within featured-memberships-grid or booking-shortcuts-panel. The browser navigates to memberships.html (no additional parameters).
5. Alternatively, user clicks a specific membership plan card. The browser navigates to membership_detail.html?id={membershipPlan.id}, passing the clicked plan’s id.

Workflow 4: Explore camps and enroll from camp listings
1. User opens index.html.
2. homepage-data-loader calls getHomepageOverview and populates featured_camp_sessions.
3. featured-camps-list renders each featured camp session using camp_session_id, camp_name, age_group_label, start_date, end_date, session_type, and price.
4. User clicks on a specific featured camp session.
5. featured-camps-list derives month and year from start_date (month = month(start_date), year = year(start_date)). If the application has a mapping from age_group_label to an AgeGroup.id, it also resolves ageGroupId; otherwise it omits or leaves ageGroupId empty according to implementation rules.
6. The browser navigates to camps.html?ageGroupId={resolvedAgeGroupId}&month={month}&year={year}.
7. User reviews camp options and enrolls in a specific session on camps.html; that page will manage CampEnrollment and cart updates.
8. When user later returns to index.html, cart-summary-loader refreshes getCartSummary to reflect the new enrollment in the mini-cart.

Workflow 5: Consume training content and manage cart from homepage
1. User opens index.html.
2. homepage-data-loader calls getHomepageOverview and populates featured_articles and promotions.
3. featured-articles-list displays the featured_articles with title, summary, and any other available metadata.
4. User clicks the 'Training Articles & Resources' header or CTA in featured-articles-list. The browser navigates to articles.html.
5. Alternatively, user clicks an individual article card. The browser navigates to article_detail.html?id={article.id}.
6. In parallel, cart-summary-loader calls getCartSummary and mini-cart-summary renders the current cart total and item count.
7. At any time, user clicks 'View Booking Summary' or the mini-cart element. The browser navigates to cart.html.
8. After modifying the cart on cart.html and returning to index.html, getCartSummary is called again on load to show updated cart state.
interactions: On page load of index.html, homepage-data-loader automatically calls getHomepageOverview (no parameters) and distributes the returned data to dependent components: this-week-classes-list, next-available-courts-list, featured-memberships-grid, featured-camps-list, featured-articles-list, and promotions-strip.
On page load of index.html, cart-summary-loader automatically calls getCartSummary (no parameters) and passes the cart and items to mini-cart-summary.

When a user changes audience or level filters in class-filters, the component updates the audience and level query parameters in the URL (e.g., index.html?section=classes&audience=adult&level=intermediate) via history.replaceState, and triggers this-week-classes-list to re-filter this_week_group_classes client-side.

When a user chooses a different primary focus section in section-switcher (e.g., classes, courts, camps, memberships, articles, promotions), section-switcher updates the section query parameter in the URL (e.g., ?section=courts) and controls which main content groups are visible. No new API calls are made; data from the initial getHomepageOverview response is reused.

When a user clicks 'View all classes' or a filter-specific CTA in booking-shortcuts-panel, the browser navigates to programs.html with audience and level taken from the current filter state (if set) or with defaults (e.g., level=all_levels).

When a user clicks on a class card in this-week-classes-list, the browser navigates to class_detail.html?id={class_type_id}&sessionId={session_id} based on the clicked item’s class_type_id and session_id.

When a user clicks on a court slot row in next-available-courts-list, the component reads slot_start_datetime, slot_end_datetime, surface, and location_type from that item, computes date, timeFrom, and timeTo from the datetimes, and navigates to court_booking.html?date={date}&timeFrom={timeFrom}&timeTo={timeTo}&surface={surface}&locationType={locationType}.
When a user clicks the 'View Memberships' CTA in featured-memberships-grid, the browser navigates to memberships.html (no parameters).
When a user clicks an individual membership plan card in featured-memberships-grid, the browser navigates to membership_detail.html?id={membershipPlan.id}.

When a user clicks a camp session card in featured-camps-list, the component parses start_date to obtain month and year and (optionally) resolves ageGroupId from age_group_label. It then navigates to camps.html?ageGroupId={ageGroupId}&month={month}&year={year}.
When a user clicks the 'Upcoming Tournaments & Events' CTA in booking-shortcuts-panel, the browser navigates directly to tournaments.html.
When a user clicks the 'Training Articles & Resources' section header in featured-articles-list, the browser navigates to articles.html.
When a user clicks an individual article card in featured-articles-list, the browser navigates to article_detail.html?id={article.id}.
When a user clicks the mini-cart summary or 'View Booking Summary' CTA in mini-cart-summary, the browser navigates to cart.html.

When the page detects navigation back to index.html from another page (via standard page load), both getHomepageOverview and getCartSummary may be called again to refresh displayed data, ensuring the homepage reflects the latest sessions, availability, and cart state.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The Home page does not receive required incoming parameters from the server side, but it uses optional URL query parameters to manage client-side state and deep linking without localStorage:

- section: Indicates the primary focus section to display prominently (e.g., 'classes', 'courts', 'camps', 'memberships', 'articles', 'promotions').
  - On load, section-switcher reads window.location.search for section.
  - If absent or invalid, it defaults to 'classes'.
  - Changing the active section via UI updates the section query parameter using history.replaceState without reloading the page or calling APIs.

- audience and level: Control the initial filter state for the 'This Week’s Classes' list.
  - On load, class-filters reads audience and level from window.location.search.
  - If provided and they match allowed enum values of GroupClassType.audience and GroupClassType.level, the component sets its filter controls accordingly.
  - this-week-classes-list then filters getHomepageOverview.this_week_group_classes in memory using these values.
  - When the user changes filters, class-filters updates audience and level in the URL (adding, replacing, or removing them) via history.replaceState and triggers a re-filter of the existing data.

- Additional view parameters: The page may define optional parameters like view or sort for client-side behavior only (for example, view=compact or sort=start_datetime_asc). These parameters:
  - Are read on load by the relevant component (e.g., this-week-classes-list).
  - Affect only the presentation (e.g., ordering or card density) and do not cause new API calls.

API calls and data loading behavior:
- getHomepageOverview and getCartSummary are always called without URL parameters, regardless of the query string, since their interface definitions specify no parameters.
- All filtering, sorting, and section-visibility logic is applied client-side against the already-fetched data.

Navigation state propagation:
- When navigating to programs.html, class_detail.html, court_booking.html, memberships.html, membership_detail.html, camps.html, tournaments.html, articles.html, article_detail.html, and cart.html, the Home page constructs URLs using the required parameters defined in the navigation specification and the current in-memory state (e.g., selectedAudience, selectedLevel) where applicable.
- The target pages are responsible for interpreting their own URL parameters; the Home page does not maintain shared state beyond what it encodes into the URL.

components.functionality: Calls getHomepageOverview on page initialization, stores the returned object in a shared state (e.g., in-memory store or top-level component state), and makes slices of this data available to dependent components (classes, courts, memberships, camps, articles, promotions). Handles loading and error states for the homepage content.
Calls getCartSummary on page initialization (and optionally on demand) and exposes the cart and items data to the mini-cart-summary component. Handles loading and error states for cart information.

Determines which main content section is active (classes, courts, camps, memberships, articles, promotions) based on the section query parameter or user selection. Shows or hides related components accordingly and keeps the section parameter in sync with the URL.

Provides high-level shortcut buttons or links for the main booking types (group classes, courts, camps, memberships, tournaments, and optionally private lessons via programs.html). It reads current filter state (audience, level) where relevant and constructs navigation URLs with appropriate query parameters.

Manages filter controls for the 'This Week’s Classes' list, including audience and level. Initializes its state from URL query parameters, updates the URL when filters change, and notifies this-week-classes-list of the active filters.

Displays a filtered list of group class sessions occurring this week, using the this_week_group_classes array from getHomepageOverview and the current filters from class-filters. Each list item shows class_name, audience, level, focus, start_datetime, end_datetime, price, and remaining_spots as available, and provides navigation into class_detail.html or programs.html.

Shows upcoming available court slots based on next_available_court_slots from getHomepageOverview. Each row or card displays court_name, surface, location_type, slot_start_datetime, and slot_end_datetime. Clicking a slot constructs the court_booking.html URL with date, timeFrom, timeTo, surface, and locationType query parameters derived from the slot.

Displays featured membership plans using featured_membership_plans from getHomepageOverview. For each MembershipPlan, it shows key fields (name, monthly_price, included_group_sessions_per_month, description, benefits, min_commitment_months as needed). Provides a global 'View Memberships' CTA (to memberships.html) and per-card navigation to membership_detail.html?id={id}.

Lists featured junior camp options based on featured_camp_sessions from getHomepageOverview. Each item uses camp_session_id, camp_name, age_group_label, start_date, end_date, session_type, and price. On click, it derives month and year from start_date and, if available, resolves an ageGroupId from age_group_label, then navigates to camps.html with the corresponding query parameters.

Renders a list of featured training and resource articles using featured_articles from getHomepageOverview. Each Article shows title, summary (if available), category/tags or published_at as needed. The component exposes a 'Training Articles & Resources' CTA that navigates to articles.html and per-article navigation to article_detail.html?id={id}.

Displays current promotions from getHomepageOverview.promotions. Shows each promotion’s title, summary, and valid_until where provided. Optionally exposes links or buttons that trigger navigation to relevant sections (e.g., memberships, camps, or programs) based on the promotion context, but without hardcoding any specific promotion content.

Uses cart and items from getCartSummary to calculate and display a concise booking summary (e.g., number of active items, total_amount if present). Provides a click target that navigates the user to cart.html for full review and checkout.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content column for the hero text, over a full-width background image. Navigation items appear distributed horizontally across the top within the same grid.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a strictly top-to-bottom, stacked layout echoing the mockup: global nav at the very top, then a full-viewport hero with central text over a full-bleed sports image, followed by a vertically stacked sequence of full-width sections. Within the main content below the hero, components are organized into a single central content column aligned to an implied 12-column grid. Key interactive controls (section switcher, filters, booking shortcuts) are placed along the central vertical axis to maintain the strong centered flow from the hero.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a strictly top-to-bottom, stacked layout echoing the mockup: global nav at the very top, then a full-viewport hero with central text over a full-bleed sports image, followed by a vertically stacked sequence of full-width sections. Within the main content below the hero, components are organized into a single central content column aligned to an implied 12-column grid. Key interactive controls (section switcher, filters, booking shortcuts) are placed along the central vertical axis to maintain the strong centered flow from the hero.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero occupies the full viewport height. Below it, the main content area sits within a max-width (roughly 1140–1200px) central column with generous vertical spacing (64–96px) between major sections and 24–32px between related elements. Full-width elements like the promotions strip and booking shortcuts banner respect edge padding but visually stretch across the screen. Lists and grids rarely exceed 70–80% of the viewport width to keep a focused, editorial feel. The navigation bar remains a slim strip at the top, visually light and not competing with the hero.
layout_strategies.content_density: Overall density stays low, in line with the hero: big headings, short descriptive text, and compact controls with ample whitespace. Each section focuses on a single core task (view this week’s classes, see next available courts, browse memberships, etc.), avoiding multi-column clutter. Lists show a limited number of highlighted items by default (with optional 'View all' actions, outside the scope here), keeping the above-the-fold experience clean.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow starts at the centered hero headline, then moves down into a promotions strip and a booking shortcuts panel that act as a transitional bridge into the functional content. The eye then lands on the section switcher, which acts as a secondary navigation hub, followed by filters where relevant, and then into the primary lists (classes and courts). From there, attention moves down the page through featured memberships, camps, and articles. The mini-cart icon in the top-right navigation provides a subtle, persistent purchase context without breaking the vertical reading rhythm.

layout.overall_description: At the very top, a slim, full-width navigation bar runs across the page, with evenly spaced uppercase links aligned within a centered 12-column grid and a mini-cart icon anchored to the top-right. Directly beneath it, a full-bleed hero image of a tennis court fills the entire viewport height, darkened by an overlay. In the vertical center of this hero, a narrow central column contains a small purple label ('WELCOME TO'), a large white headline about the tennis academy, and a single line of supporting text, all stacked with generous vertical spacing. At the bottom center of the hero, small slider dots hint at multiple hero variations. Scrolling down, the hero gives way to a full-width, low-height promotions strip, followed by a wide but still centered booking shortcuts panel with quick links to core booking flows. Below that, the main program content sits in a max-width central column: first a horizontal section switcher (tabs), then context-specific filters (for classes), and beneath them clean, card-based lists of this week’s classes and next available courts. Further down, featured memberships, camps, and articles are presented in their own clearly separated sections, each with its heading and consistent spacing. Throughout, whitespace, a restrained color palette, and a strict central alignment preserve the modern, semi-minimal corporate sports aesthetic.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is a non-visual, background component. It is conceptually associated with the entire page and loads before or alongside the hero and main content. It does not occupy any visible space in the layout and does not shift or influence the positioning of other UI elements.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered, operates off-canvas with no spatial footprint.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Also a non-visual, background component, this loader is logically attached to the mini-cart summary and navigation bar. It runs behind the scenes and does not affect the spatial arrangement or visible dimensions of the nav or mini-cart icon.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered, operates off-canvas with no spatial footprint.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The section switcher appears in the main content area directly below the booking shortcuts panel, centered on the page within the max-width content column. It takes the form of a horizontal row of 3–4 uppercase tab-like buttons (e.g., 'CLASSES', 'COURTS', 'CAMPS', 'MEMBERSHIPS'), stretching across roughly 60–70% of the central column width. Each tab has equal width, with generous horizontal padding (~16–24px) and a subtle underlined active state that echoes the navigation bar’s underline style. Vertically, there is approximately 48–64px of whitespace above it (from the booking shortcuts panel) and 24–32px below it before any filters or lists, making it feel like a clear section header control rather than cramped within content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the document flow; optionally can become sticky within the main content on scroll for easier context switching, but remains constrained to the central content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Positioned immediately below the hero section and above the section switcher, the booking shortcuts panel spans nearly the full width of the main content but is visually contained within the central max-width grid. It appears as a shallow, card-like horizontal band centered on the page, occupying roughly 70–80% of the viewport width on desktop. Inside, 3–4 shortcut tiles (e.g., 'Book a Class', 'Reserve a Court', 'Join a Camp', 'Get Membership') are laid out in a single row on large screens, each tile taking an equal fraction of the width with 16–24px gaps between them. The panel has generous padding on all sides (24–32px), with its label or heading aligned left within the central column and the tiles aligned directly beneath. There is a 64–80px gap between the bottom of the hero and the top of this panel, so it reads as the first functional element after the storytelling hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width section (within the site content width), expanding vertically as needed on smaller screens to stack tiles while maintaining centered alignment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The class filters bar sits directly below the section switcher but only appears when a 'Classes' context is active. It is centered within the same content column, spanning roughly 70–80% of that column’s width to maintain focus. Visually, it is a low-profile horizontal strip of pill-shaped filter chips and possibly dropdowns aligned from left to right, with 8–16px horizontal spacing between controls. There is minimal vertical height (about 48–56px including padding), with 24px of whitespace above it (separating it from the section switcher) and 24–32px below before the classes list. The overall feel is light and unobtrusive, matching the semi-minimal aesthetic while still being clearly interactive.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the flow; optionally becomes sticky within the main content area so that as the user scrolls through the class list, the filters remain pinned just beneath any fixed global nav.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The 'this-week-classes-list' occupies the primary content zone below the class filters when the Classes section is active. It is placed in the center of the page within the max-width column, with its section label ('This Week’s Classes') aligned left and sitting approximately 32px above the first card. The list itself is a vertical stack of card rows, each card spanning roughly 80–100% of the central column width on desktop with 16–24px vertical spacing between cards. The left edge of the cards aligns with the heading, reinforcing the linear flow. There is generous padding inside each card (16–24px) and a clean, monochrome treatment with subtle use of the purple accent for key data (time, level) or action buttons. A 64–80px gap below the last class card separates this section from whatever list or grid follows, preserving the low-density, editorial feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrollable; the list extends vertically as needed, contained within the central column and adjusting to a single-column layout across breakpoints.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The 'next-available-courts-list' appears after the classes section in the main vertical flow when either the global 'Courts' context is active or as a secondary block within the Classes context (depending on product logic). It is positioned directly below the last class card section, with 64–80px of vertical separation. The section heading ('Next Available Courts') is aligned left within the same central column, followed by a compact list of 3–5 items. Each list item appears as a narrow, streamlined card row—slightly shorter in height than class cards—spanning about 70–80% of the content width to feel a bit lighter and more utilitarian. Items are stacked vertically with 12–16px spacing between them, and key information (time, surface, location) is set in clear, high-contrast typography. The overall block occupies roughly 25–35% of the page height on initial load, depending on item count.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, vertically stacked within the main content; expands downward as more courts are shown but always constrained to the central content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured memberships grid sits below the classes and courts sections as the next major block. It starts after a generous vertical gap (64–96px), maintaining the feeling of distinct editorial sections. The section heading ('Featured Memberships') is left-aligned within the central column, with a brief one-line description optionally centered or left-aligned beneath it. The memberships themselves are displayed as 2–3 cards per row on desktop within the central width, each card occupying an equal fraction of the row (roughly 30–32% of the content width for three columns) with 24px horizontal and vertical gaps. The grid’s overall width does not exceed 80–90% of the content column, allowing comfortable side padding. Cards are taller than court list items, similar to class cards but more focused on benefits and pricing, with purple accents highlighting call-to-action buttons or key membership labels.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static grid; it reflows responsively to fewer columns on smaller screens while remaining centered within the main content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured camps list follows the memberships grid as another clearly separated vertical section, introduced after 64–80px of whitespace. Within the central column, a left-aligned heading ('Featured Camps') sits above a vertically stacked set of camp cards. On desktop, this list can optionally adopt a mixed layout: one larger, hero-like primary camp card spanning 100% of the content width at the top, followed by 2-column rows of smaller camp cards beneath (each taking about 48% of the content width with a 4% gap). Spacing between rows is 24px, and internal card padding is 16–24px. The imagery within these cards echoes the hero’s photography style with dark overlays and high-contrast text where applicable, keeping visual cohesion with the top of the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, wrapping list that grows vertically; cards reflow to a single column on narrower viewports while maintaining consistent side padding.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured articles list appears near the bottom of the main content stack, after the camps section, with 64–80px of vertical breathing room. It is presented more like an editorial block: a left-aligned section title ('Latest Articles') and optionally a brief subtitle, followed by article teasers in a simple vertical list or a 2-column grid depending on screen width. On large screens, expect two columns of article cards (each ~48% of the central content width with a small gap), stacked over 2–3 rows. Each article card is relatively shallow, showing a small image thumbnail (or monochrome block), title, and 1–2 lines of excerpt text, with 16px internal padding and 16–24px vertical spacing between rows. The treatment here is slightly lighter-weight than program cards, reflecting a tertiary hierarchy but still integrated into the same grid rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static list/grid at the bottom of the primary content area, expanding vertically as article count increases.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The promotions strip sits immediately below the hero and above the booking shortcuts panel, acting as a slender transition band between storytelling and functional content. It spans the full width of the viewport but keeps its text and any small icons constrained within the central max-width grid. Vertically, it occupies a relatively shallow height—roughly 56–72px including padding—with a subtle background tint that differentiates it from both the dark hero and the main content’s white background. The promotional message is centered horizontally within the content column or aligned left, with a concise, single-line message and possibly a compact link or button using the purple accent color. There is a 24–32px gap between the bottom edge of the hero and the top of this strip, and another 32–48px gap between the strip and the booking shortcuts panel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width bar that scrolls with the page; does not stick to the top to avoid competing with the main navigation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The mini-cart summary presents as a small cart icon (optionally with an item count badge) located in the navigation bar at the very top of the page, anchored to the top-right corner within the same 12-column grid as the nav links. It occupies a compact clickable area—approximately 32–40px wide—with 16–24px of horizontal padding from the right viewport edge and vertically centered inside the nav bar height (roughly 64–72px tall). When opened, the mini-cart’s dropdown panel appears aligned to the right edge of the content area or viewport, extending downward over the hero or page content as a floating card about 320–360px wide. This panel respects the visual language of the site: light background, dark text, and purple accents for actions. It does not displace other content on the page; instead it overlays it, with an appropriate shadow to separate it from the hero image beneath.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Anchored within a (likely) sticky global navigation bar at the top; its expanded state is a floating, right-aligned overlay panel that scrolls independently if its content exceeds viewport height.

name: Programs & Group Classes
title: Programs & Group Classes
description: Page for searching, filtering, and reviewing scheduled group tennis classes (adults and juniors), with navigation to detailed class pages for booking.

core_features: Load available filter options for audiences, levels, focuses, price ranges, and time-of-day buckets using getGroupClassFilterOptions.
Initialize and maintain filter state (audience, level, focus, date range, time window, price range, days of week, result limit, sorting) from URL parameters.
Search scheduled group class sessions based on the current filter and sort state using searchGroupClassSessions.
Display a list of class session cards showing key details including class name, audience, level, focus, schedule, price, and remaining spots, derived from searchGroupClassSessions results.
Allow sorting of results by price or start date/time, in ascending or descending order, using searchGroupClassSessions sortBy and sortDirection parameters.
Allow users to refine results by audience (adult/junior), level, focus, date range, time of day, price range, and days of week, and immediately see updated results.
Expose applied filters in an active-filters bar, with the ability to clear individual filters or reset all, updating URL parameters and re-searching.
Provide navigation from a class or session card to the class detail page (class_detail.html) with appropriate id and sessionId URL parameters.
Display a mini cart summary (total items and amount) using getCartSummary, and provide navigation to the cart page (cart.html).
user_workflows:
Workflow: Discover suitable classes from navigation
1. User navigates to programs.html via the main navigation or footer link.
2. Page controller reads URL parameters (if any) such as audience, level, focus, startDate, endDate, timeFrom, timeTo, minPrice, maxPrice, sortBy, sortDirection, plus any page-specific parameters like daysOfWeek and limit.
3. Page controller calls getGroupClassFilterOptions to populate filter controls (audience, level, focus options, price range, time-of-day buckets).
4. Page controller derives an initial filter state by combining URL parameters with defaults inferred from getGroupClassFilterOptions (e.g., full price range when no minPrice/maxPrice are provided) without hardcoded values.
5. Page controller calls searchGroupClassSessions with the current filter and sort state.
6. The session results list component renders class session cards from the search results.
7. User scrolls through results and, if needed, uses the filter form to narrow down by audience, level, focus, date range, time window, price range, or days of week.
8. On each filter change, the filter state is updated, the URL is updated to reflect the new state, and searchGroupClassSessions is called again to refresh the results.
9. User may adjust sorting (e.g., sort by price or start date/time, ascending/descending); sorting controls update URL state and trigger a new search.
10. When the user finds a suitable session, they click the class name or specific session; the page navigates to class_detail.html with id set to the selected class_type_id and sessionId set to the selected session_id.

Workflow: Arrive with pre-applied filters from another page
1. User clicks a call-to-action on index.html, class_detail.html, or cart.html that links to programs.html with specific URL parameters (e.g., audience, level, focus, startDate, endDate, maxPrice, and optionally timeFrom/timeTo).
2. On load, the page controller reads these incoming URL parameters and sets the initial filter state accordingly, without overriding them with defaults.
3. Page controller calls getGroupClassFilterOptions to populate possible filter options.
4. Page controller calls searchGroupClassSessions using the filter state derived entirely from URL parameters (plus any derived defaults for missing values).
5. Filter form and active-filters bar display the pre-applied values so the user understands the context (e.g., audience and level already selected).
6. User can further refine filters (for example, narrowing the date range or changing focus) or clear specific pre-applied filters using the active-filters bar.
7. Updated filter actions modify URL parameters and re-trigger searchGroupClassSessions.
8. When the user selects a session, they navigate to class_detail.html with id and sessionId for detailed information and booking.

Workflow: Review cart while browsing programs
1. When programs.html loads, the page controller calls getCartSummary to retrieve current cart and cart items.
2. The mini-cart summary component displays an aggregated view (such as total quantity and total amount) using the Cart and CartItem data, without hardcoded calculations.
3. As the user browses classes and navigates back from class_detail.html, programs.html is reloaded or reactivated and getCartSummary is called again to reflect any changes.
4. User clicks the 'View Cart' action in the mini-cart summary.
5. The page navigates to cart.html without modifying URL parameters for programs.html; the browser history retains the current filter state so the user can return to the same search later via back navigation.

Workflow: Load more sessions using result limit
1. Page initializes with a default or URL-specified limit parameter passed to searchGroupClassSessions.
2. searchGroupClassSessions returns results and total_count.
3. Pagination or 'Load more' controls compare the current limit to total_count.
4. If there are more results than currently displayed, a 'Load more' interaction is enabled.
5. When the user activates 'Load more', the component increases the limit value, updates the corresponding URL parameter, and triggers searchGroupClassSessions with the new limit.
6. The session list component re-renders with the expanded results set.
7. This process can be repeated until the limit reaches or exceeds total_count.
interactions:
Interaction: Adjust audience, level, and focus filters
- Trigger: User changes audience, level, or focus selection in the filter form.
- Behavior: Filter form updates its internal state, propagates new values to the page controller, which updates URL parameters (audience, level, focus), and calls searchGroupClassSessions with updated parameters.
- Result: Session results list rerenders based on new filters; active-filters bar shows the applied audience/level/focus filters.

Interaction: Set date range
- Trigger: User selects or changes startDate and/or endDate in the date range controls.
- Behavior: Filter form updates filter state and URL parameters (startDate, endDate). Page controller calls searchGroupClassSessions with updated date range.
- Result: Results list shows only sessions whose start_datetime is within the specified window, as enforced by searchGroupClassSessions.

Interaction: Filter by time of day
- Trigger: User selects a time-of-day bucket (e.g., morning/afternoon/evening) or adjusts explicit timeFrom/timeTo controls.
- Behavior: Filter form, using time_of_day_buckets from getGroupClassFilterOptions, maps the chosen bucket to corresponding start_time and end_time or directly uses user-provided times. It updates timeFrom and timeTo in filter state and URL parameters. Page controller calls searchGroupClassSessions with updated timeFrom/timeTo.
- Result: Results list displays only sessions within the chosen daily time window.

Interaction: Filter by price range
- Trigger: User moves price range controls (for example, a min/max range slider) in the filter form.
- Behavior: Filter form uses price_range from getGroupClassFilterOptions to constrain allowed values, updates minPrice and maxPrice in state and URL, and invokes searchGroupClassSessions via the page controller.
- Result: Results list includes only sessions whose price lies within minPrice and maxPrice as enforced by searchGroupClassSessions.

Interaction: Filter by days of week
- Trigger: User selects one or more days of the week (e.g., Monday, Wednesday) in the filter form.
- Behavior: Filter form updates a daysOfWeek collection in state and in the URL (for example, as a comma-separated string). Page controller calls searchGroupClassSessions with daysOfWeek set accordingly.
- Result: Results list is restricted to sessions occurring on the selected days, based on backend logic of searchGroupClassSessions.

Interaction: Change sorting
- Trigger: User selects a sorting option (e.g., sort by price or start date/time) and a sort direction (ascending/descending) using sorting controls.
- Behavior: Sorting controls update sortBy and sortDirection in page state and synchronize them to URL parameters. Page controller calls searchGroupClassSessions with these values.
- Result: Results are returned from the interface in the requested order and rendered accordingly.

Interaction: Clear individual filters
- Trigger: User clicks a 'remove' action on a specific filter chip in the active-filters bar.
- Behavior: Active-filters bar notifies page controller of the filter key to clear (e.g., maxPrice or focus). Page controller removes that parameter from filter state and URL, then calls searchGroupClassSessions with the remaining filters.
- Result: Results broaden to reflect removal of the filter; active-filters bar updates and no longer shows the cleared filter.

Interaction: Reset all filters
- Trigger: User clicks a 'Reset filters' action in the filter form or active-filters bar.
- Behavior: Page controller resets filter state to default values derived from getGroupClassFilterOptions (for example, no audience restriction or full price range), removes corresponding URL parameters, and calls searchGroupClassSessions.
- Result: Results list shows a broader set of sessions based on the default filter state.

Interaction: Click a class or session card
- Trigger: User clicks the class name, a session time, or a dedicated 'View details' action on a session card.
- Behavior: Session results component reads the associated class_type_id and session_id from the selected result object and navigates to class_detail.html with URL parameters id set to class_type_id and sessionId set to session_id.
- Result: class_detail.html can use these parameters to show a full description and booking options for that session.

Interaction: View cart from mini-cart summary
- Trigger: User clicks 'View Cart' in the mini-cart summary component.
- Behavior: Mini-cart component triggers a navigation to cart.html without modifying current program filter URL parameters.
- Result: cart.html shows the full cart based on current cart state; returning via browser back button restores the programs.html view with its previous filter state.

Interaction: Load more results
- Trigger: User clicks a 'Load more' or similar control at the end of the current results list.
- Behavior: Pagination component calculates a new, larger limit value, updates this limit in filter state and URL, and requests searchGroupClassSessions with the new limit while preserving other filters.
- Result: More results are returned and appended or re-rendered, up to total_count.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page state is fully represented by URL parameters and their mapping to interface calls. On initial load, the page controller reads known URL parameters (audience, level, focus, startDate, endDate, timeFrom, timeTo, minPrice, maxPrice, sortBy, sortDirection) and any additional parameters such as daysOfWeek and limit if present. These values populate a filter-and-sort state object. The controller then calls getGroupClassFilterOptions to obtain available audiences, levels, focuses, recommended price_range, and time_of_day_buckets. For each filter dimension, if a corresponding URL parameter is present, it is used directly. If it is missing, the controller applies a default derived from getGroupClassFilterOptions (for example, interpreting a missing minPrice as the minimum from the server-provided price_range) rather than any hardcoded constants. This combined state is used to call searchGroupClassSessions. The search call always uses the current filter state as arguments, including audience, level, focus, startDate, endDate, timeFrom, timeTo, minPrice, maxPrice, daysOfWeek, limit, sortBy, and sortDirection. The response's applied_filters object is treated as the source of truth for what the backend actually applied; this may be used to normalize or correct the UI state and URL if the backend adjusts or ignores some parameters. Whenever the user changes filters or sorting, the page controller updates the in-memory state and immediately synchronizes the change back into URL parameters (adding, updating, or removing parameters as needed). It then calls searchGroupClassSessions with the new state. This ensures that reloading the page or sharing the URL reproduces the current view. Navigation to other pages (class_detail.html, cart.html) preserves the current URL parameters in browser history so that the back button reliably restores the same filter and results state. The mini-cart summary is independent of the filter state; it is refreshed via getCartSummary on each page load or when a visible cart-refresh event is triggered, without writing to URL state.

components.functionality: Orchestrates initial data loading, URL parameter parsing, filter/sort state management, interface calls, and data distribution to child components.
event_handlers: onPageLoad: Read URL parameters, call getGroupClassFilterOptions, derive initial filter state, then call searchGroupClassSessions and getCartSummary.
onUrlParamChange: When URL parameters change (via user actions or browser navigation), update internal state and re-call searchGroupClassSessions.
onFilterStateChange: Receive updated filter values from the filter form or active-filters bar, write them into URL parameters, and trigger searchGroupClassSessions.
onSortChange: Receive updated sortBy and sortDirection from sorting controls, update URL and re-call searchGroupClassSessions.
onLimitChange: Receive updates to the result limit from pagination component, update URL and re-call searchGroupClassSessions.
onCartRefreshRequest: Re-call getCartSummary to refresh mini-cart data when returning from other pages or when explicitly requested.

components.functionality: Displays and manages form controls for audience, level, focus, date range, time window, price range, and days-of-week filters, using options and ranges from getGroupClassFilterOptions and initial values from URL-derived state.
event_handlers: onInit: Receive filter options from page controller (audiences, levels, focuses, price_range, time_of_day_buckets) and initial filter values derived from URL parameters.
onAudienceChange: Update audience in local state and notify page controller with new audience value.
onLevelChange: Update level in local state and notify page controller.
onFocusChange: Update focus in local state and notify page controller.
onDateRangeChange: Update startDate and endDate values and notify page controller.
onTimeWindowChange: Update timeFrom and timeTo based on either a selected time-of-day bucket or explicit time inputs and notify page controller.
onPriceRangeChange: Validate minPrice and maxPrice against price_range, update values, and notify page controller.
onDaysOfWeekChange: Update the selected daysOfWeek collection and notify page controller.
onResetFilters: Clear current form inputs to defaults derived from getGroupClassFilterOptions, notify page controller to reset filter state and URL parameters.

components.functionality: Shows a concise summary of currently applied filters (such as audience, level, focus, date range, time range, price range, daysOfWeek) and allows the user to remove individual filters or reset all.
event_handlers: onInit: Receive applied_filters from page controller after each searchGroupClassSessions call to display active filter chips.
onRemoveFilterClick: When a user clicks to remove a specific filter, emit an event to page controller identifying the filter key to clear.
onClearAllClick: Notify page controller that all filters should be reset to defaults derived from getGroupClassFilterOptions.

components.functionality: Allows the user to select the sorting field (e.g., price or start date/time) and direction (ascending or descending) for group class session results.
event_handlers: onInit: Read initial sortBy and sortDirection from page controller (derived from URL parameters) and reflect them in control state.
onSortFieldChange: When the user changes the sort field, notify page controller with the new sortBy value.
onSortDirectionToggle: When the user switches between ascending and descending, notify page controller with the new sortDirection.

components.functionality: Renders the list of group class sessions returned by searchGroupClassSessions as interactive cards showing key information and actions to view details.
event_handlers: onInit: Receive initial results from page controller after the first searchGroupClassSessions call and render them.
onResultsUpdate: When page controller provides updated results from a new searchGroupClassSessions response, refresh the list of cards.

onSessionClick: When the user clicks a session card or its class name, read the session_id and class_type_id from the corresponding result item and navigate to class_detail.html with id and sessionId URL parameters.
onScrollEnd: Optionally notify pagination component or page controller that the user reached the end of the list to enable 'Load more' behaviors if implemented.

components.functionality: Controls the number of session results displayed using the limit parameter for searchGroupClassSessions, enabling 'Load more' or similar behaviors.
event_handlers: onInit: Read current limit from page controller (derived from URL or defaults) and compare with total_count to determine if additional results are available.

onLoadMoreClick: Calculate a new limit value (for example, by increasing it relative to current results count), and notify page controller to update limit in state and URL and to re-call searchGroupClassSessions.
onResultsUpdate: After each searchGroupClassSessions response, re-evaluate whether more results exist (by comparing total_count and the count of results) and update control availability.

components.functionality: Displays a compact summary of the user's current cart, including total items and total amount, and provides navigation to the cart page.
event_handlers: onInit: Request cart summary data from page controller (which calls getCartSummary) and display an aggregated view.
onCartDataUpdate: When page controller refreshes cart data (for example, after the user returns from another page), update the displayed totals.
onViewCartClick: Navigate to cart.html when the user clicks the 'View Cart' action.

components.functionality: Handles the two-way synchronization between in-memory filter/sort/pagination state and URL parameters for the Programs & Group Classes page.
event_handlers: onInit: Parse current URL parameters into a structured state object containing audience, level, focus, startDate, endDate, timeFrom, timeTo, minPrice, maxPrice, daysOfWeek, limit, sortBy, sortDirection, and provide it to the page controller.
onStateChange: When the page controller updates the filter/sort state as a result of user actions, rewrite URL parameters to reflect these values (adding, updating, or removing parameters).

onBrowserNavigation: Listen to browser back/forward events (e.g., popstate), re-parse URL parameters, and notify the page controller so it can trigger searchGroupClassSessions with the restored state.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content column for the hero text, over a full-width background image. Navigation items appear distributed horizontally across the top within the same grid.
layout.layout_pattern: Maintain the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom structure with clearly stacked sections: global navigation at the very top (shared across the site), then a shallow, image-driven page hero controlled by the page-controller-programs component. Directly beneath the hero, place the search/filter layer (filters form, then active filters bar). Below that, position the sorting controls and the vertically stacked list of session results, followed by pagination at the very bottom of the page content. Secondary utility (mini-cart) floats near the main column but does not disrupt the primary vertical reading flow.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Maintain the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom structure with clearly stacked sections: global navigation at the very top (shared across the site), then a shallow, image-driven page hero controlled by the page-controller-programs component. Directly beneath the hero, place the search/filter layer (filters form, then active filters bar). Below that, position the sorting controls and the vertically stacked list of session results, followed by pagination at the very bottom of the page content. Secondary utility (mini-cart) floats near the main column but does not disrupt the primary vertical reading flow.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Use a full-width hero band with a tennis photo and dark overlay occupying roughly 40–50% of the initial viewport height on desktop, with a centered content column maxing out around 1040px in width. All functional components (filters, active filters bar, sorting, results, pagination, mini-cart) sit within this same central grid. Vertical spacing between major sections (hero → filters, filters → results, results → pagination) follows the 64–96px separation on desktop, with internal element spacing in the 16–32px range. The results list and its surrounding controls take up the majority of the page’s vertical real estate, reinforcing this page’s utility purpose.
layout_strategies.content_density: Keep density low-to-moderate, echoing the marketing-style hero in the mockup. The hero itself is very sparse: a small label, a large headline, and a short supporting line. The filters and sorting controls use clear grouping and generous padding so they feel breathable rather than cramped. The results list is denser by necessity, but each card has sufficient padding and vertical separation to maintain a clean, editorial feel rather than a compressed table. There are no heavy sidebars; secondary information such as the mini-cart is compact and visually light.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Preserve the strong, centered vertical flow from the mockup: the eye lands first on the white hero headline over the darkened image, then moves up slightly to the small purple label. From there, the user’s gaze naturally drops into the filters form directly beneath the hero, then to the active filters chips, then to the sorting controls aligned with the top of the results. The user then scrolls down through the stacked list of sessions to the pagination controls. The mini-cart summary is visually secondary, perched toward the top-right of the content area so it’s discoverable without competing with the central column. Purple accent is used for key interactive affordances (filter apply, active chips, selected pagination) to guide attention subtly along this flow.

layout.overall_description: Under the site-wide navigation, the page opens with a wide, half-height hero band showing a full-bleed tennis court photo under a dark overlay. In the middle of the screen, a centered text column presents a small purple eyebrow label, a large white “Programs & Group Classes” headline, and a single line of supporting copy, all vertically spaced with generous breathing room. As you scroll or look just below the hero, the background shifts to a clean, light surface while the content remains aligned to the same central grid. A broad, card-like filters block spans most of the page width, presenting search and filter controls in an organized, low-profile layout. Directly below, a slim row of active filter chips runs horizontally, before the eye is drawn slightly down and right to the sorting controls above the list of program cards. The sessions are displayed as vertically stacked cards in the main column, each with clear hierarchy and ample padding. Beneath the list, a centered pagination strip sits with comfortable white space around it. Throughout, the page feels like a continuation of the hero’s modern, image-driven aesthetic—monochrome base, sharp typography, and restrained purple accents—while shifting from a purely editorial hero mood into a practical, easy-to-scan browsing interface. A compact mini-cart summary floats near the top-right of the viewport, always nearby but never interrupting the central, top-to-bottom reading flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page controller defines the topmost hero section beneath the global navigation bar. It occupies the full browser width and roughly 40–50% of the viewport height on desktop (minimum height around 320–400px), presenting a full-bleed tennis background image with a dark, semi-transparent overlay. Within this band, a centered content column (aligned to the site’s 12-column grid and constrained to about 70–80% of the page width, max ~1040px) holds the page’s primary text. Vertically, this text block is positioned slightly above the true center of the hero—roughly in the middle 30–40% of the vertical space—with generous breathing room above and below. The internal order of elements mirrors the original mockup: at the top of the block, a small, uppercase purple label like “PROGRAMS & GROUP CLASSES” sits left-aligned within the column. Below it, with about 24–32px spacing, a large white headline (e.g., “Find Your Perfect Group Session”) dominates. Another 24–32px below the headline, a single line or short paragraph of lighter-weight copy sits, also left-aligned, describing what the page offers. No additional controls or UI elements appear inside the hero to keep density low, and there are no sidebars or buttons competing with the text. The bottom edge of the hero aligns with the top edge of the filters section, maintaining a clean, linear break between the image-driven hero and the functional content below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static section anchored at the top of the page content, scrolling away as the user moves down. The background image scales to cover the full width and height of the hero area, with content always centered in the safe central column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The filters form sits immediately below the hero section, on a solid, light background. It is centered horizontally in the same content column as the hero text, spanning roughly 8–10 of the 12 grid columns and capped at about 1040px width to keep it comfortable on large screens. The block itself appears as a lightly elevated or bordered card with subtle rounding and a neutral background (slightly lighter or darker than the page background), providing a clear but understated container. Top margin from the hero is generous—around 64px—to preserve the spacious transition from editorial hero into functional controls. Inside this card, filters are arranged in 1–3 logical rows, depending on screen width, with 16–24px padding on all sides and 16–24px vertical spacing between rows. On desktop, key fields (e.g., keyword search, age group, level, days, time) are laid out in a horizontal grid: each control typically occupying 3–4 grid columns so that two or three inputs can sit side by side in a single row without feeling cramped. Labels are aligned left above each control, with consistent vertical spacing (8px) from input to maintain rhythm. The primary action control (e.g., ‘Apply’ or ‘Show Classes’) sits at the right end of the final row or aligned to the bottom-right of the card, using the brand’s purple accent color, with at least 24px top margin above it to visually separate actions from inputs. The entire filters area feels like a concise panel sitting just under the hero, clearly the first interactive module in the main flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block in the main document flow, expanding vertically as needed when filters wrap on narrower screens, always centered within the main content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The active filters bar appears directly below the filters form card, aligned to the same central content column. It occupies the full width of that column but is visually slim, with a height roughly 40–64px depending on the number of active filters. A top margin of about 16–24px separates it from the bottom of the filters card, keeping it visually connected as part of the same filtering zone without feeling crowded. Within the bar, a short label such as “Active filters” sits left-aligned, with applied filters shown as a horizontal row of pill-shaped chips aligned immediately to its right and wrapping to a second line if necessary. Each chip uses a very subtle outline or filled style with the purple accent for emphasis, but at a smaller scale and lighter weight than primary buttons to keep them secondary. A ‘Clear all’ text link or icon appears at the far right of the row, aligned on the same baseline as the chips. The background of the bar may be the same as the page background or a very subtle tint, ensuring it does not compete with the hero or the results; its purpose is to be lightly visible and immediately scannable just above the list. On mobile, chips wrap into multiple rows, but the bar remains within the same vertical band between filters and sorting/results.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, inline bar within the central column. It grows vertically as more filters are selected but always pushes content below it in a predictable manner.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sorting controls sit at the top edge of the results area, still within the same central column underneath the active filters bar. There is a 24–32px gap above them, creating a clear yet closely related transition from filtering to browsing results. On desktop, the sorting controls are right-aligned along a single horizontal line, typically sharing the row with an optional results summary on the left (e.g., ‘24 sessions found’), though that summary is part of the broader layout rather than this specific component. The main sorting dropdown or segmented control is compact, using minimal chrome, and may feature the purple accent in the active state or caret icon. It occupies roughly 2–3 grid columns on the right side, leaving negative space between itself and the left text to preserve the airy feel. The control is visually lighter than the filters card—no heavy container—so the user perceives it as a quick refinement tool rather than a major section. On smaller screens, the sort control drops beneath the results summary and becomes left-aligned, still sitting immediately above the first result card with about 16px of space below it.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element anchored at the top of the results list. It does not stick while scrolling; instead, it scrolls away with the results to maintain a simple, linear flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The session results list is the main content body of the page, occupying the full available width of the central column beneath the sorting controls. A vertical margin of about 16–24px separates the bottom of the sorting row from the top of the first result card, continuing the comfortable spacing rhythm. Each session appears as a card that spans nearly the width of the content column (leaving a modest inner margin to breathe), with internal padding of 16–24px and 24–32px vertical separation between one card and the next. Cards follow a clear internal hierarchy: at the top-left, a prominent session name, followed by key details (level, age group, location, dates/times) arranged in a structured grid or vertically stacked groups, depending on available width. A primary call-to-action (e.g., ‘View Details’ or ‘Book Now’) appears in the right-hand region of the card or at the bottom-right, highlighted with the purple accent. The cards maintain a mostly monochrome, light appearance, with only essential accents in purple and subtle lines or dividers to group content, echoing the clean, corporate sports aesthetic. The list extends vertically as many cards as needed, forming the longest section of the page and inviting continuous scroll. There are no sidebars intruding into this area; the eye travels straight down the column, card by card.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static vertical stack within the main flow. The list expands or contracts based on the number of results, pushing the pagination controls further down as needed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The pagination controls are placed at the very bottom of the main content column, directly beneath the last session card. A generous 32–40px margin separates the final card from the pagination row to create a clear visual endpoint for the list. The pagination itself is horizontally centered within the column, presenting a row of page numbers with previous/next arrows. The active page uses the purple accent to distinguish it, possibly as a filled circle or underlined number, while inactive pages remain neutral and minimal. The controls occupy only a small vertical band (roughly 40–64px high) with ample whitespace above and below so they feel light and unobtrusive. This creates a clean, calm terminus to the page content, mirroring the understated slider dots in the original hero mockup in terms of quiet, functional navigation at the edge of the main experience.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element anchored at the bottom of the results section. As the user navigates pages, the pagination row remains in this position relative to the results list, scrolling naturally with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The mini-cart summary is a compact, secondary module that sits visually adjacent to, but not inside, the main content column. On desktop, it appears as a small floating card anchored near the top-right side of the viewport, overlapping the background but staying outside the central text column’s main reading path. Position it roughly one content gutter to the right of the main column edge and around 96–120px below the global navigation (or just below the hero as the user scrolls), so it feels accessible but not dominant. The card width is about 280–320px, with internal padding of 16–24px and a vertical height that adapts to the number of items (typically 1–3 short lines plus a total). The design is minimal: light or white background, subtle shadow or border, and minimal text with a small purple-accented button or link (e.g., ‘View Cart’ or ‘Checkout’) aligned to the bottom-right of the card. Typography is smaller than the hero and primary controls to reinforce its secondary role. On mobile or narrow screens, the mini-cart drops into the main flow just above the pagination or fixed to the bottom edge of the screen as a slender bar, spanning 100% width with reduced height, ensuring it remains visible without obstructing content. In all cases, it avoids becoming a heavy sidebar and instead acts like a lightweight status panel the user can glance at while browsing sessions.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On desktop, behaves as a sticky floating card positioned relative to the viewport’s top-right region, staying visible while the user scrolls through the results. On mobile, either becomes a full-width bar fixed to the bottom of the viewport or a static block within the main flow, depending on implementation, always remaining visually unobtrusive.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component has no direct visual representation. Its behavior is reflected indirectly in how filters, sorting, pagination, and the results list stay in sync with the URL and browser history. From a layout perspective, it sits entirely behind the scenes, causing no shifts in spacing, sizing, or positioning of visible components. Instead, it ensures that when the user interacts with the filters form, active filters bar, sorting controls, or pagination, the page’s state can be restored or shared, while the visible layout remains stable and predictable.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, non-spatial behavior. It does not occupy space in the layout and does not affect flow; it only orchestrates state changes among the visible components.

name: Class Detail
title: Class Detail
description: Displays detailed information about a specific group class type and allows users to view upcoming sessions, apply date/time filters, select a session, and book it into their cart.

core_features: Load and display full details for the selected group class type using getGroupClassTypeDetail (including class information, coaches, and policies).
Display a list of upcoming sessions for the class type, retrieved via getClassSessionsForType, optionally filtered by date and time URL parameters.
Allow the user to adjust date and time filters for sessions, update the URL parameters accordingly, and re-fetch the sessions list using getClassSessionsForType.
Allow the user to select a specific session from the list, including honoring a pre-selected sessionId from the URL if present.
Provide a booking form (contact name, email, number of participants) that books the selected session via bookGroupClassSession and adds it to the unified cart.
Show an up-to-date cart/booking summary using getCartSummary and provide navigation to cart.html.
Provide navigation back to the programs list with audience and level parameters, and navigation to the coach profile page for the primary coach.
user_workflows:
View class type details:
1. User navigates to class_detail.html with a required id URL parameter.
2. On page load, the page reads id (and optional sessionId, startDate, endDate, timeFrom, timeTo) from the URL.
3. The page calls getGroupClassTypeDetail(id) to retrieve the GroupClassType, associated coach_profiles, and policies.
4. The class header, description, level, audience, focus, and policies are displayed using the returned data.
5. The primary coach (e.g., the first item in coach_profiles if available) is shown in the coach summary component.

Browse and filter upcoming sessions:
1. After loading the class details, the page calls getClassSessionsForType with classTypeId = id and any startDate, endDate, timeFrom, and timeTo values read from the URL.
2. The sessions list component displays all returned GroupClassSession records that are scheduled.
3. If a sessionId URL parameter is present, the component compares it against the loaded sessions and marks the matching session as selected.
4. The user can change filter values (date range and time window) in the filter component.
5. When filters are applied, the page updates the URL parameters (startDate, endDate, timeFrom, timeTo) and clears sessionId if the currently selected session would no longer be visible.
6. The page re-calls getClassSessionsForType with the updated parameters and refreshes the sessions list.
7. If no sessions match the filters, the sessions list component displays a message indicating that there are no available sessions for the selected criteria.

Select a session to book:
1. The user reviews the sessions list and clicks a 'Select' action on a specific session row.
2. The sessions list component updates its internal selectedSessionId state to the chosen GroupClassSession.id.
3. The page updates the URL parameter sessionId to the selected session’s id using history APIs (without reloading the page) so the selection is shareable and restorable.
4. The selected session is highlighted in the list and summarized in a dedicated selected-session summary component near the booking form.
5. If the user selects a different session, the selectedSessionId state and sessionId URL parameter are updated accordingly.

Book a selected session:
1. The user ensures a session is selected from the sessions list.
2. The user enters contact_name and contact_email (required) and optionally num_participants in the booking form.
3. On form submission, the booking form validates that a session is selected and that required fields are not empty and have valid formats.
4. If validation passes, the booking form calls bookGroupClassSession with classSessionId = selectedSessionId, contactName, contactEmail, and numParticipants.
5. On success, the page receives a booking (GroupClassBooking), the corresponding CartItem, the updated Cart, and a message from bookGroupClassSession.
6. The notification component shows a success message using the returned message.
7. The cart summary component is updated with the new Cart and CartItem data (either using the returned cart/cart_item or by re-calling getCartSummary, depending on implementation).
8. A 'View Cart / Booking Summary' button becomes active or remains visible; when clicked, the user navigates to cart.html.

View cart / booking summary:
1. At any point after initial load, the cart summary component calls getCartSummary (e.g., on page load and/or after a successful booking).
2. The component displays information derived from Cart and CartItem data (such as total amount and number of active items).
3. When the user clicks 'View Cart / Booking Summary', the page navigates to cart.html without additional parameters.

Navigate to related pages:
1. From the breadcrumb or 'Back to all classes' link, the page uses the GroupClassType.audience and GroupClassType.level values to construct a URL to programs.html with audience and level as URL parameters.
2. When the user clicks this link, they are taken to programs.html?audience={classAudience}&level={classLevel}.
3. From the coach summary panel, when the user clicks on the primary coach’s name or a 'View Coach Profile' action, the page navigates to coach_profile.html?id={primaryCoachId}, where primaryCoachId is the chosen Coach.id from the coach_profiles array.
interactions: On page load, read id, sessionId, startDate, endDate, timeFrom, and timeTo from the URL query string.
On page load, call getGroupClassTypeDetail(id) and bind the response to the class header, description, policies, and coach summary components.
On page load or on filter changes, call getClassSessionsForType(id, startDate, endDate, timeFrom, timeTo) and bind the returned GroupClassSession list to the sessions list component.
On page load and/or after booking, call getCartSummary() and bind Cart and CartItem data to the cart summary component.
When the user changes the date range filters, update the in-page filter state, update the URL parameters (startDate and endDate), clear sessionId if needed, and re-call getClassSessionsForType.
When the user changes the time window filters, update the in-page filter state, update the URL parameters (timeFrom and timeTo), clear sessionId if needed, and re-call getClassSessionsForType.
When the user clicks 'Select' on a specific session, set selectedSessionId, update the sessionId URL parameter, and highlight the selected session in the list.
When the user submits the booking form, validate the selected session and required fields, then call bookGroupClassSession with the selected session and form data.
On successful booking, update cart-related state using the returned booking, cart_item, and cart, and show a success notification.
On booking failure (e.g., validation error from the interface or no remaining spots), display an error notification without changing URL parameters.
When the user clicks 'View Cart / Booking Summary', navigate to cart.html.
When the user clicks the breadcrumb or 'Back to all classes', navigate to programs.html with audience and level parameters derived from GroupClassType.
When the user clicks on a coach name or 'View Coach Profile', navigate to coach_profile.html with the selected Coach.id as the id URL parameter.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The primary state of the Class Detail page is driven by URL parameters and the data returned from the assigned interfaces. On initial load, the page reads the required id query parameter and optional sessionId, startDate, endDate, timeFrom, and timeTo parameters from the URL. If id is missing or invalid, the page shows an appropriate error state and does not attempt to call getGroupClassTypeDetail or getClassSessionsForType.

The page immediately calls getGroupClassTypeDetail with classTypeId = id. The returned GroupClassType is stored in page state and drives multiple behaviors: it populates the header, description, and policies sections; provides the audience and level values for constructing the 'Back to all classes' link; and supplies coach information for the coach summary and navigation to coach_profile.html.

In parallel or after that, the page calls getClassSessionsForType with classTypeId = id and the optional date/time filters from the URL (startDate, endDate, timeFrom, and timeTo). These values are not hardcoded; they are taken directly from the URL if present. The sessions list is then rendered with all returned GroupClassSession objects that are scheduled. After the sessions are loaded, if a sessionId parameter exists in the URL, the page searches the loaded sessions for a session with a matching id. If found, that session id is stored as selectedSessionId in state and visually marked as selected. If no matching session is found, selectedSessionId remains unset, and the sessionId parameter may be left as-is or cleared on the next state-changing interaction.

The filter controls (date range and time window) are also initialized from the URL parameters when the page loads. When the user updates filters and applies them, the new filter values replace the existing values in the URL query string (startDate, endDate, timeFrom, timeTo) via history.pushState or history.replaceState. The id parameter is preserved. If the currently selected session would no longer be present under the new filters, the selectedSessionId state is cleared and the sessionId parameter is removed from the URL. After updating the URL, the page calls getClassSessionsForType again with the updated filter values and re-renders the sessions list.

When the user selects a session from the sessions list, the page updates selectedSessionId in its internal state and synchronizes this with the URL by setting or replacing the sessionId query parameter. The id and any filter parameters (startDate, endDate, timeFrom, timeTo) are preserved. This ensures that if the user refreshes the page or shares the URL, the same class type, filters, and selected session are restored.

The booking form state (contact name, email, and number of participants) is kept locally in the component and is not persisted to the URL. When the form is submitted and bookGroupClassSession is called, no URL parameters are changed automatically. If the booking is successful, the page may optionally clear or reset the booking form fields while leaving the selectedSessionId and sessionId intact, so the user can see which session was booked.

Cart state is derived from getCartSummary or from the cart object returned by bookGroupClassSession. The page does not store cart identifiers in the URL; instead, it relies on the backend or session mechanism behind getCartSummary to identify the user’s current cart. The cart summary component is refreshed on page load and after a successful booking, ensuring that the displayed total and item count remain consistent with the server-side cart.
