At all times, the canonical source of state that should persist across reloads or be shareable between users is the URL query string. No localStorage or other client-side persistence is used. All dynamic content and state beyond the URL parameters are reconstructed from the assigned interfaces on each page load.

components.functionality: Displays a breadcrumb or 'Back to all classes' link that navigates back to the programs list using the current class type's audience and level.
Shows the main class information for the selected GroupClassType, including name, audience, level, and focus, after loading via getGroupClassTypeDetail.
Displays the full description text of the selected group class type.
Shows class-specific policies returned from getGroupClassTypeDetail (e.g., cancellation rules or attendance expectations).
Displays coach information associated with this class type (e.g., primary coach name, photo, key information) and allows navigation to an individual coach profile page.

Allows the user to adjust filters for upcoming sessions by start date, end date, earliest time, and latest time. Initializes values from URL parameters and updates the URL and sessions list when filters change.
Lists upcoming GroupClassSession instances for the current class type, filtered by the current filter settings, and lets the user select one session to book.
Summarizes the currently selected session (date, time, price, remaining spots) based on the selectedSessionId state, helping the user confirm they are booking the intended session.

Collects contact name, contact email, and optional number of participants for the selected session and submits the booking via bookGroupClassSession, then updates cart and notifications based on the response.

Displays a concise summary of the current cart state (e.g., total amount and number of active items) using getCartSummary or updated cart data from bookGroupClassSession, and provides a button to navigate to cart.html.
Shows transient success or error messages related to loading, filtering, and booking actions, using messages from bookGroupClassSession responses and client-side validation errors.

Tracks and exposes loading states for getGroupClassTypeDetail, getClassSessionsForType, bookGroupClassSession, and getCartSummary, allowing other components to show or hide loading indicators accordingly.
Displays error messages when required URL parameters are missing, when interfaces return errors, or when no class or sessions can be loaded, and may suggest navigation back to the programs page.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content column for the hero text, over a full-width background image. Navigation items appear distributed horizontally across the top within the same grid.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows a linear, top-to-bottom, hero-first marketing layout. At the very top, the existing global navigation and hero pattern remain: full-bleed tennis photography with a dark overlay and centered text column. Within that centered column, the class-breadcrumb-navigation appears at the top as a small, subtle line, followed by the class-detail-header as the main hero text block. Below the hero, content is organized as stacked, full-width sections using a centered content column (approx. 10–12 column grid, max-width around 1040–1200px). The informational panels (class description, policies, coach summary) appear first, followed by the sessions area (filters, list, chosen session, booking form), and finally a persistent cart-summary-banner at the bottom edge of the viewport. Status elements (notification, loading, error) sit above or over this flow without disrupting the core stack.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows a linear, top-to-bottom, hero-first marketing layout. At the very top, the existing global navigation and hero pattern remain: full-bleed tennis photography with a dark overlay and centered text column. Within that centered column, the class-breadcrumb-navigation appears at the top as a small, subtle line, followed by the class-detail-header as the main hero text block. Below the hero, content is organized as stacked, full-width sections using a centered content column (approx. 10–12 column grid, max-width around 1040–1200px). The informational panels (class description, policies, coach summary) appear first, followed by the sessions area (filters, list, chosen session, booking form), and finally a persistent cart-summary-banner at the bottom edge of the viewport. Status elements (notification, loading, error) sit above or over this flow without disrupting the core stack.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero occupies essentially 100% of the initial viewport height, with the central text column taking roughly 30–40% of the vertical space and centered horizontally at about 60–70% of page width (capped with a max-width). The breadcrumb and class-detail-header share this central hero column. Below the hero, each major section (info panels, sessions block) spans the full page width with content confined to the central column. Vertical spacing between major sections is generous (64–96px), and individual components within a section use 24–32px vertical gaps. Padding inside panels and bars is around 16–24px. The cart-summary-banner occupies a slim horizontal band (56–72px) at the bottom of the viewport and spans full width, staying visually light but always accessible.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout maintains a low to medium content density. The hero remains very sparse: just breadcrumb + header text in the center. Below, panels are clean, with clear headings, short paragraphs, and comfortable spacing. Text is broken into digestible chunks with breathing room (24–32px between headings, body text, and controls). The sessions list is the densest area but still uses clear row separation (16–24px between session cards) and ample margins left and right within the central column. No sidebars or tightly packed multi-column text blocks dominate; everything is vertically stacked and easy to scan.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow starts at the hero’s large white class headline (class-detail-header), then moves slightly up to the breadcrumb for orientation, and down to any short summary line within the header. From there, the user scrolls, encountering the class-description-section first for context, then the policies and coach overview. The eye is then drawn to the sessions-filter-bar as the primary interactive entry point, down into the sessions-list where specific options are scanned. When a session is selected, attention naturally shifts slightly downward to the selected-session-summary and then to the session-booking-form as the key action area. Throughout, the cart-summary-banner at the bottom edge acts as a persistent peripheral cue for progress, while notification-center, error-state-display, and loading-state-manager temporarily take precedence when active by appearing above or over the main content column.

layout.overall_description: The page extends the hero-driven marketing layout from the mockup into a transactional class detail experience. At the top, the existing global navigation sits over a full-viewport hero with a darkened tennis background. In the center of this hero, a single, narrow column holds a subtle breadcrumb at the top and an assertive, white class title with a short description beneath—mirroring the mockup’s ‘WELCOME TO’ label, headline, and body stack. As the user scrolls, the hero gives way to a white or very light main content area, still using a centered column aligned to the same 12-column grid. First, a series of vertically stacked, card-like information panels explain the class, policies, and coach, each separated by generous vertical whitespace so the page feels editorial rather than cluttered. Below that, a dedicated sessions section presents filters at the top, then a tall list of upcoming sessions, then the selected session’s summary and the booking form, all within the same central column. A slim cart summary band subtly anchors itself to the very bottom of the viewport, spanning edge-to-edge but using the same typography and purple accent as the hero, reinforcing continuity. Notifications appear as slim banners beneath the global nav, while loading and error states temporarily overlay or replace sections, but the underlying structure remains a simple, vertical, centered flow that echoes the hero’s clarity and modern sports aesthetic.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb navigation sits inside the hero’s central text column, toward the upper portion of that column. Horizontally, it is left-aligned within the column, which itself is centered on the page and spans roughly 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop (max-width ~1040px). Vertically, it appears about 64px below the global navigation bar and 16–24px above the class-detail-header, using a small, light-weight type. There is ample empty space above and below so it feels like a subtle orientation element rather than a dense control strip. On scroll, it moves away with the hero (no stickiness), keeping the initial, spacious hero feel intact.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scrolls with the hero content; no sticky or fixed behavior. Always confined to the hero’s centered content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The class-detail-header forms the core of the hero content, mirroring the mockup’s central label–headline–subcopy stack. It sits directly beneath the breadcrumb within the same central text column, left-aligned but visually centered in the page. The header block vertically occupies roughly the central 30–40% of the hero height, with generous top and bottom breathing room (at least 96px above the topmost text and 96px below the last line). Within this block, a small purple label (e.g., class category) appears first, with 8–12px spacing to a large white headline that spans 2–3 lines at most and uses about 70–80% of the column width. 24–32px below the headline, a short, white or light-grey subheading/body line provides a concise class summary. The whole block is centered vertically between the nav and the bottom of the hero background, keeping it the primary focal point against the dark tennis image overlay.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the hero; centered vertically and horizontally relative to the hero area. Scrolls away as the user moves down the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The class-description-section is the first major content block immediately below the hero. It sits within a full-width section whose content is constrained to the same centered column as the hero text. The top edge of the description section begins approximately 80–96px below the bottom of the hero, reinforcing a clear transition. The panel itself spans roughly 70–80% of the page width on desktop (max-width ~1040px) and is centered. Inside, there is 24–32px of padding on all sides. A section title sits at the top left of the panel, with 16–24px of space below it before the body copy begins. Paragraphs are spaced with 16px line gaps to maintain a clean, readable block. There is at least 48–64px of vertical space below this section before the next info panel begins, keeping the density low and preserving an editorial feel consistent with the hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scroll-bound, stacked in the main content flow. Behaves as a standard panel within the central content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The class-policies-section is positioned directly beneath the class-description-section, with a vertical gap of about 48–64px. It shares the same central column width and alignment, spanning approximately 70–80% of the page width and centered under the hero axis. The panel uses a similar 24–32px internal padding and left-aligned text. A clear heading such as “Policies” appears at the top with 16–24px spacing to the list or paragraph content. Individual policy items (if listed) are separated by 12–16px vertical gaps, preventing visual crowding. The panel’s visual weight is slightly lighter than the description, so it feels like supporting information. Another 48–64px of vertical spacing separates it from the coach-summary-panel below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard stacked panel; no sticky behavior. Remains within the central column and scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coach-summary-panel appears below the class-policies-section, again separated by roughly 48–64px of vertical whitespace. It aligns with the same centered content column and width (about 70–80% of the page, max-width ~1040px). Internally, it uses 24–32px padding and a left-aligned layout. A coach heading sits at the top, with 16–24px below for coach name, role, and a compact bio. If a coach photo is used, it appears to the left of the text on larger screens but still contained within the same panel width, with 16–24px horizontal separation. The overall treatment remains clean and minimal, relying on whitespace rather than heavy borders. Below this panel, a more substantial vertical gap (64–80px) introduces the shift into the more interactive sessions area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scroll-bound supporting panel that remains within the main centered column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sessions-filter-bar marks the top of the interactive sessions section. It sits centered on the page directly below the coach-summary-panel, with about 64–80px of vertical spacing between them to denote a new functional area. The filter bar spans nearly the full width of the central column (around 80% of the page width, capped at ~1040px), with 16–24px of padding inside. Controls (date pickers, dropdowns, chips) are aligned horizontally where space allows, but remain in a single line or two clean rows, preserving the minimal aesthetic. The bar sits slightly above the sessions-list (24–32px separation). On scroll, once the user has scrolled past its initial position, the bar can become sticky within the viewport’s top edge, remaining visible while the sessions-list scrolls beneath, maintaining clarity and control without adding heavy chrome.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Initially scrolls with content but becomes a sticky element at the top of the viewport within the main content area, maintaining full-width alignment to the centered column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sessions-list sits immediately below the sessions-filter-bar, with 24–32px of vertical spacing. It fills most of the sessions section’s vertical real estate and is contained within the same central content column (approximately 80% of page width, max-width ~1040px). Each session appears as a horizontal row or card with 16–24px internal padding and clear separation (16–24px vertical gaps) between items, keeping the list visually light despite its relative density. The list extends downward, potentially beyond the viewport height, encouraging vertical scrolling while the sticky filter remains at the top. There is a generous margin (48–64px) between the bottom of the last list item and the selected-session-summary, making it clear that the summary and booking area are a subsequent step. The list doesn’t introduce sidebars or complex multi-column layouts; it is a single centered column of session options.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scrolls within the page as a tall, central list beneath the sticky filter bar. No fixed positioning; it simply extends downward within the main content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The selected-session-summary appears below the sessions-list, separated by about 48–64px to visually indicate a transition from browsing to confirming. It occupies the same centered column and width (around 70–80% of the page width, max-width ~1040px), presented as a compact info panel with 16–24px padding and clear typography. The content is left-aligned within the panel but the panel itself is centered on the page. The summary is concise—key details like date, time, level, and coach—laid out in 1–2 short lines or a tight block. It sits 24–32px above the session-booking-form, so the eye moves naturally from the details into the action controls in one continuous flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline panel within the main scroll; no sticky behavior. It appears only when a session is selected or shows a placeholder prompt in the same spot when none is selected.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The session-booking-form sits directly beneath the selected-session-summary with a 24–32px vertical gap, forming the core transactional area of the page. It shares the same centered alignment and width as the summary (about 70–80% of page width, max-width ~1040px), with 24–32px of internal padding. Form fields are stacked vertically in a single column to maintain the clean, linear flow, with 16–24px vertical spacing between fields and 24–32px above the primary call-to-action button. The submit/‘Add to Cart’ button spans approximately 40–60% of the form width and is centered or left-aligned consistently, using the purple brand accent to echo the hero label. The area below the form has at least 80–96px of whitespace before the bottom of the content, so the form does not feel cramped against the cart-summary-banner at the viewport bottom.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scroll-bound and inline, remaining in place relative to the selected-session-summary. It does not stick, but the persistent cart-summary-banner keeps the outcome of the form visible.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart-summary-banner is a slim, horizontal strip pinned to the very bottom of the viewport, spanning the full page width edge-to-edge, independent of the central content column. Its height is modest—around 56–72px—with 16–24px of internal horizontal padding. The content inside (items in cart, subtotal, and a compact ‘View Cart’ or ‘Checkout’ button) is aligned to the same central axis as the rest of the page: the text and button block are centered within a max-width container matching the main column. The background can be slightly darker than the main content area for contrast, with white text and a purple action button. It floats above the main scrollable content, so as users scroll through sessions and the booking form, this banner remains visible at the bottom edge, without overlapping the main hero or nav.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed to the bottom of the viewport, full-width. Remains visible on all scroll positions, with content centered to align visually with the main content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The notification-center appears as a slim, full-width banner directly below the global navigation bar and above the hero content. When visible, it spans 100% of the viewport width but constrains its text to the same central max-width as the main content, aligning horizontally with the hero’s text column. Its height ranges from 40–56px depending on content, with 8–16px vertical padding. The background contrasts softly with the hero (e.g., tinted dark or a semi-opaque overlay) so it is noticeable but not overpowering. It pushes the hero content slightly downward while active, preserving the overall vertical rhythm and avoiding overlap with the central hero heading.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears at the top of the content stack beneath the global nav. Scrolls away with the page; no sticky behavior. Can stack multiple notifications vertically if needed with 4–8px gaps.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The loading-state-manager manifests as a subtle, full-viewport overlay that sits above all page content when a major operation (e.g., loading sessions or submitting the booking form) is in progress. It dims the underlying hero and page sections with a semi-transparent dark layer while keeping the general layout visible. At the visual center of the viewport, aligned with the same central vertical axis as the hero text, a minimalist spinner or progress indicator appears, accompanied by a short status label. The overlay respects ample breathing room, with no dense content—just the indicator and a brief line of text. The user still perceives the hero and central column structure behind the dimmed overlay, which helps anchor them even during loading.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed, full-screen overlay above all other components when active. It prevents interaction with underlying components until loading completes, then disappears to reveal the standard scrollable layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error-state-display appears inline within the main content column wherever a failure occurs (for example, in place of the sessions-list if sessions fail to load). It uses the same central alignment and width (about 70–80% of page width, max-width ~1040px) with 24–32px internal padding. The error panel sits with generous vertical margins (48–64px above and below) so it stands out without feeling cramped. A concise error heading appears at the top, followed by a short explanation and a retry action button or link. The styling is consistent with the site’s minimalist aesthetic—clear text, subtle border or background tint, and possibly the purple accent for the retry action—so it feels integrated, not like an external alert. When global or critical errors occur, this panel can also be displayed nearer the top of the content area, just below the hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, replacing or pushing aside the affected content area within the central column. Scrolls with the page and does not use fixed positioning.

name: Memberships
title: Memberships
description: View, filter, and compare tennis academy membership plans by price and included group sessions per month, and navigate to detailed plan pages or contact for more information.

core_features: Fetch and display all active membership plans using listMembershipPlans with optional minimum included group sessions per month.
Apply a minimum included group sessions threshold (from URL or user input) to either filter or visually highlight matching membership plans.
Display a side-by-side comparison table of basic membership attributes (e.g., monthly price, included group sessions, commitment) to help identify cheaper options.
Navigate from any membership plan to its detail page (membership_detail.html?id=...) for full information and plan-specific inquiries.
Show a cart summary using getCartSummary to keep users aware of their current bookings or purchases while browsing memberships, and provide navigation to the cart page if implemented.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Arrive with preselected minimum sessions and highlighted plan
1. User lands on memberships.html, possibly from index.html or membership_detail.html, with URL parameters minSessions and/or highlightPlanId.
2. On load, the page controller reads minSessions and highlightPlanId from the URL.
3. The page calls listMembershipPlans with onlyActive=true and minIncludedGroupSessionsPerMonth equal to the minSessions value if the current mode is set to filter; otherwise it calls the interface without that minimum and uses minSessions only for visual highlighting.
4. The membership list renders all returned plans, with plans that meet or exceed the minSessions threshold visually distinguished when in highlight mode.
5. The comparison table displays all plans, sorted by a default order (e.g., monthly price ascending), visually emphasizing the plan whose id equals highlightPlanId.
6. User may click any plan to go to membership_detail.html?id=<plan_id> or choose to adjust the minimum sessions filter in the controls, which updates the URL and triggers a refresh of the listing.

Workflow: Manually filter plans by minimum group sessions per month
1. User opens memberships.html without URL parameters; all active membership plans are loaded via listMembershipPlans with onlyActive=true and no minimum sessions filter.
2. User interacts with the filter controls to set a desired minimum number of included group sessions per month.
3. User chooses whether this minimum should filter out non-matching plans or only highlight matching ones (e.g., via a toggle or radio buttons).
4. On confirmation (e.g., clicking an Apply button), the filter component updates the URL query string with minSessions and the current mode if represented, and the page controller re-calls listMembershipPlans accordingly.
5. The membership list updates to either show only qualifying plans or show all plans with qualifying ones highlighted. The comparison table synchronizes with the current set of plans and highlighting.
6. User can reset filters, which clears related URL parameters, re-calls listMembershipPlans without a minimum sessions filter, and returns the UI to the unfiltered state.

Workflow: Compare and select a cheaper membership plan
1. User views the membership list and comparison table, which are populated from listMembershipPlans results.
2. The comparison table computes relative cost information (e.g., identifies the lowest monthly_price among the displayed plans) based on the MembershipPlan data and visually marks cheaper options.
3. User scans the table to identify a plan that fits their budget and session needs; they may click column headers or controls to change sort order (e.g., sort by monthly_price or included_group_sessions_per_month).
4. User clicks either the membership card or a 'Learn more' link for the chosen plan, triggering navigation to membership_detail.html?id=<selectedMembershipPlanId>.
5. Optionally, user uses a dedicated 'Contact us about memberships' link to navigate to contact.html?subject=generalMembershipQuestion if they have a general question instead of selecting a specific plan.
interactions: User selects or edits a numeric value for minimum included group sessions per month in the filter controls, which updates the URL's minSessions parameter and reconfigures the call to listMembershipPlans depending on the chosen mode (filter vs. highlight).

User toggles between using the minimum sessions as a hard filter (only show plans with at least that many included sessions) or as a visual highlight (show all plans but emphasize those meeting the threshold). This toggle updates internal page state and may be encoded in the URL so that the state persists on reload or share.

User clicks on a membership card or a 'Learn more' link for a specific MembershipPlan; this triggers navigation to membership_detail.html with the selected plan's id passed as a URL parameter (id=<selectedMembershipPlanId>).

User clicks a control such as 'Highlight in comparison' on a given plan, which updates the URL's highlightPlanId parameter and causes the comparison table and list to visually highlight that plan column/row.
User clicks a generic 'Contact Us About Memberships' link, which navigates to contact.html?subject=generalMembershipQuestion as specified in the outgoing connections.

User interacts with sorting controls in the comparison table (e.g., clicking column headers) to change the sort key or direction, which reorders the displayed plans client-side without additional API calls.

On initial page load and whenever the page is revisited or the URL changes, the page controller automatically calls getCartSummary to fetch the current cart and line items, and the cart summary component updates to display the latest totals and item count.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page state is driven primarily by URL query parameters and the results of listMembershipPlans and getCartSummary.

URL parameters:
- minSessions (number): When present, this value is interpreted as the desired minimum included_group_sessions_per_month. On load or URL change, the page controller reads this parameter. If the current mode is 'filter', it passes this value to listMembershipPlans as minIncludedGroupSessionsPerMonth. If the mode is 'highlight' or if no mode is specified but the implementation chooses to use visual highlighting, the controller calls listMembershipPlans without that minimum and uses minSessions only in client-side highlighting logic.
- highlightPlanId (string): When present, components that display plans (list and comparison table) compare each MembershipPlan.id against this value and visually emphasize any matching plan. This parameter is not sent to the backend; it is used purely for front-end state.

Default behavior:
- If minSessions is absent, the controller calls listMembershipPlans with onlyActive=true and without a minimum sessions filter, effectively retrieving all active plans. The filter controls show an empty or neutral state (no minimum applied).
- If highlightPlanId is absent, no specific plan is specially highlighted beyond any 'cheapest plan' or threshold-based highlighting defined in the UI.

State updates:
- Changing filter values or modes in the UI updates the URL query string (e.g., adding or changing minSessions, and optionally a mode parameter) via navigation or history APIs, then triggers the controller to refresh data by re-calling listMembershipPlans with appropriate arguments.
- Interactions that change the highlighted plan (e.g., 'Highlight in comparison') update highlightPlanId in the URL. Components listen to URL changes via the controller and re-render highlighting accordingly.
- The cart summary state comes exclusively from getCartSummary; it is refreshed on initial load and can optionally be refreshed on an interval or after navigation from other booking-related pages. No cart-related state is stored in localStorage; if a full refresh occurs, getCartSummary is called again to repopulate the widget.

All dynamic behavior, including filtering, highlighting, comparison, and cart display, derives from API responses and URL parameters, without using any hardcoded membership data or persistent client storage.

components.functionality: Coordinates data loading, URL parameter parsing, and state distribution for the Memberships page. On initialization and whenever the URL changes, it reads minSessions and highlightPlanId from the query string, determines whether the current mode should filter or highlight based on internal logic or an optional URL mode parameter, and calls listMembershipPlans with onlyActive=true and an optional minIncludedGroupSessionsPerMonth argument. It also calls getCartSummary to fetch the cart and passes all resulting data and derived state (such as current minimum sessions, current highlight plan, and comparison sort settings) to child components.

Provides user inputs to set a minimum included group sessions per month threshold and to choose how this threshold should be applied (filter vs. highlight). It displays the current minimum value based on the minSessions URL parameter or an empty state when not set. When the user applies changes, it requests the page controller to update the URL (setting or clearing minSessions and optionally a mode indicator), which triggers a new call to listMembershipPlans as needed. It can also expose a reset action that clears the minimum and returns to the unfiltered state.

Renders the list of MembershipPlan entities returned by the controller as individual cards or rows. Each card displays key plan attributes such as name, monthly_price, included_group_sessions_per_month, brief description, and min_commitment_months if available. It uses the current minSessions and filter/highlight mode to either hide plans that do not meet the minimum (filter mode) or visually highlight those that do (highlight mode). It also highlights any plan whose id matches highlightPlanId. Each plan includes actions to open the plan detail page and optionally to designate it as the highlighted plan for comparison.

Displays a side-by-side comparison of the currently loaded MembershipPlan objects. For each plan, it shows at least the name, monthly_price, included_group_sessions_per_month, min_commitment_months where available, and a subset of benefits. It computes derived attributes such as the minimum monthly_price across all displayed plans and can mark plans with that value as cheaper options. The table uses highlightPlanId to visually emphasize a particular plan and can apply additional emphasis to plans meeting or exceeding the minSessions threshold when in highlight mode. Sorting controls allow the user to reorder the table by basic attributes such as monthly_price or included_group_sessions_per_month, with sort state managed by the controller. Clicking a plan name or a dedicated action cell navigates to membership_detail.html?id=<plan_id>.

Shows a concise summary of the user’s current cart based on getCartSummary. It displays information such as the number of items (derived from the length of the items array) and total_amount from the Cart entity. It refreshes whenever the controller re-fetches cart data. An optional action allows navigation to a dedicated cart page (e.g., cart.html) using URL-based navigation without modifying cart contents.

Provides a generic action for users who want to ask about memberships without selecting a specific plan. When clicked, it navigates to contact.html with the subject parameter set to generalMembershipQuestion as defined in the outgoing connections, enabling the contact page to pre-fill or interpret the subject accordingly.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content column for the hero text, over a full-width background image. Navigation items appear distributed horizontally across the top within the same grid.
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps the mockup’s linear, top‑to‑bottom flow: global navigation at the very top (outside this spec), followed by a full‑height hero dedicated to the memberships page controller. Directly beneath the hero, a narrow band hosts the membership filter controls. Below that, the membership plan list occupies the main scrolling area, followed by a full‑width membership comparison table section. A compact cart summary widget floats over the right side (or bottom on mobile) as a persistent secondary element. The memberships contact link appears centered near the bottom of the comparison section as the final on‑page call to action.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps the mockup’s linear, top‑to‑bottom flow: global navigation at the very top (outside this spec), followed by a full‑height hero dedicated to the memberships page controller. Directly beneath the hero, a narrow band hosts the membership filter controls. Below that, the membership plan list occupies the main scrolling area, followed by a full‑width membership comparison table section. A compact cart summary widget floats over the right side (or bottom on mobile) as a persistent secondary element. The memberships contact link appears centered near the bottom of the comparison section as the final on‑page call to action.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero (page controller) consumes the full viewport height, visually dominated by a darkened tennis background image. Within it, the central text/controller block occupies roughly the central 30–40% of the vertical space. The filter controls sit in a low‑height, full‑width band with 64px vertical padding, while the plan list takes up the majority of the scrollable area below, within a centered max‑width column (≈1100–1200px). The comparison table forms its own section with generous 64–96px top and bottom padding. The cart summary widget uses a small footprint (about 20–25% of viewport width on desktop) and floats in the right gutter. Horizontal padding follows the grid, keeping all primary content within a center‑aligned 12‑column layout.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density remains low and airy, mirroring the hero’s marketing feel. Each major section has clear breathing room: 64–96px vertical separation between sections, 24–32px spacing between headings and body text, and 16–24px between controls within each component. The plan cards and comparison table cells are spaced to feel readable and uncluttered, favoring fewer, clearer elements over dense information. The cart summary is concise, with minimal text and clear hierarchy, and the contact link is a single, unobtrusive line of text.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye is first drawn to the large white memberships headline in the hero, then up to the small purple label above it, and down to any descriptive text or subtle controls in the page controller. Scrolling, attention moves to the centered filter band, then to the membership cards directly beneath as the primary decision area. As users look for deeper details, the flow continues down into the comparison table. The cart summary widget remains visually available but off to the side so it doesn’t compete with the main content. Finally, the purple‑accented contact link at the bottom center provides a tidy endpoint, reinforcing the brand accent color and offering a next step.

layout.overall_description: The Memberships page opens with a full‑bleed tennis court hero, darkened with a subtle overlay so a centered column of white typography stands out crisply in the middle of the viewport: a small purple label, a bold memberships headline, and a short supporting line, all aligned to the invisible 12‑column grid. Below this immersive hero, the page transitions into a calm, dark content area where a centered band of filter controls stretches across about four‑fifths of the page width. Underneath, the main column of membership cards runs down the page, aligned centrally and spaced evenly with generous gaps, while a slim cart summary panel quietly hovers in the right gutter, following the scroll. Further down, a full‑width comparison section presents a clean, horizontally scrolling or wide table anchored to the same central grid, with ample padding around it. The section ends with a single, purple‑accented contact link centered beneath the table, framed by extra whitespace before the global footer, maintaining the overall modern, image‑driven, and minimally decorated aesthetic seen in the original mockup.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The memberships page controller sits within the hero section, which fills 100% of the viewport height and the full browser width, using a full‑bleed tennis background image with a dark overlay. The controller content is placed in a single, center‑aligned column that spans roughly 60–70% of the page width on desktop (max-width about 800–900px), horizontally centered within the 12‑column grid. Vertically, this column is positioned in the middle third of the hero: there is generous empty space (≈25–30% of viewport height) above and below the content to preserve the spacious, editorial look. Inside the column, a small purple, uppercase label (e.g., “MEMBERSHIPS”) is aligned left within the column but still centered on the page overall, with approximately 16–24px of space above the main headline. The primary headline is large, bold, and white, occupying up to two or three lines and visually dominating the center of the hero. Below the headline, a short line or two of supporting body copy sits with 24–32px vertical spacing, also left‑aligned within the column. If any page‑level controls (e.g., a toggle between “Cards” and “Compare”) are present, they appear as a subtle horizontal row directly beneath the body copy, center‑aligned within the same column and spaced 24px below the text. The navigation bar sits as a slim, translucent band at the very top of the page (outside this component), leaving about 64px of visual breathing room between the bottom of the nav and the start of the hero’s text block. The overall arrangement ensures the hero feels clean, with the text stack clearly dominating the center against the darkened image.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The hero and its page controller are static within the normal document flow, occupying the full initial viewport height. As the user scrolls, the hero scrolls offscreen; it is not sticky or fixed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership filter controls occupy a dedicated band immediately below the hero, spanning the full page width but constrained to a centered content area with a max-width of about 1100–1200px. This band begins roughly 64–80px below the bottom edge of the hero, maintaining clear separation between the immersive image and the functional content. The vertical padding of the band is generous—approximately 24–32px above and below the controls—to keep the low‑density feel. On desktop, the filters form a single horizontal row across the center of the page, approximately 70–80% of the content width, with individual control groups (e.g., price range, sessions per month, plan type) evenly distributed from left to right within the grid. Controls are aligned on a common baseline and separated by 16–24px horizontal gaps. The entire row is centered relative to the page, not hugging either edge. On narrower viewports (tablet and mobile), the controls flow into two columns or a vertical stack while remaining centered, with 16–24px vertical spacing between rows. The band’s background continues the dark theme from below the hero (slightly lighter than the hero overlay), ensuring the white and light‑gray control labels remain legible without competing visually with the hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Once the user scrolls so that the filter band reaches the top of the viewport, it becomes sticky: the band pins to the top edge and remains visible while the user scrolls through the membership plan list, then releases as the user reaches the comparison table and footer. On mobile, the sticky behavior persists but the band occupies the full width, with careful height management to avoid covering too much of the viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership plan list forms the main content block directly beneath the filter controls. It sits in a full‑width section whose background matches or slightly darkens the filter band, but all substantive content is constrained to a center‑aligned column across the 12‑column grid, with a max-width of roughly 1100–1200px. There is a 32–48px vertical gap between the bottom of the filter controls and the top of the first row of plans, giving the list its own breathing room. On desktop, memberships are presented as cards arranged in a grid of three columns per row (each card occupying roughly 3–4 grid columns with consistent gutters), centered so that the grid feels balanced left and right. Each card has internal padding of around 24px, with 24–32px vertical spacing between the card’s internal elements (plan name, price, key features), echoing the hero’s typography spacing. Vertically, rows of cards are separated by approximately 32–48px, maintaining a low‑density, premium feel. The cards do not stretch to the page edge; instead, they float within the central column with at least 40–64px of horizontal margin from the outer viewport edges. On tablet, this grid collapses to two cards per row; on mobile, to a single full‑width card per row, each still within the consistent side padding. The list section extends for as many rows as needed, with at least 64px of clear space at the bottom before transitioning into the comparison table section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The membership plan list scrolls normally within the document flow. It benefits from the sticky filter band above and the floating cart summary widget to the side, but itself has no fixed or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership comparison table resides in a distinct section that starts after a generous vertical gap—approximately 80–96px—below the last row of membership cards, signaling a shift from browsing to detailed comparison. This section uses a full‑width background band, slightly differentiated in shade from the plan list’s background (e.g., marginally lighter or darker within the same dark palette), while the content is still constrained to a centered column aligned to the same 12‑column grid (max-width around 1100–1200px). At the top of this section, a centered heading such as “Compare Plans” appears, left‑aligned within the column but visually centered on the page, with 16–24px of space above any subheading or descriptive line. The table itself begins about 32px below the heading. On desktop, the table spans nearly the full width of the content column, with plan names as columns and comparison attributes as rows. Cell content uses a restrained, readable density with 16–24px vertical padding per row and clean horizontal dividers, maintaining the minimalist, corporate sports look. There is at least 32–48px of padding between the outer edges of the table and the section’s top and bottom boundaries so the table feels framed rather than cramped. On smaller screens, the table may become horizontally scrollable within the centered column, keeping a clear 16px margin to the viewport edges, or switch to a stacked mobile comparison format, but still preserves the same central alignment and ample vertical spacing. The section ends with a roomy 48–64px padding below the table before the contact link or footer content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The comparison table section scrolls normally and anchors in the main flow. The table may allow horizontal scrolling within its own container on small viewports while remaining vertically fixed in place.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart summary widget is a compact panel that floats independently of the main vertical content, designed to be visible yet unobtrusive. On desktop and large tablets, it appears along the right side of the viewport, aligned vertically around the middle of the membership plan list area. It sits inside the page’s right gutter: its left edge aligns roughly with the outer edge of the main content column, leaving about 24–32px gap between the rightmost plan card and the widget, and about 24–32px from the viewport’s right edge. The widget is approximately 280–320px wide and has 16–24px internal padding all around. Its height is compact—roughly equivalent to 2–3 card rows of content—and it uses a slightly lighter dark background with a subtle elevation (shadow) to distinguish it, while still respecting the minimal aesthetic. The typography follows the hierarchy: plan summary title at the top, followed by selected plan names and price, with 8–16px spacing between lines. It never overlaps the filter controls or hero; it only becomes visible once the user has scrolled into the plan list area. On mobile, the widget reflows into a full‑width horizontal bar anchored near the bottom of the viewport: its height is about 64–80px, stretching from left to right with 16px side padding and a simple one‑ or two‑line summary plus a primary action button. There is a small vertical offset from the actual bottom edge (e.g., 8–16px), and content behind it remains scrollable.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On desktop, the cart summary widget behaves as a sticky/fixed element: once triggered (e.g., when a plan is added or the user scrolls into the plans area), it floats relative to the viewport and remains visible as the user scrolls through the plan list and comparison table, until the very bottom of the page where it may shift up to avoid overlapping the footer. On mobile, it is fixed to the bottom edge of the viewport as a full‑width bar, remaining present while scrolling but dismissible if necessary.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The memberships contact link is placed near the bottom of the comparison table section, serving as the final in‑page action. It is center‑aligned within the same max‑width content column used by the table. There is a clear vertical gap of approximately 32–40px between the bottom edge of the comparison table and the contact link, allowing it to stand on its own without feeling cramped. The link may be presented as a short line of text such as “Need a custom plan? Contact us,” with the actionable “Contact us” styled in the brand’s purple accent color, underlined or with a subtle hover underline consistent with the navigation style. The text is kept concise and set in the same body style as other supporting copy, maintaining a tertiary level of prominence. Below the link, there is an additional 64–80px of blank vertical space before the page footer or any subsequent sections, providing a clean visual endpoint to the memberships content and echoing the generous spacing seen beneath the hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The contact link is static, sitting naturally at the end of the comparison section within the normal document flow. It does not have sticky or floating behavior; its prominence comes from its centered placement, brand accent color, and generous surrounding whitespace.

name: Membership Detail
title: Membership Plan Detail
description: Displays full details for a selected membership plan, validates key inclusion criteria, and provides a plan-specific inquiry form with inline confirmation, alongside a cart summary.

core_features: Read the membership plan ID from the URL parameter `id` and load the corresponding plan using `getMembershipPlanDetail`.

Display full membership plan details, including name, monthly price, description, included group sessions per month, benefits, and terms, using data from `MembershipPlan` and the `terms` field returned by `getMembershipPlanDetail`.
Evaluate whether the selected plan meets the criterion of including at least 4 group sessions per month and display a dynamic status indicator based on `included_group_sessions_per_month`.
Provide a plan-specific membership inquiry form pre-bound to the current membership plan ID, collecting name, email, message, preferred contact method, and preferred contact time.
Submit the membership inquiry using `submitMembershipInquiry`, handle validation and error states, and show an inline confirmation message on the same page without navigation.
Display a live cart summary using `getCartSummary`, including overall cart information and a list of cart items, so users can review current bookings while viewing the membership plan.

Offer navigation back to the full membership list (`memberships.html`) with the current plan highlighted via `highlightPlanId`, and navigation to the general contact page (`contact.html`) with membership-specific context parameters.
user_workflows: Viewing membership plan details: 1) User navigates to `membership_detail.html?id={membershipPlanId}` from `memberships.html` or `index.html`. 2) On page initialization, the page reads the `id` URL parameter as `currentPlanId`. 3) The page calls `getMembershipPlanDetail` with `membershipPlanId = currentPlanId`. 4) While loading, a loading state is displayed. 5) On success, membership plan details (including `plan`, `included_group_sessions_per_month`, and `terms`) are stored in page state. 6) All detail components (header, inclusions, terms, criteria indicator) render using this data.

Checking the minimum group session criterion: 1) After `getMembershipPlanDetail` returns, the page reads `included_group_sessions_per_month` from the API response. 2) The page computes a derived boolean `meetsMinimumSessions` where `meetsMinimumSessions = included_group_sessions_per_month >= 4`. 3) A criteria indicator component displays a positive or negative status based on this derived value. 4) If the value does not meet the criterion, the component displays an informational message; no additional API calls are made.

Submitting a plan-specific membership inquiry: 1) The user reviews the plan information and then fills in the inquiry form fields (name, email, message, preferred contact method, preferred contact time). 2) On form submission, client-side validation checks that required fields (name, email) are not empty and that email has a valid format; if invalid, field-level errors are shown and the API is not called. 3) If validation passes, the page calls `submitMembershipInquiry` with `membershipPlanId = currentPlanId` and the form values. 4) While waiting for the response, the form shows a submission-in-progress state and disables resubmission. 5) On success, the returned `inquiry` object and confirmation `message` are stored in page state, the success message is shown inline, and the form may be optionally reset based on design rules. 6) On error, an error state is shown near the form, and the form is re-enabled for correction and retry.

Reviewing current cart summary from the membership detail page: 1) On page initialization, the page calls `getCartSummary` with no parameters. 2) The returned `cart` and associated `items` (array of `CartItem`) are stored in cart state. 3) A cart summary component displays the key values (such as total amount and number of items) and a list of items using fields provided by the API. 4) Optionally, the user can trigger a manual refresh action in the cart summary component, which invokes `getCartSummary` again and updates the display without leaving the page.

Navigating to related pages with context: 1) When the user clicks the 'Back to all membership plans' control, the page navigates to `memberships.html?highlightPlanId={currentPlanId}`, allowing the memberships page to highlight the previously viewed plan. 2) When the user clicks the 'Need more help? Contact us' control, the page navigates to `contact.html` with URL parameters `subject` set to a membership-inquiry-related label, `contextType=membership`, and `contextId={currentPlanId}` so the contact page can prefill or adjust its form based on the membership context.
interactions: Page initialization: On page load, read the `id` parameter from the URL. If present, trigger `getMembershipPlanDetail(id)` and `getCartSummary()`. If `id` is missing or empty, show an error state indicating that no membership plan was specified.

Plan detail loading: While `getMembershipPlanDetail` is in progress, disable any actions that assume plan data (e.g., inquiry form submission or context-based navigation) and show a loading indicator. On success, enable dependent components and emit an internal `onPlanLoaded` event to re-render bound components.

Plan detail error handling: If `getMembershipPlanDetail` fails (e.g., network or plan not found from backend), show an error message in the plan detail area and keep the inquiry form disabled or hidden until valid data is available.

Criteria indicator interaction: Whenever new plan data is loaded or reloaded, recompute the `meetsMinimumSessions` flag. The criteria indicator updates automatically without additional user interaction.

Inquiry form input: Users can type into the name, email, and message fields and select values from dropdowns/radio controls for preferred contact method and contact time. Each change updates the form state in memory but does not call any APIs.

Inquiry form validation and submission: On clicking the form's submit button, validate required fields and email format. If validation fails, display inline errors and prevent submission. If validation passes, call `submitMembershipInquiry` with `membershipPlanId` from URL state and the current form values, switch to a submitting state, and prevent duplicate submissions until a response is received.

Inquiry submission response handling: On successful `submitMembershipInquiry` response, display the returned confirmation `message` and possibly details derived from the `inquiry` record. The form may optionally be cleared or collapsed based on design. On error, display a submission error message and allow the user to modify fields and resubmit.

Cart summary refresh: On page load and optionally when a user clicks a 'Refresh cart' control in the cart summary component, call `getCartSummary`. While loading, show a cart-specific loading indicator. On success, update the cart total and items list; on error, show an error message within the cart summary component.

Back to memberships navigation: When the user clicks the back navigation control, construct a URL of the form `memberships.html?highlightPlanId={currentPlanId}` and navigate to it using standard browser navigation, without invoking any additional API on this page.

Contact navigation with context: When the user clicks the general contact link, construct a URL of the form `contact.html?subject={encodedSubject}&contextType=membership&contextId={currentPlanId}` and navigate to it, so the contact page can use the context information.

URL change handling (if supported in routing): If the `id` URL parameter changes while the user is on the page (for example via client-side routing), detect the change, update `currentPlanId`, reset detail and inquiry state, and call `getMembershipPlanDetail` again for the new ID.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary piece of page state is `currentPlanId`, derived from the URL parameter `id`. On initial load, the page parses the current URL and sets `currentPlanId = valueOf(id)`. If `currentPlanId` is undefined or empty, the page shows a 'plan not specified' state and does not call `getMembershipPlanDetail` or enable the inquiry form. When `currentPlanId` is valid, the page calls `getMembershipPlanDetail({ membershipPlanId: currentPlanId })`. The response populates a `planDetailState` object containing the `plan` (a `MembershipPlan` instance), `included_group_sessions_per_month`, and `terms` string. A derived boolean `meetsMinimumSessions` is calculated using `included_group_sessions_per_month >= 4`. This derived state is used by the criteria indicator component. The inquiry form state (`inquiryFormState`) tracks the current form field values, validation errors, and submission status (`idle`, `submitting`, `success`, `error`). On each successful `submitMembershipInquiry` call, `inquiryFormState` is updated with the returned `inquiry` object and confirmation `message`, and the status becomes `success`. Cart-related state (`cartState`) is populated by `getCartSummary`, which returns a `cart` and an array of `items` (`CartItem`). This is used solely for display and can be refreshed by re-invoking `getCartSummary`. Outgoing navigation uses URL parameters only: clicking 'Back to all membership plans' navigates to `memberships.html` with a `highlightPlanId` parameter set to the current `currentPlanId`, and clicking 'Need more help? Contact us' navigates to `contact.html` with `subject`, `contextType=membership`, and `contextId=currentPlanId`. No localStorage or other client-side persistent storage is used; all persistent context is carried via URL parameters and server responses.

components.functionality: Initializes and manages loading of membership plan details for the current page based on the URL parameter `id`. It calls `getMembershipPlanDetail` using `membershipPlanId = currentPlanId`, stores the returned `plan`, `included_group_sessions_per_month`, and `terms` in page state, and exposes loading and error flags for other components.

Displays the primary identity of the membership plan, including name and key pricing information, using the `plan` data loaded by the loader component. It updates automatically when a new plan is loaded.

Shows the membership plan's descriptive information and inclusions, including description, `included_group_sessions_per_month`, and the list of benefits from the `plan`. It reacts to any updates in the `plan` data from the loader component.

Calculates and displays whether the current membership plan meets the minimum requirement of including at least 4 group sessions per month. It uses the `included_group_sessions_per_month` from the plan detail response to compute a derived `meetsMinimumSessions` boolean and presents a status message accordingly.
Displays the `terms` text returned by `getMembershipPlanDetail` for the current membership plan. It updates whenever the plan detail loader retrieves new terms for a different `currentPlanId`.

Collects plan-specific membership inquiries tied to the current membership plan. The form fields include name, email, optional message, preferred contact method, and preferred contact time. On submit, it validates inputs and calls `submitMembershipInquiry` with `membershipPlanId = currentPlanId` and the current form values. It manages its own validation and submission state and passes the API response to the confirmation component.

Shows inline feedback after an inquiry submission. On successful `submitMembershipInquiry` response, it displays the confirmation `message` and can optionally reference fields from the returned `inquiry` object (such as status) for additional context. On errors, it shows a relevant error message. It listens to events emitted by the inquiry form component.

Retrieves and displays a high-level summary of the current cart by calling `getCartSummary`. It presents cart-level details (from `Cart`) and a list of items (from `CartItem`) such as item names and prices. It can be refreshed on demand by the user without leaving the membership detail page.

Provides navigation actions related to the membership plan. It includes a control that navigates back to `memberships.html` with the URL parameter `highlightPlanId` set to `currentPlanId` and a control that navigates to `contact.html` with `subject`, `contextType=membership`, and `contextId=currentPlanId`. It only enables these navigation options once `currentPlanId` is known.

Monitors the URL for changes to the `id` parameter while the user is on the page. When `id` changes, it updates `currentPlanId`, triggers the membership-plan-loader to reload data for the new plan, and resets dependent state such as the inquiry form and criteria indicator.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content column for the hero text, over a full-width background image. Navigation items appear distributed horizontally across the top within the same grid.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows a linear top-to-bottom flow derived from the hero-centric mockup: a full-bleed, image-driven hero at the top dedicated to the selected membership (with header, criteria, and navigation links), followed by stacked, full-width sections for inclusions, terms, and then an inquiry + cart section. Within these sections, content is organized into a single, center-aligned column for text-heavy panels, and a 2/3–1/3 split for the functional form + cart area, all on an implied 12-column grid.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows a linear top-to-bottom flow derived from the hero-centric mockup: a full-bleed, image-driven hero at the top dedicated to the selected membership (with header, criteria, and navigation links), followed by stacked, full-width sections for inclusions, terms, and then an inquiry + cart section. Within these sections, content is organized into a single, center-aligned column for text-heavy panels, and a 2/3–1/3 split for the functional form + cart area, all on an implied 12-column grid.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero occupies roughly a full viewport height (about 90–100% of the visible height) with a dark overlay, ensuring the membership header content sits comfortably in the central 30–40% of the vertical space. Below the hero, each major section (inclusions, terms, inquiry/cart) is full-width but constrained by a centered max-width content area. Vertical separation between sections uses generous margins of roughly 64–96px, while internal spacing within panels and between related elements follows a 16–32px rhythm based on an 8px grid.
layout_strategies.content_density: The density remains low and marketing-style, with each section focusing on a single primary task: understanding the plan, checking inclusion details, reviewing terms, and then inquiring while seeing the cart summary. Headings are large and well-separated from body text, and panels avoid clutter by grouping only closely-related information together. The form area is the densest zone, but fields are vertically spaced with 16–24px gaps to preserve the semi-minimal, corporate sports feel.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye is first drawn to the bright, central membership headline in the hero, then to the smaller label above it and the brief descriptive copy below. Immediately beneath, the criteria indicator acts as a quick status check. From there, the natural scroll moves down into the inclusions panel, then the terms, and finally into the inquiry section where the form on the left and cart on the right share focus. Navigation links near the top of the hero offer a secondary exploration path, while the cart’s sticky behavior keeps pricing context in peripheral view as users interact with the form.

layout.overall_description: The Membership Detail page mirrors the original hero-driven sports aesthetic: a full-width tennis background image stretches across the top of the viewport with a dark overlay, creating a dramatic stage for the membership’s name and key messaging, centered in a single vertical column. Above this central text, a slim row of understated navigation links sits within the same grid, while just below it a compact criteria indicator validates eligibility at a glance. Scrolling down transitions from the dark, image-rich hero into a clean, white content zone where inclusions and terms are presented in wide, breathable panels, each centered and constrained for comfortable reading. Further down, a slightly differentiated section houses the functional core: a generous inquiry form on the left and a compact cart summary card on the right, aligned within a 12-column grid. The cart card tracks the user via a sticky position, maintaining the modern, minimal feel with a single purple accent and ample whitespace. Overall, the layout feels spacious, focused, and image-led, guiding users from high-level understanding in the hero to detailed evaluation and finally to action in the inquiry section.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-plan-loader appears as a full-viewport overlay that sits above all page content, including the hero, whenever data is being fetched. It is fixed to the top-left corner of the viewport (0,0) and stretches to cover 100% of the width and height. The overlay uses a semi-opaque dark background that matches the hero’s darkened image treatment, subtly dimming the page while still hinting at the underlying layout. In the exact center of the viewport, a small loading indicator (spinner or simple progress element) and a short loading label are stacked vertically, with about 16–24px between them, mirroring the centered, low-density composition of the hero text block. No other content is interactable while this overlay is visible.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed to the viewport, covering all underlying components with a dark translucent layer and centered loader content until data is ready.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-plan-header occupies the central content zone of the hero section. The hero itself sits immediately below any global site navigation, spans the full browser width, and is approximately 90–100% of the viewport height with a full-bleed tennis background image and dark overlay. Within this hero, a centered content column (max-width around 720px) is horizontally centered and vertically positioned so that it occupies the middle 30–40% of the hero’s height. Inside this column, a small uppercase purple label (e.g., “MEMBERSHIP”) appears at the top, followed by a large white membership name headline and then a single paragraph of descriptive body copy beneath, each separated by about 24–32px of vertical spacing. All text is left-aligned within the column but visually centered on the page due to the constrained width and surrounding whitespace. The membership-criteria-indicator is placed just below this block, maintaining the same column alignment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the hero; scales responsively but remains centered in the hero’s middle band on all screen sizes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-plan-inclusions panel is the first section immediately below the hero, separated from the hero’s bottom edge by a generous vertical gap of roughly 64–80px to preserve the spacious, editorial feeling. It spans the full width of the page with a solid white background, but its content is constrained within a centered max-width container of about 1040px aligned to the same vertical grid as the hero content. Within this container, a section heading sits near the top-left, followed by inclusions content (lists, tags, or feature descriptions) arranged in either a single column or a balanced two-column layout on larger screens, while maintaining at least 24px vertical separation between items. The top and bottom padding within this panel are in the range of 48–64px, ensuring a low-density, marketing-style presentation that feels like a focused content block rather than a crowded table.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width section that stacks directly beneath the hero and above the terms panel, expanding vertically with content while maintaining a fixed max-width content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-criteria-indicator is positioned directly beneath the membership plan’s descriptive copy in the hero, within the same centered column as the header text. It appears as a compact horizontal bar or pill-shaped indicator occupying roughly 40–60% of the column width, centered or left-aligned within that column (not full-bleed across the hero). It sits about 24px below the body copy, creating a clear but close relationship to the header content. The indicator might include a small icon on the left and a short eligibility message on the right, with internal horizontal padding of around 16–24px and vertical padding of 8–12px, maintaining the minimal, corporate look. Its size ensures it is noticeable without competing with the main headline, acting as a quick validation element in the lower portion of the hero’s central text block.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the hero content column; it moves naturally with the hero as the user scrolls and does not stick or float.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-terms-panel is placed immediately below the inclusions section, separated by approximately 64px of vertical whitespace. Like the inclusions panel, it spans the full page width with a white or very subtly tinted background, but the content is constrained to the same centered max-width container (around 1040px) to maintain alignment. A section heading sits at the top-left of the container, with terms text presented in one or more blocks beneath, each separated by roughly 24px vertically. The panel’s internal padding on the top and bottom is similar to the inclusions section (about 48–64px), but its content feels slightly denser due to the legal or explanatory text. Any borders or dividers are subtle, using low-contrast lines if necessary so as not to introduce heavy visual chrome.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, stacked directly beneath the inclusions panel, expanding vertically as needed based on the volume of terms content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-inquiry-form resides in a dedicated “action” section beneath the terms panel, separated from it by roughly 64–96px of vertical space. This section spans the full width of the page and may use a very light gray or off-white background to subtly distinguish it as interactive. Inside, a centered max-width container (around 1120–1200px) is divided into an implied 12-column grid. The form occupies the left 8 columns (about two-thirds of the container width) and is left-aligned. At the top of the form column, a clear section heading and optional short subheading appear, separated by about 16–24px. Below, input fields are arranged vertically in a single column, each field spanning the full width of the form column, with 16–24px vertical spacing between fields. The primary submit button is located at the bottom-left of the form, with 24–32px of space above it from the last field. On smaller screens, this entire form column collapses to 100% width and stacks above the cart-summary-widget to maintain the linear flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the inquiry section, occupying approximately two-thirds of the content width on desktop and transitioning to full-width stacked layout on tablet and mobile.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-inquiry-confirmation appears inline within the same left column as the membership-inquiry-form, occupying exactly the same horizontal space. When active (e.g., after successful form submission), it is displayed directly above the form fields or replaces the form content area while preserving the location of the heading above. The confirmation takes the form of a full-width notification bar or card with a white or very light background, a subtle shadow or border, and a purple accent stripe or icon to connect it to the brand. It includes a concise message and any next steps, with 16–24px of internal padding on all sides. There is approximately 24px of vertical space between the heading and this confirmation block, mirroring the spacing between the heading and the form. When visible, it pushes the form fields downward or temporarily hides them, maintaining the overall vertical flow and spaciousness of the column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the form column; it appears and disappears inline, reflowing the form layout without overlaying other content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart-summary-widget sits in the same inquiry section as the form, aligned to the right side of the centered container. On a 12-column grid, it occupies the rightmost 4 columns (about one-third of the content width), starting from the same top baseline as the form’s heading. It is presented as a compact, vertically-oriented card with a white background, subtle shadow or soft border, and internal padding of 24–32px. The card’s width is visually narrower than the form column, emphasizing its role as a summary rather than a main content area. Key details (plan name, price, duration, and key inclusions count) are stacked with 12–16px spacing, maintaining a light, uncluttered composition. On long forms, the widget uses sticky positioning so that, as the user scrolls down the form, the card remains visible near the top-right of the viewport, with a top offset of around 80–96px to clear any global navigation. On smaller screens, the widget drops below the form, spanning 100% width with the same internal spacing and card styling.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sticky within the inquiry section on desktop, maintaining a fixed position near the top-right of the viewport while the user scrolls the form; stacks below the form and becomes static on smaller breakpoints.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The membership-navigation-links are positioned within the hero section, just below the global site navigation and aligned to the same centered grid as the membership-plan-header. They form a slim horizontal row at the top of the hero’s content area, occupying the full width of the centered column (around 720px) but visually light. Links such as “Back to plans” or adjacent membership links are displayed in uppercase text, spaced evenly with 16–24px horizontal gaps. The row sits approximately 32–48px above the small purple label of the membership-plan-header, creating a clear separation but still visually tying these controls to the hero content. On wider screens, the links are left-aligned within the column, while on smaller screens they may center-align to maintain balance. The styling is minimal—no heavy buttons, only understated text and possibly a subtle underline on hover—to avoid competing with the main header text.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the top zone of the hero content; scrolls away naturally as the user moves down the page and does not remain fixed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The url-state-watcher has no visible presence on the page. It operates behind the scenes, monitoring routing and URL parameters to ensure the correct membership data is displayed and that UI state (such as active hero slide or selected membership) remains in sync with the browser location. It does not occupy any space, does not affect the layout or spacing of other components, and cannot be interacted with directly by the user.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual and non-spatial; it does not render anything in the layout and has no effect on the flow of visible components.

name: Court Booking
title: Court Booking
description: Page for selecting a date, filtering courts, viewing availability by time, and creating a court reservation that is added to the booking cart.

core_features: Initialize court booking filters (date, surface, location type, time range) using URL parameters and getCourtFilterOptions.
Display an interactive date selector and filter controls that reload court availability using getCourtAvailability.
Render an availability grid of courts and time slots, allowing users to select a continuous available time block on a single court.
Populate a reservation form with the selected court and time range and collect play format, player names, and contact details.
Submit a court reservation using reserveCourt, then display a brief in-page confirmation using the returned message and reservation data.
Update the booking summary preview using getCartSummary after each successful reservation.
Provide navigation to the full booking summary (cart.html) via a 'View Booking Summary' interaction after reserving a court.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Landing on the Court Booking page from another page
1) User arrives on court_booking.html, possibly with URL parameters (date, surface, locationType, timeFrom, timeTo).
2) Page initialization reads URL parameters and sets initial filter state.
3) Page calls getCourtFilterOptions to populate dropdowns for surfaces, location types, and duration presets.
4) Page calls getCourtAvailability with the current filter state (date, surface, locationType, timeFrom, timeTo) to retrieve courts and availability_slots.
5) The availability grid displays courts and their time slots based on the returned data.

Workflow: Adjusting filters and browsing availability
1) User changes date, surface, location type, or time range in the filter bar.
2) On filter change (e.g., clicking an 'Apply Filters' button), the page updates the URL query parameters to reflect the new filter values.
3) The page calls getCourtAvailability with the updated parameters.
4) The availability grid re-renders with the new courts and availability_slots.
5) User scrolls or navigates through the grid to identify suitable times.

Workflow: Selecting a continuous time block for a court
1) User clicks an available slot cell in the availability grid to set a provisional start time for a selected court.
2) The grid highlights that slot and permissible contiguous available slots for the same court.
3) User clicks another slot on the same court at a later time to set the end time (inclusive range selection).
4) The component validates that all slots between start and end are available and belong to the same court; if invalid, an error state is displayed and the selection is reset.
5) When valid, the selected start and end times, along with the court_id, are propagated to the reservation form component.

Workflow: Completing the reservation form and submitting
