1) Reservation form auto-fills court name and selected start/end times from the grid selection; user can adjust time within the bounds of available slots if allowed by the UI logic.
2) User selects a play format (using the CourtReservation.play_format enum), enters player_names, and provides contact_name and contact_email.
3) User submits the form.
4) The form validates required fields and that start/end times and court_id are present.
5) On successful validation, the page calls reserveCourt with courtId, startDatetime, endDatetime, playFormat, playerNames, contactName, and contactEmail.
6) On success, the page receives reservation, cart_item, cart, and message. It displays a confirmation message and updates the in-page cart summary using the returned cart data.

Workflow: Viewing the booking summary (cart)
1) After a successful reservation, the page shows a 'View Booking Summary' action (e.g., button or link).
2) When the user activates this action, the browser navigates to cart.html according to the outgoing navigation rule.
3) cart.html is responsible for displaying the full cart; any cart state has already been updated by reserveCourt.

Workflow: Reviewing current cart status without leaving the page
1) On initial load (after the first successful getCourtAvailability call or after a reservation), the page calls getCartSummary.
2) The inline cart summary component displays cart.total_amount and a minimal list or count of items based on items from getCartSummary.
3) After each successful reserveCourt call, the inline cart summary refreshes using the cart data returned by reserveCourt (without an extra API call, or with an optional follow-up getCartSummary if needed by implementation).
interactions: Change date in date selector: updates internal date state, updates date URL parameter, triggers getCourtAvailability with the new date and current filters.

Change surface filter (e.g., select a surface from dropdown options populated by getCourtFilterOptions.surfaces): updates internal surface state, updates surface URL parameter, triggers getCourtAvailability.
Change location type filter (using getCourtFilterOptions.location_types): updates internal locationType state, updates locationType URL parameter, triggers getCourtAvailability.
Change time range (timeFrom/timeTo) using time inputs or dropdowns: updates timeFrom/timeTo state, updates corresponding URL parameters, triggers getCourtAvailability.

Click on an available time slot in the grid: stores the selected court_id and start time; if it is the first selection, marks it as start; if a start already exists, attempts to treat it as an end time and validate continuity.

Click on a second time slot on the same court after a start is selected: validates continuous availability between start and end using the availability_slots data; if valid, sets startDatetime and endDatetime into the reservation form; if invalid, shows an error and resets selection.
Click on a selected slot (or a 'Clear selection' control): clears the currently selected time range and resets the reservation form's start/end time fields.
Submit reservation form: triggers validation for required fields (court selection, start/end time, play_format, player_names, contact_name, contact_email). On passing validation, calls reserveCourt.

reserveCourt success handler: reads reservation, cart_item, cart, and message; shows a non-intrusive confirmation (toast/panel) using message and key reservation details; updates the inline cart summary with cart data; resets or partially resets the reservation form as appropriate.

reserveCourt error handler (e.g., server-side availability conflict): displays an error message, optionally resets grid selection, and re-calls getCourtAvailability for the affected date and filters to refresh the grid.
Click 'Refresh Cart Summary' (if provided): calls getCartSummary and updates the inline summary component.
Click 'View Booking Summary': navigates to cart.html without modifying cart state, using standard link navigation.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page's primary state (date, surface, locationType, timeFrom, timeTo) is synchronized with URL query parameters. On initial load, the page reads any existing parameters from the URL. If parameters are present, they are used as the initial filter values passed into getCourtAvailability. If a parameter is missing (for example, date), the page derives a default (such as current date for date or implementation-defined defaults for other filters) and may update the URL accordingly so that the state remains shareable. Whenever the user changes a filter (date, surface, locationType, timeFrom, timeTo), the page updates the browser URL's query string to reflect the new value (e.g., court_booking.html?date=YYYY-MM-DD&surface=...&locationType=...&timeFrom=...&timeTo=...), then calls getCourtAvailability with those parameters. This ensures that bookmarking or sharing the URL preserves the user's filter configuration. The selection of a court and time range, player details, and contact information are maintained in in-memory component state only and are not written to URL parameters. Cart and reservation state are not stored in the URL; they are derived exclusively from API responses (reserveCourt, getCartSummary). Any user navigation away from the page and back with the same URL parameters will reconstruct only the filter and availability context, not unfinished form input, ensuring that the URL encodes only shareable filter state.

components.functionality: Initializes and manages the date and court filter controls (date, surface, location type, time range), synchronizes them with URL parameters, and triggers availability updates.
event_handlers: onInit: Read URL parameters (date, surface, locationType, timeFrom, timeTo). Call getCourtFilterOptions to obtain surfaces, location_types, and default_durations_minutes. Populate dropdowns and defaults, then emit an initial 'filtersChanged' event to trigger getCourtAvailability.
onDateChange: Update internal date value, update 'date' URL parameter, and emit 'filtersChanged' with current filter values.
onSurfaceChange: Update internal surface value, update 'surface' URL parameter, and emit 'filtersChanged'.
onLocationTypeChange: Update internal locationType value, update 'locationType' URL parameter, and emit 'filtersChanged'.
onTimeRangeChange: Update internal timeFrom/timeTo values, update corresponding URL parameters, and emit 'filtersChanged'.

onDurationPresetSelect: When a duration preset from default_durations_minutes is chosen, optionally adjust timeTo relative to timeFrom or pre-populate selection logic, then emit 'filtersChanged' if this changes the effective availability query.

components.functionality: Displays courts and their time slots for the selected date and filters, allows selection of a continuous time range on a single court, and passes the chosen court_id, startDatetime, and endDatetime to the reservation form.
event_handlers: onFiltersChanged: When notified by court-booking-filters-bar, call getCourtAvailability with date, surface, locationType, timeFrom, and timeTo. Store returned courts and availability_slots and render them.

onSlotClick: If there is no existing selection, set the clicked slot as selection start (court_id and start_datetime). If there is an existing start with the same court_id, treat the click as a potential end selection and attempt to create a continuous range.

onRangeValidation: For a potential start/end pair on the same court, verify that all CourtAvailabilitySlot records between start_datetime and end_datetime for that court are is_available = true and contiguous based on the discretization defined by the returned slots. If the range is valid, set the selection (startDatetime, endDatetime, court_id) and emit 'timeRangeSelected'. If invalid, emit a validation error event and reset selection.
onClearSelection: Clear internal selection state and emit 'timeRangeCleared' so the reservation form can reset its time fields.
onAvailabilityReloadError: If getCourtAvailability fails, emit an error event describing the failure so the page can show an appropriate message.

components.functionality: Collects reservation details for the selected court and time range, including play format, player names, and contact information, and submits them via reserveCourt.
event_handlers: onTimeRangeSelected: Receive court_id, startDatetime, and endDatetime from court-availability-grid and populate corresponding form fields. Optionally display the selected court name by matching court_id against the courts currently held by court-availability-grid.
onTimeRangeCleared: Clear startDatetime and endDatetime fields and any displayed court label in the form.
onPlayFormatChange: Update internal play_format value using options corresponding to CourtReservation.play_format enum values.
onPlayerNamesChange: Update internal player_names array based on user input fields.
onContactDetailsChange: Update internal contact_name and contact_email values.

onSubmit: Validate that court_id, startDatetime, endDatetime, play_format, player_names (at least one name), contact_name, and contact_email are present and properly formatted. If validation passes, call reserveCourt with courtId, startDatetime, endDatetime, playFormat, playerNames, contactName, and contactEmail.

onReserveCourtSuccess: When reserveCourt returns reservation, cart_item, cart, and message, emit 'reservationCompleted' with this data; optionally reset or partially reset the form (e.g., clear player_names but keep contact_name/contact_email for convenience).

onReserveCourtError: If reserveCourt fails (e.g., due to a time conflict or validation issue from the backend), display an error message and emit an event so that court-availability-grid can optionally refresh availability via getCourtAvailability.

components.functionality: Displays a compact view of the current cart status (e.g., total amount and count of items) and keeps it synchronized with server-side cart state.
event_handlers: onInit: Optionally call getCartSummary when the page first loads to display the current cart state if it exists.

onReservationCompleted: When notified of a successful reservation by court-reservation-form (which provides updated cart data from reserveCourt), update displayed cart totals and item count using the cart and cart_item data without requiring an additional API call.
onManualRefresh: If a 'Refresh' control is provided, call getCartSummary to re-fetch the cart and items, then update the summary display.
onError: Handle and display errors from getCartSummary if the cart cannot be fetched.

components.functionality: Shows transient confirmation or error messages related to court reservations and availability updates.
event_handlers: onReservationCompleted: Receive reservation, cart_item, cart, and message from court-reservation-form and display a concise confirmation using message and key reservation fields (such as start_datetime and end_datetime), then auto-hide after a short duration or on user dismissal.
onReserveCourtError: Receive error details from court-reservation-form and display an error notification with guidance (e.g., asking the user to re-select times due to a conflict).
onAvailabilityError: Receive errors from court-availability-grid (e.g., getCourtAvailability failures) and display them to the user.

components.functionality: Provides a direct navigation control to open the full booking summary on cart.html after or during the court booking process.
event_handlers: onClick: Navigate the browser to cart.html as defined in the outgoing navigation rules, without modifying any cart data on this page.

onReservationCompleted: Optionally change the link state (e.g., from disabled to enabled) once at least one reservation has been added to the cart, based on whether a cart or cart_item has been returned from reserveCourt or getCartSummary.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content column for the hero text, over a full-width background image. Navigation items appear distributed horizontally across the top within the same grid.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the mockup’s linear, top‑to‑bottom flow with a strong centered column. Below the global navigation, a shallow hero‑like header band introduces the Court Booking context and houses the court-booking-filters-bar as the primary interactive element near the top center. Directly beneath, the court-availability-grid occupies the full width of the central content column. Below the grid, the court-reservation-form and inline-cart-summary sit in a shared horizontal row within the same centered column (form on the left, cart on the right), maintaining the central alignment pattern. The reservation-confirmation-toast floats above this flow when active, centered near the top of the viewport. The booking-summary-link is embedded within the inline-cart-summary, aligned to the right to read as a subtle navigational affordance rather than a competing primary element.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the mockup’s linear, top‑to‑bottom flow with a strong centered column. Below the global navigation, a shallow hero‑like header band introduces the Court Booking context and houses the court-booking-filters-bar as the primary interactive element near the top center. Directly beneath, the court-availability-grid occupies the full width of the central content column. Below the grid, the court-reservation-form and inline-cart-summary sit in a shared horizontal row within the same centered column (form on the left, cart on the right), maintaining the central alignment pattern. The reservation-confirmation-toast floats above this flow when active, centered near the top of the viewport. The booking-summary-link is embedded within the inline-cart-summary, aligned to the right to read as a subtle navigational affordance rather than a competing primary element.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The layout preserves the hero’s spacious feel. The top filtering/header band occupies roughly the upper 25–30% of the viewport height on desktop, with generous padding above and below the court-booking-filters-bar. The central content column spans about 70–80% of the page width, centered, never touching the edges. The court-availability-grid takes most of the remaining vertical space, while the reservation-form and inline-cart-summary together fill approximately the lower 30–40% of the viewport, still with 32–64px vertical breathing room between each major block. Horizontal gutters remain consistent with a 12‑column grid, with the reservation form taking about two-thirds of the column width and the cart summary one-third.
layout_strategies.content_density: All components are presented with low to medium content density, echoing the marketing hero’s spaciousness. Form controls in the filter bar and reservation form are organized into a few clear rows, with 16–24px spacing between fields and 24–32px separation between component groups. The availability grid is structured but airy, with clear banding between time rows and court columns, avoiding tight, cramped cells. The inline cart summary uses concise text and clear hierarchy with generous padding so the panel feels light and readable rather than data-heavy.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye is first drawn to the center-top area where the page title (outside this spec) and the court-booking-filters-bar sit, framed by the darker hero band and purple accents. From there, the eye naturally moves down into the bright, high-contrast court-availability-grid, which reads as the main workspace. After scanning availability, attention drops further to the left-aligned court-reservation-form (primary action area), and then slightly to the right toward the inline-cart-summary. The booking-summary-link, placed within the cart, offers a subtle outward navigation path. When visible, the reservation-confirmation-toast briefly becomes the highest-contrast focal point, appearing centered near the top of the viewport, then yielding back to the main vertical flow once dismissed.

layout.overall_description: The Court Booking page adopts the hero-driven, centered layout of the mockup and translates it into a focused booking workspace. Below the slim global navigation, a shallow hero-style band with a dark, full-width background (possibly a softened tennis image) spans the entire viewport width, with a dark overlay and the brand’s purple accent used sparingly on labels and active controls. In the middle of this band, a centered content column houses the page title (e.g., “Court Booking”) and, directly beneath it, the court-booking-filters-bar, both vertically spaced so the top of the page feels calm and intentional. As users scroll slightly or look down, the background transitions to a lighter, neutral surface where the court-availability-grid fills the central column, wide and clear, showing times and courts across the page. Beneath this grid, the content column splits into two: on the left, the court-reservation-form appears as the main action panel, and on the right, the inline-cart-summary sits as a slimmer, card-like panel, both aligned to share a common top edge and maintaining the same side margins as the grid above. Throughout, spacing follows an 8px-based scale, with large (32–64px) vertical separations between major sections to keep the page feeling spacious and modern. When a reservation is created, a reservation-confirmation-toast fades in over the content, centered horizontally near the top of the viewport, reinforcing the hero-style focal positioning before fading out. The booking-summary-link sits quietly at the top-right area of the cart summary, providing a clear yet non-dominant path to a full booking summary. The result is a clean, image-informed, center-aligned booking experience that feels consistent with the original marketing hero while being optimized for focused interaction.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The court-booking-filters-bar is positioned near the top center of the page, inside a hero-like header band directly beneath the global navigation. It sits within the central content column, which spans approximately 70–80% of the page width and is horizontally centered. Vertically, there is a clear separation: about 32–48px of space above the filters (below the page title) and 32–48px below, before the background transitions into the main content section. The filters bar itself is a horizontal form, roughly 60–70% of the page width on desktop, centered in the column, with its control groups (date picker, surface type, time range, etc.) arranged in a single or two compact rows. Each control has 16–24px horizontal spacing from the next, forming a clean line across the middle of the header band. The bar is visually unified, potentially on a slightly lighter panel or with a subtle border to separate it from the underlying hero image, but it remains low-profile and spacious, matching the mockup’s understated navigation and hero text treatment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The filters bar scrolls with the page but remains relatively high in the initial viewport; on larger screens it may become sticky just beneath the main site navigation so that it stays pinned at the top edge of the content area as the user scrolls through the availability grid, maintaining constant access without feeling heavy.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The court-availability-grid sits directly below the hero/header band that contains the filter bar, aligned within the same central content column. It starts roughly 64px beneath the bottom of the filters bar to echo the spacious, editorial separation between major sections in the mockup. Horizontally, the grid spans nearly the full width of the content column (around 70–80% of the browser width, capped at a comfortable max-width such as 1200px), with equal left and right margins creating strong alignment with the elements above and below. The grid is laid out as a matrix of time slots (rows) and courts (columns), with column labels on top and a leftmost time column, all styled with plenty of vertical padding in each cell (around 12–16px) to avoid a cramped feel. The total height of the grid can easily occupy 40–60% of the viewport on desktop, depending on the number of time slots, but it is visually broken into clear bands with subtle horizontal and vertical lines, reinforcing order without feeling heavy. There is 48–64px of space below the grid before the reservation form and cart summary area begins, maintaining the page’s low-density, stacked rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The grid scrolls naturally with the page. On smaller screens it may become horizontally scrollable within the central column to preserve cell readability while keeping the overall page layout centered.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The court-reservation-form is positioned directly beneath the court-availability-grid within the same central content column, forming the left side of a two-column block. After a vertical gap of about 48–64px below the grid, this block starts, with the reservation form aligned to the left edge of the content column. The form’s width is roughly two-thirds of the column (about 60–65% of the central content width), leaving space on its right side for the inline-cart-summary. It appears as a clean, vertically stacked panel with a subtle card-like treatment (light background, soft border or shadow) to distinguish it from the page background while staying minimalist. Internally, its fields (selected court, date, time, player info, etc.) are arranged in a single column with 16–24px vertical spacing between them, and logical groupings (e.g., schedule vs. player details) separated by 24–32px. The main action button (e.g., “Add to Booking”) is anchored at the bottom of the form, left-aligned with the inputs but prominent, possibly full-width relative to the form, and styled with the brand’s purple accent. There is at least 32px of padding inside the form’s borders on all sides, maintaining the airy feel of the hero’s typography spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The reservation form scrolls with the page. On taller pages, the form content itself may scroll within the viewport, but the form does not stick; instead, it stays aligned with the grid and cart, preserving a cohesive vertical flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The inline-cart-summary is placed to the right of the court-reservation-form, forming the right column of the shared block beneath the availability grid. It aligns to the right edge of the central content column and shares the same top alignment as the reservation form, creating a balanced two-column layout within the otherwise single centered column. The cart summary’s width is about one-third of the central content width (roughly 30–35%), making it noticeably narrower than the form but tall enough to list a few booked items. It is presented as a compact, card-like panel with a light or slightly darker neutral background, soft border, or subtle shadow, mirroring the reservation form’s styling for visual cohesion. Internal padding is generous (24–32px on all sides), with the title or label (e.g., “Your Booking”) at the top left and a clear vertical list of items beneath, each separated by 12–16px. At the top-right corner of this panel, the booking-summary-link is placed, aligned horizontally with the cart title but separated to the far right, making it easy to spot without pulling focus from the item list. There is a modest vertical gap (around 16–24px) between the bottom of the cart contents and any total/CTA area, keeping the layout clean.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On larger screens, the inline cart panel may behave as a sticky element within its column, remaining visible as the user scrolls slightly through the reservation form content. On smaller screens, it collapses beneath the reservation form into a stacked layout, maintaining full-width alignment with the central column while preserving its card-like treatment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The reservation-confirmation-toast appears as an overlay panel centered horizontally near the top of the viewport, echoing the mockup’s centered hero text. When triggered, it sits approximately 80–120px below the global navigation bar, floating above all other page content. The toast spans about 30–40% of the viewport width on desktop (never exceeding the central content column’s width), with its left and right edges roughly aligned with the inner bounds of the main content. Its height is compact—enough for one or two lines of confirmation text and perhaps a small icon—surrounded by 16–24px of internal padding. The background is a dark, high-contrast surface (dark charcoal or near-black) with white text and a purple accent for any inline icon or action label, strongly reminiscent of the hero overlay. The corners are slightly rounded, and a soft shadow reinforces that it sits above the page. There is clear negative space above and below the toast so it does not feel cramped against the nav or content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The toast is fixed relative to the viewport while visible, unaffected by scrolling. It fades or slides in and out (e.g., from slight upward motion), then automatically dismisses after a short duration, restoring the user’s focus to the underlying booking content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking-summary-link is positioned inside the inline-cart-summary panel, aligned to the top-right corner of that card. It sits on the same horizontal line as the cart’s title text, but on the opposite side, creating a clear yet understated navigational affordance to a more detailed booking summary. The link is rendered as uppercase or small-caps text consistent with the site’s navigation style, using the brand’s purple accent color and possibly a subtle right-pointing arrow icon. There is 8–16px of horizontal padding between the link text and the inner edge of the cart panel, and about 16–24px of vertical padding between this top row (title + link) and the first booking item. The link’s size is visually smaller than primary CTAs and does not introduce new visual blocks; instead, it feels like a lightweight, inline text control anchored to the cart’s upper-right corner, maintaining the minimal, non-chrome-heavy design language.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The link scrolls with the inline cart panel. On smaller screens where the cart stacks below the form, the link remains in the cart’s top-right corner and stays accessible without occupying extra screen real estate or converting into a button.

name: Coaches & Private Lessons
title: Coaches & Private Lessons
description: Page to find and compare tennis coaches for private lessons using specialty and rating filters, then open a coach’s profile to view details and book.

core_features: Load available filter options (specialties and rating buckets) via getCoachFilterOptions and render them as interactive filters.
Read incoming URL parameters (specialty, minRating) and initialize the filter state to match them.
Search and filter coaches using searchCoaches, passing the current specialty and minRating filter values and restricting results to active coaches.
Display a dynamic list of coach results including photo, rating, specialties, and key credentials based on the Coach data model returned by searchCoaches.
Allow users to refine results to backhand (or other) specialists with a minimum rating, using the filter controls wired to searchCoaches.
Allow users to open an individual coach profile (coach_profile.html?id=...) by clicking a coach card or a dedicated 'View Profile' action.
Fetch and display a live cart summary (total items and total amount) in the header using getCartSummary, with navigation to cart.html when selected.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Initial page load with optional pre-selected filters
1. User navigates to coaches.html, optionally with URL parameters such as specialty and minRating (e.g., from index.html or coach_profile.html).
2. The page controller reads specialty and minRating from the URL query string.
3. The page calls getCoachFilterOptions with no parameters to retrieve available specialties and rating buckets.
4. The filter bar initializes its selected specialty and rating values to match the URL parameters (if present) or to an unfiltered state (if not).
5. The page calls searchCoaches with specialty, minRating, and onlyActive=true.
6. The coach list displays the returned coaches using coach cards, reflecting each coach’s dynamic data from the Coach model.
7. In parallel, the header calls getCartSummary to display the current cart’s item count and total amount.

Workflow: Find a backhand-specialist coach with a minimum rating
1. User lands on coaches.html (with or without pre-set filters).
2. In the filter bar, the user selects 'backhand' (or another specialty value) from the specialty options populated via getCoachFilterOptions.
3. The user selects a minimum rating from the rating bucket options (also from getCoachFilterOptions), which sets the minRating filter.
4. When the user applies the filters, the page updates the URL query parameters (specialty and minRating) to the newly selected values.
5. The page calls searchCoaches with the updated specialty and minRating and onlyActive=true.
6. The coach list refreshes to show only coaches matching the selected specialty and minimum rating.
7. The user scans the list and selects a preferred coach based on rating, specialties, and credentials.

Workflow: Open a coach profile to view schedule and book
1. On the filtered coaches list, the user identifies a suitable coach.
2. The user clicks anywhere on the coach card or on a dedicated 'View Profile' link.
3. The page triggers navigation to coach_profile.html with the selected coach’s id as the URL parameter (coach_profile.html?id=selectedCoachId).
4. The user continues the booking flow on the coach profile page (outside the scope of this page).

Workflow: View cart while browsing coaches
1. At any time while viewing coaches.html, the header cart summary component has already called getCartSummary and displays the current total items and price.
2. The user clicks on the cart summary area or a 'View Cart' link in the header.
3. The page navigates to cart.html with no additional parameters.
4. The user reviews or completes their bookings from the cart page (outside this page’s scope).

Workflow: Adjust filters and use browser navigation
1. User changes filters (specialty and/or minRating) and applies them; the page updates the URL and calls searchCoaches.
2. Later, the user hits the browser Back button.
3. The page re-reads specialty and minRating from the URL, restores the filter UI to those values, and re-calls searchCoaches with the restored parameters.
4. The coach list updates to match the previous filter state, allowing consistent navigation history.
interactions:
Interaction: Change specialty filter
- Component: coach-filter-bar
- User selects a specialty from a dynamically populated list (from getCoachFilterOptions.specialties).
- Behavior: The component updates its internal filter state for specialty and optionally waits for the user to click 'Apply Filters' (or triggers an immediate search, depending on implementation).
- On apply, the page updates the URL parameter specialty to the selected value (or removes it if cleared) and calls searchCoaches with the new specialty, the current minRating, and onlyActive=true.

Interaction: Change minimum rating filter
- Component: coach-filter-bar
- User selects a rating bucket from a list built from getCoachFilterOptions.rating_buckets (using their dynamic label and min values).
- Behavior: The component updates its internal minRating state to the selected bucket’s min value.
- On apply, the page updates the URL parameter minRating to match the selected minimum rating (or removes it if cleared) and calls searchCoaches with the updated minRating, the current specialty, and onlyActive=true.

Interaction: Reset filters
- Component: coach-filter-bar
- User clicks a 'Reset' or equivalent control.
- Behavior: The filter component clears its specialty and minRating selections.
- The page removes specialty and minRating from the URL query string.
- The page re-calls searchCoaches with null/undefined (no filter) for specialty and minRating and onlyActive=true, showing all active coaches.
- The coach list re-renders with the unfiltered results.

Interaction: Click a coach card or 'View Profile'
- Component: coach-card (within coach-list)
- User clicks the card area or a specific 'View Profile' link/button.
- Behavior: The component emits the selected coach’s id to the page controller.
- The page navigates to coach_profile.html with ?id=<selectedCoachId> using standard URL navigation.
- No additional data is sent beyond the id parameter.

Interaction: View Cart from header
- Component: cart-summary-header
- User clicks on the cart summary area or an associated 'View Cart' link/button.
- Behavior: The page executes a navigation to cart.html.
- The existing cart state (Cart and CartItem records) is not modified by this page; it is only retrieved via getCartSummary.

Interaction: Refresh page or direct link with filters
- User directly visits a URL such as coaches.html?specialty=<value>&minRating=<value>.
- Behavior: On load, the page controller reads these parameters, configures the filter UI, and calls searchCoaches with matching parameters and onlyActive=true.
- The user sees a pre-filtered list consistent with the URL state.

Interaction: Handling empty or error states
- If searchCoaches returns an empty array, the coach-list component displays an appropriate 'no results' message and suggests adjusting filters (without hardcoding any specific specialty or rating values).
- If getCoachFilterOptions or searchCoaches fails, the page can display a generic error message and allow the user to retry the operation, without substituting any static fallback data.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page’s authoritative state is maintained through URL query parameters and live API responses, not through localStorage or other persistent client storage. On initialization, the page controller parses the current URL for specialty (string) and minRating (number) query parameters. These values directly initialize the filter state in the coach-filter-bar component and are immediately passed into searchCoaches as specialty and minRating, along with onlyActive=true.
Whenever the user changes filters and applies them, the page updates the URL query parameters using pushState or a full reload (e.g., setting specialty=<selectedSpecialty> and minRating=<selectedMinRating>). After updating the URL, the page calls searchCoaches with the new parameter values, ensuring that the coach list always reflects the URL. Clearing a filter removes the corresponding query parameter from the URL and passes a null/undefined value into searchCoaches.
Back/forward browser navigation is handled by re-reading the URL on navigation events and re-initializing the filter component and the searchCoaches call with the URL’s current specialty and minRating. The coach list always derives its content from the latest searchCoaches result, not from cached or hardcoded data.
The list of available specialties and rating buckets is always loaded from getCoachFilterOptions during page initialization (and can be re-requested on demand), so no specialty or rating options are hardcoded. The cart summary in the header is kept up to date by calling getCartSummary on initial load (and optionally on demand) and is not stored in URL parameters, since it is global to the user’s session and not specific to this page’s filters.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial data loading, URL parameter parsing, filter state synchronization, and API calls (getCoachFilterOptions, searchCoaches, getCartSummary). It passes data and callbacks to child components and handles URL updates on filter changes.

Displays and manages the specialty and minimum rating filters. Specialties and rating buckets are populated from getCoachFilterOptions. The component reads initial values from the page controller (which uses URL parameters) and notifies the controller when filters are applied or reset.
Receives an array of Coach entities from the page controller (result of searchCoaches) and renders a list/grid of coach-card components. Handles empty-state rendering when the array is empty.

Represents a single coach in the list. Displays dynamic properties such as name, photo_url, rating, num_reviews, specialties, and key credentials (experience_years, certifications, teaching_style) from the Coach model. Provides a clickable area and/or a 'View Profile' control to navigate to that coach’s profile page.

Displays an at-a-glance summary of the current cart at the top of the page. It uses getCartSummary to retrieve the current Cart and associated CartItems, showing the total number of items and the cart.total_amount. Provides a control to navigate to cart.html.

Shows an informational message when searchCoaches returns an empty list. It may also provide a control to reset filters by notifying the page controller to clear URL parameters and re-run searchCoaches without filters.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content column for the hero text, over a full-width background image. Navigation items appear distributed horizontally across the top within the same grid.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom arrangement: global navigation at the very top (shared across the site), then a full-width hero-style header dedicated to the coaches page controller, followed immediately by a horizontal filter bar, then the coaches results grid with coach cards. Cart summary elements sit within the results section, aligned to the right but still inside the central content column. A no-results state replaces the results grid area when applicable. There are no sidebars; everything lives in a single, centered content column over full-bleed background treatments.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom arrangement: global navigation at the very top (shared across the site), then a full-width hero-style header dedicated to the coaches page controller, followed immediately by a horizontal filter bar, then the coaches results grid with coach cards. Cart summary elements sit within the results section, aligned to the right but still inside the central content column. A no-results state replaces the results grid area when applicable. There are no sidebars; everything lives in a single, centered content column over full-bleed background treatments.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero-style page controller band occupies approximately 70–100% of the initial viewport height on desktop, with its text column centered vertically and horizontally within the safe max-width grid. The filter bar sits directly beneath the hero, spanning nearly the full content width (about 80–90% of the page width, capped at a max content width), but with enough breathing room above and below to preserve the editorial feel (32–64px). The coach results grid occupies the bulk of the remaining page height, extending down the scroll, with cards arranged in a 3-column grid on desktop and generous gaps between rows and columns. Side margins remain consistent with the implied 12-column grid, maintaining roughly 5–10% horizontal padding on each side of the content. Major vertical separations between hero, filters, and results follow the 64–96px spacing guideline.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout keeps content density low to medium: a hero-like page header with a simple label, title, and short explanatory line; a single compact row of filters; and coach cards that prioritize a hero photo, name, rating, and a limited set of key details rather than dense text blocks. White (or dark, lightly textured) space is used generously around each section and between cards. Interactive elements (filters, buttons, tags) are clear and comfortably spaced, with hit areas padded by 16–24px. The cart summary remains visually light and unobtrusive, conveying key information in a compact, single-line or small-card format.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual flow mirrors the mockup’s strong vertical, centered hierarchy: the user’s eye first lands on the large white headline of the page controller in the hero area, then moves up slightly to the purple label, then down to the short descriptive line. From there, the eye is guided downward to the filter bar, which uses the purple accent selectively to highlight active filters. The user then scans into the results grid, moving left to right across the top row of coach cards. Within each card, the flow runs from the coach photo to the coach name, rating, specialty tags, and then to the booking call-to-action at the bottom. The cart summary is positioned at the upper-right of the results section, entering peripheral vision once the user begins interacting with coaches. The no-results state, when active, recenters the eye in the middle of the results area with a concise message and a primary action to reset or adjust filters.

layout.overall_description: The page presents as a modern, image-driven listing experience that clearly inherits the hero treatment from the main site. A full-width tennis-court background with a subtle dark overlay stretches from the bottom of the global navigation to roughly the bottom of the initial viewport. In the vertical center of this hero band sits a single, centered content column: a small purple label reading the page context, a bold white headline like “Find Your Private Tennis Coach,” and a brief description underneath. All three are stacked with generous vertical spacing, tightly aligned to the same left edge within the center column. As the user scrolls, this hero glides up and reveals a horizontally oriented filter bar that spans the central width of the page, set on a contrasting neutral surface to distinguish it from the photographic hero above. Below the filter bar, a wide but still centered content area holds a grid of coach cards arranged in three columns, each card featuring a strong coach image on top and structured typography beneath. A light cart summary appears in the top-right area of this results section, visually connected but secondary to the grid. The background remains largely monochrome with occasional purple accents for active filters, ratings highlights, and primary actions. In cases where filters return no coaches, the grid area is replaced by a large, calm no-results message, centered both horizontally and vertically relative to the results section, echoing the hero’s centered, low-density feel. Overall, the layout feels spacious, focused, and professional, directing the user smoothly from discovery (hero and filters) into comparison and selection (cards and cart summary).

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coaches-page-controller occupies the central content column within a full-width hero band directly beneath the global navigation. This hero band uses a full-bleed tennis imagery background with a dark overlay, extending from just under the nav to roughly 70–100% of the initial viewport height on desktop. Within this band, the controller’s content is vertically centered, with the main text block sitting in the middle 30–40% of the hero’s height. Horizontally, it is constrained to the central 8 out of 12 columns of the implied grid, resulting in an effective width of about 60–70% of the viewport, capped at a max width (e.g., ~960–1120px) and centered on the page. The text itself is left-aligned inside this centered column: at the top of the block, a small all-caps purple label (e.g., “COACHES & PRIVATE LESSONS”) sits with breathing room above; 24–32px below this, a large white headline spans up to two lines; 24–32px below the headline, a single short line or two of body text in a smaller, lighter-weight white font provides guidance. If the page controller includes view toggles (e.g., “List / Map”) or sorting controls, these sit directly below the body text, aligned to the right edge of the text column, with about 24px vertical spacing, appearing as a compact horizontal group. Overall, there is at least 64–80px of clear space between this text block and both the top and bottom edges of the hero band, preserving the airy, editorial feel. All elements are stacked vertically in a clean column, focusing the user’s attention on the headline as the primary entry point.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The controller is static within the hero section; as the user scrolls, the entire hero (including this content) moves off-screen, handing focus to the filter bar and results. It does not stick or float, preserving the classic hero-then-content scroll behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coach-filter-bar sits immediately below the hero band, creating a clear transition from high-level page context to actionable filtering. It occupies a full-width horizontal band that is visually distinct from the hero by using a solid or slightly tinted neutral background (light or dark, depending on overall theme), while still extending edge-to-edge across the viewport. Within this band, the actual filter controls are confined to the same central 12-column grid as the hero text, typically spanning about 80–90% of the viewport width and capped at the same max width as the main content. The filter elements (e.g., search input, specialty dropdown, rating filter, price range, availability toggles) are organized in a single horizontal row on desktop, left-aligned within the content column, with each filter module occupying roughly 2–3 columns of the grid and separated by 16–24px gaps. The bar has a total height of approximately 72–96px, with 16–24px padding on the top and bottom to give the controls breathing room. The top edge of the filter bar sits 32–48px below the bottom of the hero content (or flush with the hero’s bottom edge if a more seamless transition is desired), and 32–48px of clear space is maintained between the filters and the first row of coach cards below. Active filters, filter labels, and the primary “Reset filters” or “Clear all” action use the brand’s purple accent for text and/or small underlines, while inactive controls remain monochrome. On smaller screens, the filter bar collapses into one or two stacked rows but still occupies the full content width with consistent internal padding.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The filter bar becomes sticky when the user scrolls past its original position: it pins to the top of the viewport directly beneath the global navigation bar, maintaining its full width and internal layout. This keeps filtering always accessible while the user browses the coach list. On mobile, the bar may collapse into a toggleable panel but retains its sticky behavior at the top.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coach-list fills the main content area beneath the filter bar, occupying the same central max-width grid and extending down the scroll. It begins approximately 32–48px below the bottom of the filter bar, maintaining ample separation. Horizontally, the list is constrained to roughly 80–90% of the viewport width, centered within the 12-column grid. On desktop, coaches are arranged in a 3-column grid: each coach card spans 4 columns with 24–32px horizontal gaps between cards, resulting in each card occupying roughly 28–32% of the content width. Vertically, rows of cards are separated by 32–40px. The list runs downwards as many rows as needed, creating a spacious, scrollable gallery. The background behind the cards is a clean, neutral surface with minimal visual noise; cards themselves have subtle separation via soft shadows, thin borders, or elevated surfaces, echoing the minimalist corporate sports aesthetic. At the very top-right of this coach-list area (still inside the main content width), space is reserved for the cart-summary-header, while the first row of cards begins aligned with the left edge of the grid. On tablet devices, the grid collapses to 2 columns (each card roughly 48–50% of width), and on mobile to a single column (cards at 100% of the content width with 16–24px side padding). The vertical spacing between the last visible row and any footer or pagination control maintains at least 64px to avoid crowding.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The coach-list scrolls naturally as the main page content. It respects the sticky behavior of the filter bar above and any sticky behavior of the cart summary to the right, allowing coach cards to move underneath or beside those fixed elements as the user scrolls down.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each coach-card is a vertically oriented card within the coach-list grid, designed to feel like a mini hero for each coach while staying clean and uncluttered. On desktop, a card’s width is approximately one-third of the main content column (4 out of 12 columns), with a fixed or minimum height that grows slightly with content. The card surface is either white or a very light neutral, lifted subtly off the background via a soft shadow or thin border. At the top of the card, a full-bleed coach photo spans the entire card width, with a 3:2 or 4:3 aspect ratio, occupying roughly the top 40–50% of the card’s height. Directly below the image, a text block is padded on all sides by 16–24px. The first line is the coach’s name, set in a bold, high-contrast type, with enough size to stand out but smaller than the hero headline. 8–12px below the name, a horizontal row shows the rating (e.g., star icons and numeric rating) and possibly the number of reviews; stars or rating highlights use the purple accent or a subtle contrasting color. 8–12px below this, a line or two of specialty tags (e.g., “Beginner,” “Serve & Volley,” “Junior Players”) appear as pill-shaped badges or small uppercase labels with fine borders, arranged left to right and wrapping to a second line if necessary. A short one- or two-line bio or focus statement sits 12–16px below the tags, using lighter-weight text. At the bottom of the card, separated by 16–24px from the bio, a horizontal row presents price and primary action: the left side lists the price per hour and perhaps location, while the right side contains a prominent “View Profile” or “Book Now” button. The button uses the purple brand color for the fill with white text and has 12–16px vertical padding. Overall vertical spacing inside the card follows the base spacing scale, ensuring each text group is clearly separated but still part of a cohesive block. On hover (desktop), the card slightly elevates with a subtle shadow increase and/or a thin purple border appears, reinforcing interactivity without visual clutter.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Coach cards are responsive elements within the grid: on tablet they resize to fit a 2-column layout, and on mobile they expand to full width with increased vertical spacing (24–32px) between cards. They do not fix or float; they scroll with the page content. The card’s click behavior can be either entire-card clickable or button-only, but the layout visually supports the entire card feeling interactive.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart-summary-header appears within the same main content area as the coach-list but is visually anchored to the upper-right corner of that area, just below the filter bar. It is rendered as a compact horizontal card or pill, approximately 25–30% of the content width on desktop, aligned to the right edge of the 12-column grid. Vertically, it sits at the same baseline as, or 16–24px above, the top edge of the first row of coach cards, creating a clear relationship without overpowering the grid. The cart summary card has 16–20px of internal padding and a clear, minimalist design: on the left, a concise label like “Selected Lessons” or an icon; next to it, a bold number of items; and on the far right, the current total price and a subtle link-style or small solid button labeled “View Cart” or “Checkout”. The typography is smaller than the hero headline and coach names but still clearly legible. The card uses a neutral background with a thin border or soft shadow; the primary action and any key numeric values may use the purple accent to draw attention without shouting. Ample whitespace around the card (especially below it and to its left) ensures it does not visually collide with the coach cards; the first column of coach cards starts aligned with the left edge of the main grid, leaving a clear separation.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The cart summary behaves as a sticky element within the results area: once the user scrolls past its initial position, it pins to the top-right of the viewport, just below the sticky filter bar, allowing coach cards to scroll behind and beneath it. On narrower screens (tablet and mobile), it can reposition to sit directly beneath the filter bar, spanning the full content width, and may convert to a single-line banner that remains sticky at the top for easy access.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The no-results-state occupies the same spatial footprint as the coach-list when active, effectively replacing the grid of coach cards beneath the filter bar. It respects the same central max-width constraints and sits 32–48px below the filter bar, ensuring continuity in the vertical rhythm. Instead of multiple cards, the area is dominated by a single, centered content block that echoes the hero’s low-density, central focus: at the vertical center of the current viewport (within the bounds of the results section), a soft, monochrome icon or illustration appears at the top of the block; 16–24px below it, a clear, slightly larger headline such as “No coaches match your filters” is centered and set in high-contrast text; 12–16px below that, a two-line explanatory sentence in a smaller, lighter font offers guidance (e.g., “Try adjusting your specialties, rating, or availability filters.”); and 24–32px below the explanation, a primary action button is centered, using the purple accent, labeled “Clear filters” or “Reset search”. The block width is limited to the central 6–8 columns (about 50–70% of the content width), leaving generous blank space to the left and right. The background remains the same neutral surface as the results area, with no additional panels or heavy boxes, so the message feels calm and uncluttered.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The no-results state is static and scrolls with the page, occupying the vertical space that the coach-list would otherwise use. It appears only when filters yield zero coaches; when active, coach cards are completely hidden to avoid confusion. The sticky filter bar and cart summary behavior remain unchanged above; as users adjust filters, the no-results state disappears as soon as results become available.

name: Coach Profile
title: Coach Profile
description: Displays a single coach’s profile with detailed information and a live schedule of private lesson availability, and allows users to book a lesson and add it to their unified cart.

core_features: Load and display detailed coach information (name, rating, specialties, bio, experience, teaching style, certifications) using getCoachProfile based on the required URL parameter id.

Load and display a list or calendar-style view of available private lesson slots for the selected coach using getCoachAvailableSlots, filtered by optional URL parameters startDate, endDate, weekdaysOnly, timeFrom, and timeTo.

Provide interactive filter controls (date range, weekdays-only toggle, time-from/time-to) that update the URL parameters and re-fetch available slots via getCoachAvailableSlots without using localStorage.
Allow the user to select any single available PrivateLessonSlot as the target for their private lesson booking.
Provide an action to automatically select the earliest available suitable 60-minute lesson slot (respecting the current filters) from the getCoachAvailableSlots results.
Provide a booking form where the user chooses a lesson duration (up to the selected slot’s length), enters name, email, and lesson notes (e.g., desired focus), and submits the booking.

Call bookPrivateLesson with the selected lesson slot, duration, and contact details, and handle the response booking, cart_item, cart, and message objects to confirm that the lesson was added to the cart.
Show a contextual confirmation/feedback area summarizing the booked lesson details using the PrivateLessonBooking and CartItem returned by bookPrivateLesson.
Provide a "View Booking Summary" call-to-action that navigates to cart.html (no extra parameters) after a successful booking, where the booking summary is shown.
Optionally display a mini cart summary on the page using getCartSummary so the user can confirm current cart contents and totals while browsing the coach’s schedule.
Provide a "Back to all coaches" navigation which returns the user to coaches.html and passes a specialty URL parameter derived from the coach’s primary specialty (if available).
user_workflows: View coach profile and basic info: (1) User navigates to coach_profile.html with id in the URL. (2) On page load, the page reads id (and any filter parameters) from the URL. (3) The page calls getCoachProfile with coachId = id. (4) The coach’s core details (name, rating, specialties, experience) are displayed in a header component. (5) Additional details (bio, teaching_style, certifications) are displayed in a details component. (6) User can click "Back to all coaches" to go to coaches.html with specialty derived from the coach’s primary specialty.

Filter and browse available private lesson slots: (1) After loading the coach, the page reads startDate, endDate, weekdaysOnly, timeFrom, and timeTo from the URL (if present). (2) The page calls getCoachAvailableSlots with coachId = id and those filter values (or undefined if not present). (3) The availability grid/list component renders all returned PrivateLessonSlot entries. (4) User adjusts filters via controls (date fields, weekdays-only toggle, time range selectors). (5) On each filter change, the page updates the corresponding URL parameters (using history/navigation) and re-calls getCoachAvailableSlots with the updated parameters. (6) The availability view updates to show slots matching the new criteria. (7) If there are no slots, an empty-state message is shown and booking actions are disabled.

Select a manual slot and book a private lesson: (1) User clicks on a specific available slot in the availability grid. (2) The selected slot is stored in page state (e.g., selectedSlotId and selectedSlot object) and visually marked as selected. (3) The booking form component is enabled and pre-fills the start and end time based on the selected slot. (4) The duration input in the form is constrained to be less than or equal to the difference between the slot’s end_datetime and start_datetime (e.g., max 60 minutes if the slot is 60 minutes long). (5) User enters required fields: durationMinutes, contactName, contactEmail; notes is optional. (6) User submits the form, triggering bookPrivateLesson with lessonSlotId = selectedSlot.id and the form data. (7) On success, the page receives booking, cart_item, cart, and message, updates the confirmation component, and optionally refreshes the mini cart using getCartSummary. (8) User can click "View Booking Summary" to navigate to cart.html.

Automatically select earliest available 60-minute slot and book: (1) User clicks the "Select earliest suitable 60-minute slot" action. (2) If the current slot list is stale or not yet loaded, the page ensures getCoachAvailableSlots has completed for the current filters. (3) The page computes the duration of each returned PrivateLessonSlot (end_datetime minus start_datetime). (4) It filters the list to slots whose duration is at least 60 minutes and whose status is available. (5) It sorts those slots by start_datetime ascending and selects the earliest one. (6) The selected slot is stored in state and highlighted in the availability component; the booking form is pre-populated with durationMinutes = 60. (7) User reviews details, fills in name, email, and optional notes, and submits the booking via bookPrivateLesson. (8) On success, the confirmation component is updated, and the user can optionally navigate to cart.html.

Review cart summary from the coach profile page: (1) At page load and/or after any successful bookPrivateLesson call, the page calls getCartSummary with no parameters. (2) The cart summary widget displays cart.total_amount and a list of CartItem entries (e.g., including private_lesson_booking items). (3) If there is no active cart or items array is empty, the widget shows a message indicating that the cart is currently empty. (4) User can click "View Booking Summary" in the cart widget to navigate to cart.html.
interactions: On page load: Read URL parameters id (required), startDate, endDate, weekdaysOnly, timeFrom, and timeTo. If id is missing or invalid (e.g., getCoachProfile returns an error or an inactive coach), show an error state and do not attempt to load slots.

Back to all coaches link: When clicked, compute a primaryCoachSpecialty value from the loaded Coach.specialties (for example, the first element if available). Navigate to coaches.html?specialty=primaryCoachSpecialty. If there are no specialties, navigate to coaches.html without the specialty parameter.

Initial data fetch: After successfully getting the coach profile via getCoachProfile, call getCoachAvailableSlots with coachId = id and filters derived from the current URL. Show a loading state in the availability component until the call resolves.

Filter controls – date range: When the user edits the start date or end date filter, update the startDate and endDate URL parameters (adding, changing, or removing them as appropriate), then re-call getCoachAvailableSlots with those new values. Disable dates or show validation messages if endDate is earlier than startDate (client-side validation only, no static default values).

Filter controls – weekdays-only toggle: When the user toggles the weekdays-only option, set weekdaysOnly URL parameter to true or false accordingly, then re-call getCoachAvailableSlots with weekdaysOnly reflecting the URL state.

Filter controls – time range: When the user sets timeFrom or timeTo, update the corresponding URL parameters with the chosen time strings and re-call getCoachAvailableSlots. Perform client-side validation to ensure timeFrom is earlier than timeTo before re-fetching.

Slot selection: When the user clicks on an available slot in the grid/list, set that slot as selected in state (selectedSlotId and selectedSlot). Enable the booking form and pre-fill its read-only start and end time fields using selectedSlot.start_datetime and selectedSlot.end_datetime. If a previously selected slot exists, unselect it visually.

Earliest 60-minute slot selection: When the user clicks the dedicated action, validate that the slots list is present and non-empty. Calculate each slot’s duration from its start_datetime and end_datetime. Filter to slots with at least 60 minutes of availability and status = "available". If none exist, show a user-visible message and keep the current selection unchanged. If one exists, select the earliest one and set the booking form’s durationMinutes to 60.

Booking form – duration selection: When the user adjusts the duration field, ensure that the chosen value does not exceed the current slot’s duration or fall below any minimum duration rule enforced by the business logic (if exposed in validation rules rather than static values). If validation fails, show inline error feedback and prevent form submission.

Booking form – validation and submission: On submit, validate that a slot is selected and required fields (contactName, contactEmail, durationMinutes) are filled and valid (e.g., email format check). If valid, call bookPrivateLesson with lessonSlotId = selectedSlot.id, durationMinutes, contactName, contactEmail, and notes. Disable the submit button and show a loading state while awaiting the response. On success, clear or lock the form and update confirmation and cart summary sections. On error, show an error message and re-enable the form.

Post-booking confirmation: After a successful bookPrivateLesson call, display the returned message and key fields from booking (e.g., start_datetime, end_datetime, duration_minutes) and cart_item (e.g., name, price, subtotal) in a confirmation component. Optionally allow the user to start another booking by clearing the selected slot and form fields.
View Booking Summary navigation: When the user clicks any "View Booking Summary" button (from confirmation or mini cart), navigate to cart.html (no additional URL parameters required).
Cart summary refresh: After any successful bookPrivateLesson call, trigger getCartSummary and update the cart summary widget with the latest cart and items data.

Error handling for availability loading: If getCoachAvailableSlots fails, show an error message in the availability area and offer a retrigger action (e.g., a "Retry loading slots" button) that calls getCoachAvailableSlots again using the current filters from the URL.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page’s primary state is driven by URL parameters and API responses, not localStorage. The mandatory URL parameter id identifies which Coach to display; its value is passed directly as coachId to getCoachProfile and getCoachAvailableSlots. Optional parameters startDate and endDate (YYYY-MM-DD) constrain getCoachAvailableSlots to a date range when present; if omitted, the interface is called without these filters, allowing the backend to apply its own default range. The weekdaysOnly parameter, when present as a boolean-like value (e.g., 'true' or 'false'), is parsed and passed to getCoachAvailableSlots; the UI toggle is always synchronized with this parsed value. timeFrom and timeTo parameters represent earliest and latest start times as strings (e.g., '16:00'); these are passed as-is to getCoachAvailableSlots and mirrored in the time-range inputs. Whenever the user changes filters via UI, the page updates the URL query string to reflect the new filter state and immediately re-calls getCoachAvailableSlots with values taken from the URL, ensuring the URL is the single source of truth for filters. Transient UI state such as selectedSlotId, selectedSlot, form inputs (durationMinutes, contactName, contactEmail, notes), loading flags, and error messages exists only in memory in the page and is reset on full navigation or reload; it is not persisted across sessions. After bookPrivateLesson succeeds, the page may optionally call getCartSummary without parameters to refresh a mini cart, but the cart identifier and contents are managed entirely by the backend and not stored in URL or localStorage. Navigation to coaches.html passes a computed primaryCoachSpecialty value via the specialty URL parameter, permitting the coaches listing page to pre-filter its results based on the coach viewed.

components.functionality: Displays core coach information at the top of the page, including name, photo, rating, number of reviews, specialties, experience, and active/inactive state, along with a 'Back to all coaches' link.
Shows extended coach information, such as bio text, teaching style description, and certifications list, retrieved from getCoachProfile.

Provides controls to adjust the availability filters: date range (startDate, endDate), weekdays-only toggle, and time range (timeFrom, timeTo). Updates the URL parameters and triggers re-fetching of available slots using getCoachAvailableSlots whenever the user changes filter values.

Displays the list or calendar-style grid of available PrivateLessonSlot entries returned by getCoachAvailableSlots for the current coach and filters. Supports selecting a single slot and highlights the currently selected slot.

Provides an action to automatically select the earliest available PrivateLessonSlot that can accommodate a 60-minute lesson, based on the currently loaded slot list and filters. When a suitable slot is found, it sets that slot as selected and pre-fills the booking form duration to 60 minutes.

Collects the data necessary to book a private lesson for the selected slot: durationMinutes (bounded by the slot duration), contactName, contactEmail, and optional notes. On submission, validates inputs and calls bookPrivateLesson. Displays submission loading state and passes the API response to the confirmation and cart summary components.

Shows the outcome of the most recent booking attempt. On successful bookPrivateLesson response, it displays the backend-provided message along with details from PrivateLessonBooking (e.g., start_datetime, end_datetime, duration_minutes) and CartItem (e.g., name, price, subtotal). Also provides a 'View Booking Summary' button to navigate to cart.html and may offer a 'Book another lesson' option to reset selection and form state.

Displays a concise summary of the user’s current cart using getCartSummary, including total_amount and a list of CartItem entries. Refreshes automatically after successful lesson bookings so that the user can see the newly added private lesson in their cart.

Coordinates URL parameter parsing, initial data loading, and re-fetching of data when filters change. On load, reads id, startDate, endDate, weekdaysOnly, timeFrom, and timeTo from the URL; calls getCoachProfile and then getCoachAvailableSlots; and exposes the resulting data and loading/error states to child components. It also centralizes error handling and ensures that only valid filter values are forwarded to the interfaces.
Displays global error or warning messages, such as failures from getCoachProfile, getCoachAvailableSlots, bookPrivateLesson, or getCartSummary. Provides actions to retry the relevant interface call.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content column for the hero text, over a full-width background image. Navigation items appear distributed horizontally across the top within the same grid.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom, hero-first pattern. At the very top sits the global navigation (inherited from the site). Immediately beneath it, the coach-profile-header becomes the full-viewport hero, with a full-bleed coach/tennis background image and a dark overlay. Centered within this hero is a single main content column containing the coach’s name and key info. Below the hero, sections are stacked vertically: first the coach-details-panel as a concise bio/details band, then an availability & booking section where the availability-filter-bar sits above the availability-calendar, followed by the lesson-booking-form. The earliest-slot-selector is integrated into the filter bar row. The cart-summary-widget floats unobtrusively at the bottom-right of the viewport. System-level UI (error-notification-banner and booking-confirmation-panel) overlays the main flow when needed.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom, hero-first pattern. At the very top sits the global navigation (inherited from the site). Immediately beneath it, the coach-profile-header becomes the full-viewport hero, with a full-bleed coach/tennis background image and a dark overlay. Centered within this hero is a single main content column containing the coach’s name and key info. Below the hero, sections are stacked vertically: first the coach-details-panel as a concise bio/details band, then an availability & booking section where the availability-filter-bar sits above the availability-calendar, followed by the lesson-booking-form. The earliest-slot-selector is integrated into the filter bar row. The cart-summary-widget floats unobtrusively at the bottom-right of the viewport. System-level UI (error-notification-banner and booking-confirmation-panel) overlays the main flow when needed.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero consumes essentially 100% of the initial viewport height, with the coach text block occupying the central 30–40% vertically and a centered column width of roughly 60–70% of the page on large screens. Subsequent sections use full-width background bands with a central content column aligned to the same grid as the hero text, max-width about 960–1120px. Vertical separation between major sections (hero → details → availability/booking) is generous (64–96px). Key interactive components (filters, calendar, booking form) have 24–32px internal padding and 24–32px vertical gaps between them, maintaining the spacious, editorial feel.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout keeps a low, marketing-style content density. Each section focuses on one main task: understanding the coach, reviewing details, selecting availability, then confirming a booking. Text blocks are short, typography is large, and elements are generously spaced using the 8px scale. The calendar is the most information-dense component but is visually calmed by clear grid lines, large slots, and sufficient padding around it. No decorative chrome or extra panels clutter the view; secondary information is kept compact and peripheral (e.g., cart-summary-widget).
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow mirrors the mockup: attention lands first on the large, white coach name in the center of the hero, then moves slightly up to a small purple label (e.g., 'HEAD COACH') and down to the brief description. From there, users naturally scroll down into the coach-details-panel, then into the availability section where their eye moves from the filter bar (with the purple earliest-slot-selector CTA on the right) down into the calendar grid. After a slot is chosen, the eye continues down to the lesson-booking-form. The cart-summary-widget, anchored at the bottom-right, is noticeable but not dominant, supporting the primary flow rather than competing with it. When present, the error-notification-banner at the very top and the booking-confirmation-panel centered overlay temporarily redirect attention without breaking the underlying vertical structure.

layout.overall_description: The Coach Profile page opens with a full-height hero section that visually mirrors the original tennis-themed mockup: a full-bleed photograph of the coach on a court, covered by a dark, semi-transparent overlay. At the visual center of the screen, a single, vertically stacked column presents a small purple label, the coach’s name in large white type, and a one-line positioning statement. The rest of the screen around this column is intentionally quiet, with the image and subtle court lines guiding the eye toward the text. Scrolling down, the hero gently transitions into clean, full-width bands of content laid out on a monochrome background. A centered details panel gives a compact, readable snapshot of the coach. Below that, an availability and booking section spans the same central width, with a slim filter bar and call-to-action on top, a spacious, grid-based availability-calendar in the middle, and a simple, vertically stacked booking form at the bottom. A small cart summary card hovers at the bottom-right corner of the viewport, styled in dark tones with purple accents, always accessible but visually light. Any system feedback appears as overlays: errors in a slim banner at the very top under the navigation, and booking confirmation in a centered, modal-like panel over a darkened backdrop, both aligned to the same central grid, preserving the clean, modern, image-led aesthetic throughout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coach-profile-header occupies the entire first, full-viewport hero section. It uses a full-width, edge-to-edge background image (the coach on a tennis court) with a dark overlay to ensure high contrast for the text. A central content column, aligned to an implied 12-column grid and constrained to about 60–70% of the page width (max-width ~960px), is vertically centered within the hero, roughly spanning the middle 30–40% of the screen height. Within this column, at the top sits a small uppercase purple label (e.g., 'HEAD COACH'), followed by a large white coach name, then a short description or tagline, each separated by 24–32px of vertical space. Any key coach metadata (rating, years of experience, primary location) appears in a single row or tight vertical stack directly beneath the description with slightly smaller type and 16–24px spacing, still within the same column. The navigation bar above is slim and visually light; the hero content clearly dominates. At the very bottom center of the hero, just above the fold, a small row of slider dots can optionally indicate multiple coaches or slides, mirroring the mockup, occupying only a narrow horizontal strip and not extending beyond ~20–30% of the hero’s width.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The hero header is static within the normal document flow, anchored beneath a fixed or standard top navigation bar. It fills 100% of the initial viewport height on load (min-height: 100vh) and scales responsively with the browser size, maintaining the central alignment of its content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coach-details-panel sits directly below the hero, separated by a generous 64–80px vertical gap to maintain the editorial feel. It appears as a full-width section with a solid, dark or mid-tone monochrome background (slightly lighter than the hero overlay for contrast) and a centered content card. This card aligns to the same central grid as the hero column and spans approximately 70% of the viewport width on desktop (max-width ~960px), with 24–32px padding on all sides. Inside, the coach’s bio, specialties, certifications, and key stats are arranged in a low-density layout: a short section heading on top, a brief paragraph or two, and a simple list or 2–3 column stat row below, each block separated by 16–24px. The overall height of this panel is moderate (roughly 30–40% of a viewport height), leaving comfortable space above and below before the next section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The details panel scrolls with the page and remains part of the main vertical content stack. It does not fix or float; it simply occupies its section in the linear flow beneath the hero.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The availability-filter-bar is the topmost element of the availability & booking section, positioned about 64px below the bottom of the coach-details-panel. It sits within a full-width background band but is constrained to a central content width matching the panels above (about 70% of page width, max-width ~960px). The filter bar itself is a slim, horizontal strip, roughly 56–72px tall, with 16–24px of vertical padding. Filters (date range selector, day-of-week toggles, time-of-day chips, etc.) are aligned from left to center in a single row, using the 12-column grid to space them evenly. There is ample horizontal spacing (16–24px) between filter elements to prevent visual clutter, and the bar has clear top and bottom whitespace (24–32px) to separate it from the calendar below. The earliest-slot-selector sits at the far right of this same row, so the entire bar reads as one cohesive control band.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The filter bar is static within the availability section.
