Its top edge aligns horizontally with the cart-summary-bar, creating a clean, shared baseline across the row. Each cart item inside the list is rendered as a horizontal row card stretching the full width of this left column, with generous padding (16–24px top and bottom, 16–24px left and right) and separated from the next item by either a 16–24px gap or a subtle divider line to maintain clarity without clutter. The items list grows vertically as more items are added, pushing the schedule and budget panels further down but always maintaining at least 64px of vertical separation between the bottom of the last item and the start of the next section. On mobile, this list expands to 100% width of the screen, and each item card maintains its horizontal padding but focuses on a more vertical stacking of content to fit the narrower viewport.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, vertically expanding list within the main content area. It scrolls naturally with the page while the cart-summary-bar may remain sticky alongside it on larger screens. It respects the grid’s left margin and max-width, never touching the viewport edges, preserving the centered, editorial feel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each cart-item-row is a structured card within the cart-items-list, occupying the full width of the left content column. It uses an internal 12-column sub-grid to arrange its content horizontally in a way that feels balanced yet spacious. Typically, the left 2–3 sub-columns (about 15–20% of the row width) are reserved for a small course or session thumbnail image, lightly rounded and cropped to align with the sports photography style. The central 6–7 sub-columns (roughly 50–60% of the row width) display the item’s core details—program name, location, dates, and player information—arranged in a vertical stack with consistent 4–8px spacing and a clear hierarchy (program name in bolder, larger type; supporting details in smaller, muted text). The rightmost 2–3 sub-columns (about 20–25% width) are dedicated to the cart controls and price: the price is aligned top-right in bold, high-contrast type, with quantity or player count controls directly beneath it and a remove action aligned along the bottom right. The entire row maintains 16–24px of padding and a minimum height of around 80–96px to avoid feeling cramped, even for items with less text. The visual design is clean—no heavy borders, instead a subtle background tint or bottom divider and hover highlight, consistent with the semi-minimalist aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the list, but responds to available width by collapsing its internal sub-grid on smaller screens: the image moves to the top, details follow beneath it, and controls / price stack at the bottom, all still within a card that respects the global horizontal padding. This maintains legibility and tap targets on mobile while preserving the central content alignment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The schedule-overview-panel is positioned in the second content row beneath the cart-items-list and cart-summary-bar. After a vertical gap of roughly 64–80px from the bottom of the last cart item, a new full-width row begins, and this panel occupies the left half of that row, spanning 6 of the 12 columns within the max-width container. Its left edge aligns flush with the left edge of the cart-items-list above, while its right edge meets the centerline of the grid, mirrored by the budget-overview-panel on the right. The panel is rendered as a medium-height card with 24–32px padding on all sides and a slightly elevated treatment (subtle shadow or border) to differentiate it from the page background. Within the panel, content is arranged top to bottom: a small purple label (e.g., 'SCHEDULE OVERVIEW') at the top left, followed by a concise headline (e.g., 'Check for conflicts'), a short explanatory line, and then the primary schedule visualization—a compact calendar or timeline strip that runs horizontally across most of the card width. Any conflict warnings or status messages appear below or beside this visualization in a concise, stacked format, highlighting problems with purple or a single alert color while maintaining overall visual calm. The panel’s height is balanced so it feels substantial but not overwhelming, generally occupying about 1/3–2/5 of the viewport height on first view, depending on screen size.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static card within the second row of the main content grid. It scales responsively: on wide screens, it stays in the left half of the row; on smaller screens, it breaks to full width and stacks above or below the budget-overview-panel, maintaining at least 32–48px vertical spacing between the two. It does not scroll independently; it moves with the page scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The budget-overview-panel sits directly to the right of the schedule-overview-panel in the same second content row, completing a balanced, two-column layout beneath the main cart row. It spans the right 6 of the 12 columns, with its right edge aligning with the right edge of the cart-summary-bar above, and its left edge touching the centerline of the grid, leaving a 24–32px horizontal gap between it and the schedule-overview-panel. This card mirrors the schedule panel’s visual weight and dimensions—identical padding (24–32px), similar height, and matching card styling—so the two form a harmonious pair. Inside, content flows from top to bottom: a small purple label at the top left (e.g., 'BUDGET HELPER'), a short headline such as 'Stay within your budget', a one-line explanation, and then the primary budget visualization—perhaps a simple horizontal bar or set of key metrics (total, per-player cost, savings) arranged in 2–3 clean rows. Important numbers are emphasized in larger, high-contrast type, while secondary text is smaller and more muted. The card ends with an optional suggestion line or helper tip, keeping text concise to avoid clutter and preserve the spacious, editorial tone.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static card paired with the schedule-overview-panel. On large screens, it remains anchored to the right half of the row; on smaller screens, it becomes a full-width block stacked under or over the schedule panel, following the same centered alignment and maintaining generous vertical spacing. It scrolls with the page and has no sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart-empty-state-handler takes over the main content area whenever there are no items in the cart. In this mode, the hero/header band at the top of the page can remain visible, still presenting the 'Your Cart' context, but the entire grid of cart-items-list, cart-summary-bar, schedule-overview-panel, and budget-overview-panel is replaced by a single, centrally aligned empty state composition. This empty state sits in the max-width container directly below the hero band, but is visually centered in the remaining viewport: it uses a vertical margin of approximately 96px from the bottom of the hero, and additional flexible top/bottom spacing so that on most screens the core message appears within the middle 40–50% of the viewport height. Horizontally, it occupies about 50–60% of the content width for desktop, centered in the page to echo the hero’s central text column. The layout inside is strongly vertical: an optional monochrome or duotone illustration/icon related to tennis or an empty bag, centered at the top, followed by a small purple label (e.g., 'NO BOOKINGS YET'), a large headline (e.g., 'Your cart is empty'), one concise supporting sentence, and a single primary CTA button (purple) that invites users to browse programs. There is ample whitespace around and between these elements (24–32px vertical gaps) to maintain the low-density, image-driven hero feel even in the empty state.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally displayed full-width state that replaces the standard cart content when active. It remains vertically centered within the available space beneath the nav/hero as the user scrolls (which is minimal because the content is compact). On smaller screens, it expands to nearly the full screen width with preserved central alignment and vertical spacing, ensuring the main message and button remain easily tappable and visually dominant.

name: About
title: About
description: Page that loads and displays dynamic information about the tennis academy’s mission, coaching approach, history, facilities, key staff, and core contact details, with quick access to the Contact page and current cart summary.

core_features: Load and display academy mission, coaching approach, history, facilities overview, and key staff summary using getAboutContent.
Load and display core contact information (phone, email, address, map embed) using getContactInfo to help users understand where and how to reach the academy.
Provide a structured set of sections (mission, coaching approach, history, facilities, staff) that can be navigated via in-page links and URL parameters.
Offer a clear navigation action to the Contact page, including passing the configured subject parameter for general questions about the academy.
Show a compact cart summary (item count and total amount) using getCartSummary so users can stay aware of their current bookings/enrollments while reading about the academy.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Learn about the academy’s philosophy and background
1. User navigates to about.html (either from footer link or direct URL).
2. On page load, the about-content-loader component calls getAboutContent and stores mission, coaching_approach, history, facilities_overview, and key_staff_summary in page state.
3. The about-main-sections component reads the URL parameter section (if present) to determine which section to focus or scroll to.
4. The component renders all sections using the data from getAboutContent; if section is specified, that section is placed into focus (e.g., expanded or scrolled into view).
5. User clicks internal section links (e.g., “Coaching approach”, “History”); the about-section-nav component updates the URL parameter section accordingly and triggers the about-main-sections component to adjust focus.
6. User can continue switching sections; the URL remains in sync, allowing them to bookmark or share direct links to a specific section.

Workflow: Find facility information and locate the academy
1. User lands on about.html, optionally with ?section=facilities in the URL.
2. about-content-loader calls getAboutContent and makes facilities_overview available to the about-main-sections component.
3. contact-info-summary calls getContactInfo and renders the dynamic phone, email, address, and map_embed_code.
4. If section=facilities, about-main-sections automatically scrolls or jumps to the facilities section, highlighting the facilities_overview content.
5. User reviews facilities_overview and then checks the address and map_embed_code in contact-info-summary.
6. User clicks on the map interaction (e.g., “View larger map” or any link present inside map_embed_code, depending on implementation) to open a full map view in a new context.
7. User may click the phone or email links to start a call or an email using the dynamically loaded contact info.

Workflow: Move from About page to Contact while monitoring cart status
1. User is browsing about.html; they may already have items in their cart from previous actions on other pages.
2. cart-summary-mini calls getCartSummary on load and receives the current Cart and CartItem list, including total_amount.
3. cart-summary-mini displays the number of items and the total_amount, updating if the page is refreshed.
4. User reviews About content and decides to ask a question to the academy.
5. User clicks the Contact Us button in about-contact-cta.
6. The click event constructs a navigation URL contact.html?subject=General%20question%20about%20the%20academy (as defined in the page architecture) and redirects the browser to the Contact page.
7. The Contact page can then read the subject URL parameter and prefill or configure its contact form accordingly.
interactions: On page load, the about-content-loader component automatically calls getAboutContent and populates page state with mission, coaching_approach, history, facilities_overview, and key_staff_summary, then notifies dependent components that data is ready.

On page load, the contact-info-summary component calls getContactInfo and binds the resulting phone, email, address, and map_embed_code to interactive elements (click-to-call, mailto link, map container).
On page load, the cart-summary-mini component calls getCartSummary and binds the returned Cart and CartItem list to a compact summary view, including total_amount and item count.

When a user clicks an internal section link (e.g., to jump to the coaching approach or facilities section), the about-section-nav component updates the URL parameter section without reloading the page (or triggers a controlled reload if desired) and calls a handler in about-main-sections to adjust focus/scroll.

When a user clicks the Contact Us button in about-contact-cta, the component triggers navigation to contact.html with the subject URL parameter set to "General question about the academy" as defined by the outgoing navigation configuration.
When a user clicks on the phone number in contact-info-summary, the component uses the dynamically loaded phone value to initiate a tel: link, allowing the device to start a call.
When a user clicks on the email address in contact-info-summary, the component uses the dynamically loaded email value to initiate a mailto: link, pre-addressed to the academy.

When a user interacts with any link or control provided within map_embed_code, the map provider’s embedded interactions (such as zoom or open-in-maps) are enabled using the embed code returned by getContactInfo.
page_functionality.state_logic: The About page uses URL query parameters to manage which content section is emphasized and to coordinate navigation flows. A query parameter section controls the currently focused section of the About content. Expected values include mission, coaching, history, facilities, and staff. On initialization, about-main-sections reads section from the URL. If section matches a known section identifier, that section is scrolled into view or otherwise highlighted; if it is missing or unrecognized, a default ordering is shown without special focus. When users click internal navigation links (provided by about-section-nav), the component updates the URL’s section parameter (e.g., about.html?section=facilities) so that the current view is shareable and persists on refresh or direct re-entry. The outgoing navigation to contact.html always appends the subject query parameter set to "General question about the academy"; this is fixed by configuration and not derived from local state. No state is stored in localStorage or similar; any state beyond what is encoded in the URL is held in memory within the page’s components and re-initialized on each load via getAboutContent, getContactInfo, and getCartSummary.

components.functionality: On page initialization, this component calls getAboutContent and stores the returned mission, coaching_approach, history, facilities_overview, and key_staff_summary in a shared page state or context for use by other components.

Displays the main informational sections about the academy (mission, coaching approach, history, facilities, key staff) using the data loaded by about-content-loader, and adjusts which section is focused based on the section URL parameter.

Provides in-page navigation controls (e.g., links or buttons) to jump between mission, coaching approach, history, facilities, and staff sections, and synchronizes the selected section with the section URL parameter.
Retrieves and displays core contact details for the academy, including phone, email, address, and an embedded map snippet, enabling click-to-call, click-to-email, and map interactions.

Renders a Contact Us control that, when activated, navigates the user from about.html to contact.html and appends the subject query parameter set to "General question about the academy" as specified in the page’s outgoing navigation configuration.

Displays a compact summary of the user’s current cart by calling getCartSummary, showing key information such as the number of items and total_amount, and may offer a navigation control to a dedicated cart page.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content column for the hero text, over a full-width background image. Navigation items appear distributed horizontally across the top within the same grid.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows a linear, top-to-bottom flow anchored by the existing global navigation and hero pattern. At the very top, a slim horizontal navigation bar runs full width with the cart summary mini integrated on the far right. Below that, the About page content occupies a full-viewport hero at the top of the scrollable area, styled like the home hero: full-bleed tennis imagery with a dark overlay and a centered text column introducing the academy. Attached to the bottom of this hero is a horizontal section navigation that allows quick jumping to different About subsections. Below the hero and section nav, the about-main-sections component stacks multiple full-width sections (mission, coaching approach, history, facilities, staff, contact) in a single main content column. In the lower portion of the page, the contact-info-summary and about-contact-cta form a compact contact cluster aligned with the main content, giving quick access to the Contact page. The about-content-loader temporarily overlays the whole About content area (hero + sections) when data is loading.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows a linear, top-to-bottom flow anchored by the existing global navigation and hero pattern. At the very top, a slim horizontal navigation bar runs full width with the cart summary mini integrated on the far right. Below that, the About page content occupies a full-viewport hero at the top of the scrollable area, styled like the home hero: full-bleed tennis imagery with a dark overlay and a centered text column introducing the academy. Attached to the bottom of this hero is a horizontal section navigation that allows quick jumping to different About subsections. Below the hero and section nav, the about-main-sections component stacks multiple full-width sections (mission, coaching approach, history, facilities, staff, contact) in a single main content column. In the lower portion of the page, the contact-info-summary and about-contact-cta form a compact contact cluster aligned with the main content, giving quick access to the Contact page. The about-content-loader temporarily overlays the whole About content area (hero + sections) when data is loading.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The global navigation occupies a shallow band at the very top (roughly 64px tall). The About hero below it consumes nearly 100% of the remaining viewport height on desktop, with the central text block taking up about 30–40% of that vertical space and centered both vertically and horizontally. Content is confined to a centered column approximately 60–70% of the viewport width, with a max width around 960–1040px, while the background image remains full-bleed edge to edge. The section navigation sits just below or kissing the bottom edge of the hero, spanning roughly the same width as the main content column. Below that, each major section in about-main-sections uses full-width background with the same centered content column and generous vertical margins (64–96px) between sections. The contact-info-summary and about-contact-cta together occupy a modest block in the lower third of the page: on desktop they sit in the right portion of the main content column, taking roughly 30–35% of the column width, while the left side holds the descriptive contact section text. The about-content-loader covers the entirety of the content area below the navigation while active.
layout_strategies.content_density: The hero maintains low content density: a small purple label, a large white headline, and a concise supporting sentence, all with generous vertical spacing and plenty of empty space around them so the background image dominates without clutter. The section navigation is light and minimal, a thin row of text links with ample breathing room above and below. The about-main-sections maintain moderate density: each section focuses on one theme with a clear heading, brief paragraphs, and possibly a supporting image, but still lots of white space between elements to preserve readability. The contact-info-summary is compact but airy, with clear separation between contact details lines and comfortable padding inside its card-like container. The about-contact-cta is a single, bold button rather than a cluster of actions, keeping the interface focused. The cart-summary-mini is visually subtle and compact, adding information without making the navigation feel crowded.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye first lands on the bold hero headline in the center of the screen, then travels slightly up to the smaller purple label, then down to the supporting copy, mirroring the original mockup. From there, as the user glances upward, the global nav and cart summary mini become available for broader site actions. As users scroll or look downward, their attention is guided to the horizontally centered section navigation, which becomes the next focal point for navigating the About content. Scrolling further, the vertical stack of sections leads the eye down through the academy story in a clear, linear path. In the lower third of the page, the contact-info-summary and the about-contact-cta stand out as a focused action cluster on the right side of the content column, drawing attention as the logical “what next” step. The about-content-loader, when active, sits above everything in the content area, temporarily becoming the primary focus but with a subdued, neutral styling so it does not feel visually heavy.

layout.overall_description: The About page inherits the site’s modern, image-driven hero layout: a full-width navigation bar sits at the very top, light and flat, with uppercase links spaced evenly across and a compact cart summary mini anchored on the far right. Beneath it, the hero fills the entire visible viewport with a tennis court photograph under a dark overlay. In the vertical center of this hero, a single narrow column of text sits on the grid’s centerline: a small purple label like “ABOUT THE ACADEMY” at the top, a large white headline beneath, and a short descriptive sentence below that, each separated by generous vertical spacing so the composition feels open and editorial. At the lower edge of the hero, a slim horizontal section navigation bar aligns with the same central column, providing quick links to Mission, Coaching, History, Facilities, Staff, and Contact. As you scroll down, the hero gives way to a series of full-width sections, each with content confined to the centered column, plenty of white space, and subtle alternation of imagery and copy while maintaining the overall minimalist look. In the lower portion of the page, the Contact section introduces key details in the left part of the column, while the right portion contains a clean, padded contact-info-summary card and a bold purple about-contact-cta button immediately below it, visually inviting users to move to the main Contact page. Throughout, the experience is linear and calm, with the vertical flow leading from hero story to detailed information to actionable contact, while the section navigation and cart summary mini remain accessible. When dynamic data is loading, a soft overlay spans the entire content area below the nav, visually centered on the same column, signaling loading without breaking the underlying structure.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-content-loader appears as a semi-transparent overlay covering the entire About page content area from just beneath the fixed top navigation bar down to the bottom of the viewport (and extending further down if content scrolls). It matches the full width of the page and sits above the hero, section navigation, main sections, contact summary, and CTA, but below the global nav and cart summary mini so that the top bar remains visible. Within this overlay, any loading indicator (e.g., spinner or skeleton lines) is centered both horizontally and vertically within the same main content column width (approximately 60–70% of page width, max 960–1040px), echoing the centered hero composition. The overlay uses generous padding around the indicator (24–32px) and a soft dark or neutral tint that slightly dims the underlying content while it loads, maintaining the clean, minimalist feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Positioned absolutely or fixed within the page content container, full-width and full-height beneath the global navigation; appears only while data is loading, then fades out and becomes non-interactive, allowing the standard scrollable layout beneath to be used.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-main-sections component occupies the full width of the page below the global navigation bar and is the primary vertical stack that defines the About experience. Its first subsection is styled as an About hero: a full-bleed background image of the tennis facility spans edge to edge across the viewport, with a dark overlay applied. Within this hero, a centered text column sits roughly in the middle 30–40% of the viewport height, aligned to the central 12-column grid and limited to about 60–70% of the viewport width (max 960–1040px). The text in this column is vertically and horizontally centered, with a small purple label at the top, a large white headline just below, and a concise supporting paragraph beneath, each separated by 24–32px of vertical spacing. Below this hero block, the rest of the about-main-sections continues as a series of full-width bands stacked top to bottom. Each band uses the same centered column width and sits within generous vertical spacing: 64–96px of margin between major sections and 24–32px padding between headings and body copy. Sections like Mission, Coaching Approach, History, Facilities, and Staff appear in sequence down the page, each clearly separated but visually unified by consistent typography, alignments, and breathing room.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard scrollable block-level container extending vertically down the page; it begins immediately below the global navigation and underpins most of the page’s scroll length. The topmost part behaves like a hero (occupying nearly one full viewport height), while subsequent sections follow a standard stacked layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-section-nav is positioned directly beneath the hero content of the about-main-sections component, visually attached to the lower edge of the hero so that when the hero fills the screen, the section nav sits just at or slightly below the fold. It aligns with the main content column, spanning approximately the same width (60–70% of the page, centered), but uses a low-profile horizontal layout: a single row of evenly spaced, uppercase text links representing the key About subsections (e.g., Mission, Coaching, History, Facilities, Staff, Contact). The row sits in a shallow band with 16–24px padding above and below the link text, and 24–32px of vertical space separates it from the first main body section below. The overall presentation is light and minimal—no heavy borders, perhaps just a subtle underline for the active section—in keeping with the understated navigation style of the main hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Initially positioned in the normal flow directly under the hero. As the user scrolls past the hero, it becomes sticky, pinning itself to the top of the content area just below the global navigation bar. In this sticky state, it remains a slim horizontal band that stays visible at the top of the viewport, ensuring quick access to intra-page navigation while the rest of the about-main-sections content scrolls underneath.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-info-summary appears within the lower portion of the about-main-sections stack, inside the Contact-themed section. On desktop, the Contact section uses the standard full-width band with a centered content column; the descriptive contact copy (e.g., a short introduction or reassurance) occupies the left two-thirds of this column, while the contact-info-summary is presented as a compact, card-like panel aligned to the right third. This panel sits roughly midway down the Contact section’s height, starting about 32–48px below the section heading, and is right-aligned with the main text column’s right edge. It occupies about 30–35% of the column’s width and includes 24–32px of internal padding, with neatly stacked lines of key contact data (phone, email, address, maybe hours) separated by 8–16px. The card background subtly contrasts with the main background (slightly lighter or darker within the same monochrome palette), and it may have a faint border or shadow for separation while still feeling minimal. On smaller screens, the contact-info-summary shifts to full width and appears directly beneath the contact heading and lead paragraph, maintaining the same internal spacing but aligning centrally rather than to the right.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows within the Contact section of the about-main-sections component. On desktop, it behaves like a right-side sub-column within the main content column; on mobile and narrow viewports, it reflows to a full-width block stacked vertically for better readability.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-contact-cta is a prominent button positioned immediately below the contact-info-summary, visually tied to it as the next action. On desktop, it is aligned with the contact-info-summary card’s left edge, sitting 16–24px beneath the bottom of the card, occupying roughly 60–70% of the card’s width so that it feels substantial but not edge-to-edge. The button uses the brand’s purple accent as its primary fill or strong outline, with white or very light text, making it stand out clearly against the otherwise muted, monochrome Contact section. Vertical padding inside the button follows the spacious scale (at least 12–16px top and bottom) to create a large, tappable target. The button label is short and imperative (“Go to Contact” or similar), set in uppercase or a strong sans-serif weight consistent with the hero typography. On mobile, the button expands to almost the full width of the content column (minus standard side padding), centered below the contact-info-summary block with the same 16–24px vertical gap.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows directly after the contact-info-summary within the Contact section. It does not float or fix but remains anchored to that section’s position; as users scroll to the bottom of the About content, it naturally comes into view as the primary action. Its alignment and width adapt with the contact-info-summary’s responsive behavior (right-aligned, partial width on desktop; centered, near-full-width on mobile).

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart-summary-mini sits inside the global top navigation bar, anchored at the far right end of the nav content area. It shares the same horizontal baseline as the navigation links but is visually separated by slightly more space (16–24px) from the last nav item. The element itself is compact: roughly the height of the nav bar and no more than 15–20% of the nav’s width, typically appearing as a small icon (cart or bag) with a numeric badge or a short “Cart (2)” label. There is 8–16px of internal horizontal padding so that it remains easily clickable without dominating the bar. The style remains minimal and aligned with the site’s aesthetic—no large pill or bright color flood—perhaps using a subtle outline or a small purple accent in the badge to signal interactivity while ensuring the hero content remains the primary focal point.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Integrated into the fixed or sticky global navigation at the top of the page, remaining visible at all times as users scroll through the About content. It does not scroll with the main page body; instead, it stays locked in position within the nav bar, providing persistent access to cart details without disrupting the hero-centric visual flow.

name: Contact
title: Contact
description: Contact page where visitors can submit general inquiries, view contact details for the tennis academy, and see their current cart summary without leaving the page.

core_features: Display general contact details (phone, email, address, map embed) using getContactInfo.
Provide a general contact form capturing name, email, subject, message, and preferred contact method.
Prefill the contact form subject and context based on URL parameters (subject, contextType, contextId).
Submit the contact form using submitContactMessage and show an inline success or error state without page navigation.
Show a live cart summary (item count and total) using getCartSummary so users stay aware of existing bookings while contacting the academy.

user_workflows.name: Submit a general contact inquiry from the Contact navigation
steps: User navigates to contact.html via header or footer navigation without URL parameters.
On page load, the page controller component calls getContactInfo to retrieve phone, email, address, and map_embed_code.
The page controller also calls getCartSummary to fetch the current cart and items.
The contact form initializes with empty fields: name, email, subject, message, preferred contact method (no selection).
User fills in the form fields and selects a preferred contact method.
User clicks the form submit button, triggering validation (required: name, email, message; optional: subject, preferred contact method).
If validation passes, the form component calls submitContactMessage with the current field values.

On successful response, the form displays an inline confirmation message using the returned message value and may show key details from the created ContactMessage (e.g., timestamp or status) within the same view.
The form may clear or disable inputs after success, while the rest of the page (contact info and cart summary) remains visible and unchanged.

user_workflows.name: Contact about a specific membership plan from membership_detail.html
steps: User clicks a “Contact us” link or button on membership_detail.html that navigates to contact.html with URL parameters: subject, contextType, and contextId.
On page load, the controller parses URL parameters: subject, contextType, contextId.
The contact form component initializes its subject input with the subject URL parameter if provided.

If contextType and contextId are present, the form prepares an internal hidden context string that will be appended to the message or subject before calling submitContactMessage (e.g., including contextType and contextId as metadata at the top of the message body).
The controller calls getContactInfo and getCartSummary as in the general flow to populate contact details and cart summary.
User reviews the prefilled subject, adjusts it if needed, and completes name, email, message, and preferred contact method.
User submits the form; the form component composes the final subject and message payload (including any context metadata derived from contextType/contextId) and calls submitContactMessage.
On success, the page shows inline confirmation while keeping the user on contact.html; the URL parameters remain unchanged unless the application decides to clean them via history API.

user_workflows.name: Check cart status while on the Contact page
steps: User navigates to contact.html (with or without URL parameters).
On page load, the page controller calls getCartSummary.
The cart summary component reads the returned cart and items and calculates or displays summary information (e.g., number of items and total amount) based on Cart and CartItem data.
User can decide to continue contacting the academy or navigate back to booking or membership pages (navigation links are outside this page’s API scope).
If the user reloads the page, getCartSummary is called again to keep the summary up to date.

interactions: User types into name, email, subject, and message fields; each input updates the form’s internal state.
User selects preferred contact method (e.g., email or phone) via radio buttons or a select dropdown; selection updates the form’s internal state.
User clicks the submit button on the contact form, triggering client-side validation and, if valid, a submitContactMessage call.
On submitContactMessage success, the form displays an inline success message (using the API’s returned message property) and may disable or reset the inputs.
On submitContactMessage error (e.g., network or API error), the form displays an inline error state and keeps the user’s input so they can retry.

On initial page load, the controller reads URL parameters (subject, contextType, contextId) and passes their values into the contact-form component as initial props, which prefill subject and context metadata.
On initial page load and subsequent manual refreshes, the controller calls getContactInfo to populate the contact-info component and map-embed component.
On initial page load and refreshes, the controller calls getCartSummary to populate the cart-summary component, which updates any cart badge or summary text.
page_functionality.state_logic: Page state is driven entirely by URL parameters and API responses. When contact.html loads, it parses the query string parameters: (1) 'subject' (string) is used to prefill the subject field in the contact form if present. (2) 'contextType' (string) and 'contextId' (string) are optional metadata used only in front-end state. They are not persisted as separate fields but are appended into the subject and/or message payload before calling submitContactMessage. For example, if contextType and contextId exist, the form builds an internal context string (combining them) and prepends or appends it to the message body before submission. No localStorage or other persistent client storage is used; all context is derived from the current URL and kept in memory while the user remains on the page. When the URL changes (e.g., user navigates in from a different page with a different subject), a new page load or router event re-reads the parameters and reinitializes the form. Contact details are always loaded from getContactInfo on page initialization, and cart state is always based on getCartSummary. The ContactMessage record state (status, created_at) comes from the submitContactMessage response and is only used to render the inline confirmation state for the current session.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial data loading, URL parameter parsing, and passes props to child components on contact.html.
Displays the academy's primary contact information (phone, email, address) using data from getContactInfo.
Renders the location map using the map_embed_code returned by getContactInfo.

Captures user input for general inquiries (name, email, subject, message, preferred contact method), incorporates optional context from URL parameters, and submits the data through submitContactMessage while handling validation and inline confirmation.
Shows inline success or error messages after a contact form submission, based on the submitContactMessage response (including status and message).
Displays a compact summary of the user's current cart and bookings (e.g., total items and total amount) using getCartSummary so users can see ongoing bookings while on the Contact page.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column center-aligned grid with a max-width content column for the hero text, over a full-width background image. Navigation items appear distributed horizontally across the top within the same grid.
layout.layout_pattern: Maintain the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom flow with: (1) a slim, full-width navigation band at the very top (handled by the page controller, also housing the cart summary on the right); (2) beneath it, a full-viewport hero-style contact section with a full-bleed tennis image and dark overlay, containing a centered single column with a small purple label, large white 'Contact' heading, descriptive copy, and the main contact form; (3) below the hero, a stacked full-width section that pairs the contact info panel and map embed using the same 12-column grid (info on the left, map on the right). The form confirmation state replaces the form in-place within the hero to preserve the clean, minimal arrangement.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Maintain the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom flow with: (1) a slim, full-width navigation band at the very top (handled by the page controller, also housing the cart summary on the right); (2) beneath it, a full-viewport hero-style contact section with a full-bleed tennis image and dark overlay, containing a centered single column with a small purple label, large white 'Contact' heading, descriptive copy, and the main contact form; (3) below the hero, a stacked full-width section that pairs the contact info panel and map embed using the same 12-column grid (info on the left, map on the right). The form confirmation state replaces the form in-place within the hero to preserve the clean, minimal arrangement.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Reserve roughly 100% of the first viewport height for the hero section, minus the height of the top navigation band. The central text + form block inside the hero should occupy about 30–40% of the vertical space, centered vertically with generous padding above and below. Horizontally, keep all main content within a centered max-width column (~960–1200px), with the form constrained to ~50–60% of that width for comfort. The lower contact info + map section spans full width but uses the same centered grid; the info panel takes about 4–5 columns (≈35–40% width) and the map about 7–8 columns (≈60–65%), with 64–96px vertical padding around the section to mirror the spacious feel.
layout_strategies.content_density: Keep the hero very low-density: a short label, a bold heading, 1–2 lines of supporting text, and a compact but well-spaced form. Inputs are vertically stacked with 16–24px gaps and ample internal padding; no extra decorative elements. The lower section remains fairly light: a concise contact info panel and a single large map embed, each with clear breathing room. The cart summary is compact and unobtrusive in the top-right of the nav band, showing only essential cart info (e.g., icon + item count / total).
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Primary attention is drawn to the central hero heading and form: eye goes first to the high-contrast white 'Contact' headline, slightly up to the small purple label, then down into the explanatory text and form fields. From there, the eye can move upward to the top navigation and the right-aligned cart summary for exploration or checkout, or downward to the lower section containing detailed contact info and the map. The diagonal lines of the tennis background and the centered column keep focus anchored to the hero’s middle column, while the stacked section beneath continues a clear top-to-bottom reading flow.

layout.overall_description: The page opens with a full-bleed tennis court background stretching edge-to-edge across the screen, slightly darkened by an overlay so that white and purple text sits crisply on top. A slim, understated navigation band runs along the very top, using the same centered 12‑column grid: logo on the left, evenly spaced uppercase links across the center, and a compact cart summary aligned to the far right. Below this, the hero fills nearly the full viewport height. In the vertical middle of this hero, a single centered column holds a small purple label such as 'CONTACT US', a large white 'Get in touch' headline, a short line of descriptive body copy, and the main contact form stacked directly beneath, all aligned left within the column but visually anchored to the center of the page. Generous vertical spacing and tight horizontal alignment make this block feel calm and focused against the dynamic sports imagery. As users scroll down, the hero gives way to a second full-width section on a solid dark or near-black background, still using the same central grid. At the top of this section, a subtle section title may appear, followed by a two-column layout: on the left, the contact info panel lists address, phone, email, and hours in a clean text block; on the right, the map embed occupies a larger area, visually balancing the layout. This section has ample top and bottom padding so it feels like a stable, grounded counterpart to the hero. The cart summary remains accessible in the top-right navigation band throughout, while submitting the hero’s contact form gracefully swaps the form for a confirmation message in the same centered area, keeping the page visually steady and uncluttered.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page controller defines a full-width, edge-to-edge canvas with a full-bleed hero immediately below a slim top navigation band. The navigation band spans 100% of the width and sits at the very top of the page, using a centered 12‑column grid with a max content width of approximately 1200px. Within this grid, the leftmost columns house the logo, the center columns contain evenly spaced, uppercase navigation links, and the rightmost columns reserve space for the cart-summary-header component. Directly beneath this band, the hero occupies the remainder of the first viewport (approximately 100vh minus the nav height), displaying a tennis background image with a subtle dark overlay. All hero content (label, heading, body text, and form/confirmation) is aligned within a central column that spans roughly 8 of 12 columns and is horizontally centered, with 48–64px padding from the top of the hero to the beginning of the content block and similar breathing room below. Below the hero, the page controller stacks subsequent full-width sections vertically: the contact-info-panel and contact-map-embed share a single section that still adheres to the same centered 12‑column grid, separated from the hero by about 64–96px of vertical space to maintain visual hierarchy.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Defines overall page grid, hero background, and section stacking. The top navigation band is fixed to the top of the document flow (optionally sticky on scroll), while all sections below scroll normally in a linear vertical sequence.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact form sits in the visual center of the hero, directly below the small purple label and main white headline, forming the lower part of the central content block. It is left-aligned within the centered content column, occupying roughly 50–60% of that column’s width (about 4–5 of 12 grid columns) so it feels substantial but not edge-to-edge. There is approximately 24–32px of vertical space between the body copy and the first form field, and 16–24px between each stacked input. Inputs are full width within the form’s own column, with generous internal padding (12–16px) and clear vertical spacing, preserving the hero’s low-density aesthetic. The submit button sits aligned with the inputs, with about 24px of spacing above it, and may stretch to the same width as the inputs or be slightly narrower but still left-aligned. Vertically, the form plus its surrounding text is centered in the hero area so that there is ample space above (toward the navigation) and below (toward the bottom of the hero), creating a balanced middle band of content against the darkened background image.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Rendered within the hero’s central column as part of the normal document flow. On submission, this component yields its space to the contact-form-confirmation component, which appears in the same position and approximate size, keeping the layout stable.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact form confirmation message occupies exactly the same footprint as the contact form within the hero’s central column. When the form is successfully submitted, the confirmation replaces the form fields in place, preserving the surrounding label, headline, and overall vertical centering. The confirmation content—typically a short heading like 'Message sent' and 1–2 lines of supporting text—is left-aligned and constrained to the same width as the previous form (about 4–5 grid columns within the central column). There is approximately 24–32px of vertical space between the body copy above and the confirmation heading, mirroring the spacing previously used before the form. The overall block remains in the middle 30–40% of the hero’s height, with generous empty space above and below, so the hero maintains its clean, spacious composition even after submission.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears conditionally in place of the contact-form within the hero’s central column, occupying the same layout slot. It does not shift other components; instead, it swaps content in the same area to avoid layout jumps.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact info panel is located in the first stacked section immediately below the hero. This section spans the full width of the page and uses the same centered max-width 12‑column grid. Within this section, a subtle section title or label may appear at the top center with 32–48px of padding from the section’s top edge, followed by a two-column layout. The contact info panel anchors the left side of this layout, occupying approximately 4–5 of the 12 columns (around 35–40% of the content width). It sits with 32–48px of top padding from the section header and is vertically centered relative to the map embed to its right when screen height allows. Inside the panel, the academy name, address, telephone, email, and hours are stacked vertically with 8–16px spacing between items, keeping the text concise and easy to scan. Generous outer margins ensure at least 32–64px of whitespace around the panel, preserving the airy, editorial feel from the hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Normal document flow within the lower section. On wider screens, it forms the left column next to the map; on narrow/mobile screens, it stacks above the map, maintaining the same spacing proportions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The map embed shares the same section as the contact-info-panel, positioned to the right within the centered 12‑column grid. It spans approximately 7–8 of the 12 columns (about 60–65% width), starting where the info panel’s columns end. The top of the map aligns roughly with the top of the contact info block, and its height is set to create a clear, substantial visual element—about 1/2 to 2/3 of the viewport height on desktop, with at least 32–48px of padding between its edges and the section’s top and bottom. The map’s right edge and bottom edge align with the page’s content margins, maintaining the same side padding as the hero content. This arrangement makes the map a large, easy-to-understand visual anchor that balances the more text-heavy panel on the left while still respecting the overall minimal, grid-driven aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Normal document flow in the lower section. On wide screens it sits to the right of the contact-info-panel as a large block; on small screens it stacks below the info panel, spanning nearly the full width of the content area.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The cart summary header is positioned within the top navigation band, aligned to the far right of the centered 12‑column grid. It occupies roughly 2–3 grid columns and is horizontally anchored flush with the right content margin, opposite the logo on the left. Vertically, it is centered within the nav band, which itself is a slim horizontal strip at the top of the page (about 56–72px tall). The cart summary displays minimal information: likely a cart icon, a compact label such as 'Cart', and a small badge or inline text showing item count or total. It uses the brand accent color (purple) sparingly—perhaps for the icon or badge—to stand out without overshadowing the main hero. There is about 16–24px of horizontal padding on either side of its clickable area to meet comfortable tap targets, and at least 8–16px of separation from the nearest nav link to avoid visual crowding.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Lives inside the top navigation band controlled by the page controller. It stays aligned to the top-right as the user scrolls; if the nav is implemented as sticky, the cart summary remains visible at all times, otherwise it scrolls away with the nav in normal document flow.
